blocks.html_en:
To play devil's advocate: child abuse is usually a crime without direct evidence. CSAM is child abuse but the perpetrator films their crime as evidence. Everyone else who obtains and keeps this material also holds evidence against themselves as the possession of it is the crime.

Now for real: People often do not care a lot about child abuse. It does not affect them as it happens in private where they do not see it. CSAM however does affect them as it makes the abuse visible and shows something deemed reprehensible. In a lot of ways people see themselves being witness (even indirect by purely hearing about it) to CSAM as the victim rather than the children who are harmed in the making and by redistribution.

My cynical opinion is that it is a lot more about social norms and feelings arising from the violation of these norms rather than the harm done through the violation of children.

sentences.en: To play devil's advocate: child abuse is usually a crime without direct evidence.
sentences.zh: 唱反调：虐待儿童通常是一种没有直接证据的犯罪行为。

sentences.en: CSAM is child abuse but the perpetrator films their crime as evidence.
sentences.zh: CSAM 属于虐待儿童行为，但犯罪者将其犯罪行为拍摄下来作为证据。

sentences.en: Everyone else who obtains and keeps this material also holds evidence against themselves as the possession of it is the crime.Now for real: People often do not care a lot about child abuse.
sentences.zh: 其他获得并保留这些材料的人也持有对自己不利的证据，因为拥有这些材料就是犯罪。现在说实话：人们通常不太关心虐待儿童问题。

sentences.en: It does not affect them as it happens in private where they do not see it.
sentences.zh: 它不会影响他们，因为它发生在他们看不到的私下里。

sentences.en: CSAM however does affect them as it makes the abuse visible and shows something deemed reprehensible.
sentences.zh: 然而，CSAM 确实对他们产生了影响，因为它使虐待行为变得显而易见，并显示出一些应受谴责的行为。

sentences.en: In a lot of ways people see themselves being witness (even indirect by purely hearing about it) to CSAM as the victim rather than the children who are harmed in the making and by redistribution.My cynical opinion is that it is a lot more about social norms and feelings arising from the violation of these norms rather than the harm done through the violation of children.
sentences.zh: 在很多方面，人们认为自己是 CSAM 的受害者（甚至是间接地通过纯粹听到它），而不是在形成和再分配中受到伤害的儿童。我愤世嫉俗的观点是，这更多的是关于社会规范和因违反这些规范而产生的感受，而不是通过侵犯儿童而造成的伤害。

blocks.html_en:
> CSAM is child abuse but the perpetrator films their crime as evidence

This isn't entirely true. In much of the Western world – outside the US – CSAM (or equivalent legal terms such as "child pornography", "child abuse material", "child exploitation material", etc) can include text, drawings, AI-generated imagery, etc, which no child was abused to create, indeed the child depicted in it may be entirely fictional. Canadian law goes so far as to treat material which "advocates or counsels" the commission of CSA as CSAM - which the Supreme Court of Canada insisted (in the 2001 case of R v Sharpe) doesn't include mere political advocacy for its legalisation (as in e.g. the NAMBLA Bulletin), although some will question whether that insistence actually cashes out in practice.

Now, as a father of school-aged children, I find the whole idea of groups like NAMBLA rather abhorrent – but, I'm hesitant about the government making it illegal for people to express abhorrent ideas, because there are likely ideas which you or I hold which somebody out there considers abhorrent.

sentences.en: > CSAM is child abuse but the perpetrator films their crime as evidenceThis isn't entirely true.
sentences.zh: > CSAM 是虐待儿童，但犯罪者将他们的犯罪行为拍摄下来作为证据这并不完全正确。

sentences.en: In much of the Western world – outside the US – CSAM (or equivalent legal terms such as "child pornography", "child abuse material", "child exploitation material", etc) can include text, drawings, AI-generated imagery, etc, which no child was abused to create, indeed the child depicted in it may be entirely fictional.
sentences.zh: 在美国以外的大部分西方世界，CSAM（或类似的法律术语，如“儿童色情制品”、“虐待儿童材料”、“儿童剥削材料”等）可以包括文本、图画、人工智能生成的图像等，这些图像中没有儿童被虐待，事实上，其中描绘的儿童可能完全是虚构的。

sentences.en: Canadian law goes so far as to treat material which "advocates or counsels" the commission of CSA as CSAM - which the Supreme Court of Canada insisted (in the 2001 case of R v Sharpe) doesn't include mere political advocacy for its legalisation (as in e.g. the NAMBLA Bulletin), although some will question whether that insistence actually cashes out in practice.Now, as a father of school-aged children, I find the whole idea of groups like NAMBLA rather abhorrent – but, I'm hesitant about the government making it illegal for people to express abhorrent ideas, because there are likely ideas which you or I hold which somebody out there considers abhorrent.
sentences.zh: 加拿大法律甚至将“倡导或建议”CSA 委员会的材料视为 CSAM - 加拿大最高法院坚持认为（在 2001 年 R v Sharpe 案件中）不包括单纯对其合法化的政治倡导（例如 NAMBLA 公报），尽管有些人会质疑这种坚持是否在实践中真正兑现。现在，作为一名学龄儿童的父亲，我发现像这样的团体的整个想法NAMBLA 相当令人憎恶——但是，我对政府是否将人们表达令人憎恶的想法定为非法感到犹豫，因为你或我可能持有一些别人认为令人憎恶的想法。

blocks.html_en:
> CSAM is child abuse but the perpetrator films their crime as evidence

This isn't entirely true. In much of the Western world – outside the US – CSAM (or equivalent legal terms such as "child pornography", "child abuse material", "child exploitation material", etc) can include text, drawings, AI-generated imagery, etc, which no child was abused to create, indeed the child depicted in it may be entirely fictional. Canadian law goes so far as to treat material which "advocates or counsels" the commission of CSA as CSAM - which the Supreme Court of Canada insisted (in the 2001 case of R v Sharpe) doesn't include mere political advocacy for its legalisation (as in e.g. the NAMBLA Bulletin), although some will question whether that insistence actually cashes out in practice.

Now, as a father of school-aged children, I find the whole idea of groups like NAMBLA rather abhorrent – but, I'm hesitant about the government making it illegal for people to express abhorrent ideas, because there are likely ideas which you or I hold which somebody out there considers abhorrent.

sentences.en: > CSAM is child abuse but the perpetrator films their crime as evidenceThis isn't entirely true.
sentences.zh: > CSAM 是虐待儿童，但犯罪者将他们的犯罪行为拍摄下来作为证据这并不完全正确。

sentences.en: In much of the Western world – outside the US – CSAM (or equivalent legal terms such as "child pornography", "child abuse material", "child exploitation material", etc) can include text, drawings, AI-generated imagery, etc, which no child was abused to create, indeed the child depicted in it may be entirely fictional.

blocks.html_en:
Fictional erotica portraying fictional children is not CSAM. There is no child abuse taking place as there is no child in the first place.

Conflating the two is 1. wrong as a matter of fact and ethics and 2. a great injustice to the victims of child abuse.

This is exactly what I wrote about in the second part of my comment. People see themselves as the victim of CSAM or proclaimed CSAM through being witness to it. In fictional works there is no child who could be the victim showing very clearly what these people are really about. They are not primarily concerned about children being exploited but they do not like the existence of such material as them deem it offensive to themselves.

CSAM is a very real crime; including cases where real children are not physically harmed but get sexualized through the composition of the material. It is a crime because it violates children, not because it is offensive to societal norms.

sentences.en: Fictional erotica portraying fictional children is not CSAM.
sentences.zh: 描绘虚构儿童的虚构色情作品不是 CSAM。

sentences.en: There is no child abuse taking place as there is no child in the first place.Conflating the two is 1.
sentences.zh: 不存在虐待儿童的情况，因为根本就没有孩子。将两者合并为 1。

sentences.en: wrong as a matter of fact and ethics and 2.
sentences.zh: 事实上和道德上都是错误的，2.

sentences.en: a great injustice to the victims of child abuse.This is exactly what I wrote about in the second part of my comment.
sentences.zh: 这对虐待儿童的受害者来说是极大的不公正。这正是我在评论的第二部分中所写的内容。

sentences.en: In fictional works there is no child who could be the victim showing very clearly what these people are really about.
sentences.zh: 在虚构的作品中，没有哪个孩子可以成为受害者，清楚地表明这些人的真实情况。

sentences.en: They are not primarily concerned about children being exploited but they do not like the existence of such material as them deem it offensive to themselves.CSAM is a very real crime; including cases where real children are not physically harmed but get sexualized through the composition of the material.

sentences.zh: 这是犯罪，因为它侵犯了儿童，而不是因为它违反了社会规范。

blocks.html_en:
> Fictional erotica portraying fictional children is not CSAM. There is no child abuse taking place as there is no child in the first place.

That’s your definition, but it isn’t the definition many authorities/activists/etc use.

And I’m not defending their definition, merely pointing out it is a real live definition in active use

blocks.html_en:
To clarify, I am aware you are objectively describing the legal situation. I am not making a legal, but an ethical argument. We seem to be agreeing.

There is even a Wikipedia article about the legality. It's quite complicated in a lot of places because people really like to ban it but it very much is free speech or protected artistic expression in countries that have these concepts: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Legal_status_of_fictional_porn...

To be clear fictional pornography about real children has to be distinguished from fictional characters. Even when the depiction is purely fictional. Sexualizing real children is unacceptable.

sentences.zh: 需要澄清的是，我知道您客观地描述了法律情况。

sentences.en: I am not making a legal, but an ethical argument.
sentences.zh: 我不是在进行法律论证，而是在进行道德论证。

sentences.zh: 我们似乎同意。甚至有一篇关于合法性的维基百科文章。

sentences.zh: 即使描述纯粹是虚构的。

blocks.html_en:
> I personally know of 1 who took a leak across from a playground at 2am walking home from the bars and was put on the sex offender registry because it was within 500'.

You might want to look up if that's actually true. It might be. It might also be a lie to hide the real reason why they are registered.

blocks.html_en:
in my limited experience with the law, they tend to really go for you if you aren't the kind of person who enjoys breaking the law.

It's almost like they can spot an easy target.

Deal drugs and conduct yourself violently in society, sit with a gang and destroy property... Police seem to have a hard time with you.

Push someone over who is threatening to knock your teeth out when you're walking with your wife and kids... that's an assault charge, because naively you explained what happened to the police, and they're going to make sure you see the inside of a court room for it.

blocks.html_en:
The lowest of the low hanging political points scoring fruit will be picked.

I make this point on HN each time it comes up: Dealing with actual CSA requires actual people going to investigate actual reports of children's living conditions out in the real world, not "scanning digital files". Seems obvious when you say it like that, but the alternative seems to still score heavily on the political scale. Ironically, spending more money on "scanning digital files" takes away from resources that could prevent those digital files from being created in the first place, so all the additional time, money, and effort towards combatting CSAM is time, money, and effort specifically _not_ working against stopping CSA. It's categorically _not_ protecting the children.

Teachers, in Australia at least, have mandatory reporting where any kind of abuse is suspected. The heart-breaking irony is that the resources to investigate the reports are so scarce that they can only respond to reports where the child's life is in immediate danger.

Caveat: the above was true a few years ago, I genuinely don't know if it's still true. What I do know is that 'social work' isn't suddenly a high paying job, so I doubt resource availability has changed much.

There is no easy answer to this. It's entirely nuance.

sentences.en: The lowest of the low hanging political points scoring fruit will be picked.I make this point on HN each time it comes up: Dealing with actual CSA requires actual people going to investigate actual reports of children's living conditions out in the real world, not "scanning digital files".
sentences.zh: 每次 HN 出现时，我都会指出这一点：处理实际的 CSA 需要实际的人去调查现实世界中儿童生活状况的实际报告，而不是“扫描数字文件”。

sentences.en: Seems obvious when you say it like that, but the alternative seems to still score heavily on the political scale.
sentences.zh: 当你这样说时，这似乎是显而易见的，但另一种选择似乎在政治层面上仍然得分很高。

sentences.en: Ironically, spending more money on "scanning digital files" takes away from resources that could prevent those digital files from being created in the first place, so all the additional time, money, and effort towards combatting CSAM is time, money, and effort specifically _not_ working against stopping CSA.
sentences.zh: 具有讽刺意味的是，花更多的钱在“扫描数字文件”上会占用一些资源，而这些资源可能会阻止这些数字文件的创建，因此所有用于对抗 CSAM 的额外时间、金钱和精力都只是用于阻止 CSA 的时间、金钱和精力。

sentences.en: It's categorically _not_ protecting the children.Teachers, in Australia at least, have mandatory reporting where any kind of abuse is suspected.
sentences.zh: 这绝对不是在保护儿童。至少在澳大利亚，教师必须强制报告任何可疑的虐待行为。

sentences.en: The heart-breaking irony is that the resources to investigate the reports are so scarce that they can only respond to reports where the child's life is in immediate danger.Caveat: the above was true a few years ago, I genuinely don't know if it's still true.
sentences.zh: 令人心碎的讽刺是，调查这些报告的资源如此稀缺，以至于他们只能对孩子的生命面临直接危险的报告做出回应。警告：几年前上述情况是真实的，我真的不知道是否仍然如此。

sentences.en: What I do know is that 'social work' isn't suddenly a high paying job, so I doubt resource availability has changed much.There is no easy answer to this.
sentences.zh: 我所知道的是，“社会工作”不会突然成为一项高薪工作，因此我怀疑资源的可用性是否发生了很大变化。对此没有简单的答案。

sentences.en: It's entirely nuance.
sentences.zh: 这完全是细微差别。

blocks.html_en:
> Seems obvious when you say it like that,

That's exactly what happens. CSAM has two parts to it.

The first is the known and most horrific videos of CP out there. These aren't shared, instead only hashes are used to determine if the image/video matches. It's next to impossible to get an mis-hit on that.

The second is nudity and age detection AI. Apple has this on their devices, but warns the user before sharing not reporting.

It normally takes more than one hit to get flagged for investigation. At that point law enforcement are informed and take over.

The majority of investigations are children sending naked pictures of themself to their friends.

So it is absolutely being used as the purpose to protect children and it's amazing how many people don't realise how it works when they are very transparent about it.

Of course it is not without its issues that need to fixed. For example providers will just ban the person before it's determined if they broke a law or not.

sentences.en: > Seems obvious when you say it like that,That's exactly what happens.
sentences.zh: > 当你这么说时，似乎很明显，事实确实如此。

sentences.en: CSAM has two parts to it.The first is the known and most horrific videos of CP out there.
sentences.zh: CSAM 有两个部分。第一个部分是已知且最恐怖的 CP 视频。

sentences.en: These aren't shared, instead only hashes are used to determine if the image/video matches.
sentences.zh: 这些不是共享的，而是仅使用哈希值来确定图像/视频是否匹配。

sentences.en: It's next to impossible to get an mis-hit on that.The second is nudity and age detection AI.
sentences.zh: 几乎不可能出现失误。第二个是裸体和年龄检测人工智能。

sentences.en: Apple has this on their devices, but warns the user before sharing not reporting.It normally takes more than one hit to get flagged for investigation.
sentences.zh: 苹果公司的设备上有这样的功能，但会在分享之前警告用户不要报告。通常需要不止一次点击才会被标记为调查。

sentences.en: At that point law enforcement are informed and take over.The majority of investigations are children sending naked pictures of themself to their friends.So it is absolutely being used as the purpose to protect children and it's amazing how many people don't realise how it works when they are very transparent about it.Of course it is not without its issues that need to fixed.
sentences.zh: 到那时执法部门就会接到通知并接管。大多数调查都是儿童向朋友发送自己的裸照。所以这绝对是被用作保护儿童的目的，令人惊讶的是，当他们对此非常透明时，有多少人没有意识到它是如何运作的。当然，这并不是没有需要解决的问题。

sentences.en: For example providers will just ban the person before it's determined if they broke a law or not.
sentences.zh: 例如，提供商只会在确定此人是否违法之前禁止该人。

sentences.en: At that point law enforcement are informed and take over.The majority of investigations are children sending naked pictures of themself to their friends.So it is absolutely being used as the purpose to protect children and it's amazing how many people don't realise how it works when they are very transparent about it.Of course it is not without its issues that need to fixed.
sentences.zh: 到那时执法部门就会接到通知并接管。大多数调查都是儿童向朋友发送自己的裸照。所以这绝对是被用作保护儿童的目的，令人惊讶的是，当他们对此非常透明时，有多少人没有意识到它是如何运作的。当然，这并不是没有需要解决的问题。

sentences.en: For example providers will just ban the person before it's determined if they broke a law or not.
sentences.zh: 例如，提供商只会在确定此人是否违法之前禁止该人。

sentences.zh: > 当你这么说时似乎很明显，

blocks.html_en: That's exactly what happens. CSAM has two parts to it.

sentences.zh: 这正是发生的情况。

sentences.en: CSAM has two parts to it.
sentences.zh: CSAM 有两个部分。

blocks.html_en: The first is the known and most horrific videos of CP out there. These aren't shared, instead only hashes are used to determine if the image/video matches. It's next to impossible to get an mis-hit on that.

sentences.en: The first is the known and most horrific videos of CP out there.
sentences.zh: 第一个是众所周知的、最恐怖的CP视频。

sentences.en: These aren't shared, instead only hashes are used to determine if the image/video matches.
sentences.zh: 这些不是共享的，而是仅使用哈希值来确定图像/视频是否匹配。

sentences.en: It's next to impossible to get an mis-hit on that.
sentences.zh: 击中失误几乎是不可能的。

blocks.html_en: The second is nudity and age detection AI. Apple has this on their devices, but warns the user before sharing not reporting.

sentences.en: The second is nudity and age detection AI.
sentences.zh: 第二个是裸体和年龄检测人工智能。

sentences.en: Apple has this on their devices, but warns the user before sharing not reporting.

blocks.html_en: It normally takes more than one hit to get flagged for investigation. At that point law enforcement are informed and take over.

sentences.en: It normally takes more than one hit to get flagged for investigation.
sentences.zh: 通常需要不止一次点击才会被标记为需要调查。

sentences.en: At that point law enforcement are informed and take over.
sentences.zh: 届时执法部门就会收到通知并接管。

blocks.html_en: The majority of investigations are children sending naked pictures of themself to their friends.

sentences.en: The majority of investigations are children sending naked pictures of themself to their friends.
sentences.zh: 大多数调查都是儿童向朋友发送自己的裸照。

blocks.html_en: So it is absolutely being used as the purpose to protect children and it's amazing how many people don't realise how it works when they are very transparent about it.

sentences.en: So it is absolutely being used as the purpose to protect children and it's amazing how many people don't realise how it works when they are very transparent about it.
sentences.zh: 因此，它绝对被用作保护儿童的目的，令人惊讶的是，当他们对此非常透明时，有多少人没有意识到它是如何运作的。

blocks.html_en: Of course it is not without its issues that need to fixed. For example providers will just ban the person before it's determined if they broke a law or not.

sentences.en: Of course it is not without its issues that need to fixed.
sentences.zh: 当然，这并非没有问题需要解决。

sentences.en: For example providers will just ban the person before it's determined if they broke a law or not.
sentences.zh: 例如，提供商只会在确定此人是否违法之前禁止该人。

blocks.html_en:
What you are describing is indiscriminate mass surveillance. There is absolutely nothing stopping Apple or whoever maintains that listof hashes from putting things other than child abuse on there, like dissident memes for example.

It also requires devices to run this spyware without a way to disable it. The natural consequence of this is to ban everything but government approved, restricted OSes and to outlaw devices capable of running anything but these. Mandatory CSAM scanning is part of the war on general computing and privacy.

sentences.en: What you are describing is indiscriminate mass surveillance.
sentences.zh: 你所描述的是不分青红皂白的大规模监视。

sentences.en: There is absolutely nothing stopping Apple or whoever maintains that listof hashes from putting things other than child abuse on there, like dissident memes for example.It also requires devices to run this spyware without a way to disable it.
sentences.zh: 绝对没有什么可以阻止苹果或任何维护该哈希列表的人将虐待儿童以外的东西放在那里，例如持不同政见的模因。它还要求设备运行这种间谍软件，而无法禁用它。

sentences.en: The natural consequence of this is to ban everything but government approved, restricted OSes and to outlaw devices capable of running anything but these.
sentences.zh: 这样做的自然结果是禁止除政府批准的、受限制的操作系统之外的所有操作系统，并禁止能够运行除这些操作系统之外的任何操作系统的设备。

sentences.en: Mandatory CSAM scanning is part of the war on general computing and privacy.
sentences.zh: 强制 CSAM 扫描是通用计算和隐私战争的一部分。

blocks.html_en:
CSA doesn't neatly fit into a narrative.

If there's a person committing abuse against a child, usually a family member in their own household, there's no story there. They (hopefully) get arrested, get a minor mention in the media if that, and life moves on.

If, on the other hand, they send CSAM using a platform, device or protocol, then clearly the makers of that technology failed to uphold their sacred duty to protect the child. If most CSAM cases happen using a few major technologies (and they will, because societies tend to standardize on what technologies they use for communication), the technologies get blamed for the problem they supposedly enable.

If you dislike the technology for other reasons (the distrust of corporations from left-leaning politicians, censorship allegations on the right, a moral panic about the impact of smartphones on children's mental health), you can use people's misunderstanding of statistics to exaggerate the CSAM problem, blame the tech, and gain some notion of control over it in a way that is politically palatable to citizens.

It's worth saying explicitly that centralization doesn't have much to do with this. We've seen similar stories play out with bicycles, Walkmans, pagers, AI, heavy metal and Uber rapes. They were different moral panics, but the mechanic was roughly the same. I think the situation wouldn't change much if we all used PGP-encrypted email over personal mail servers to communicate.

An exception that proves the rule is the moral panic over CSA in primarily-catholic countries, notably relating to abuse committed by catholic priests, which are statistically no more likely to commit it than anybody else. As the influence of the Church on government policy is a hotly-debated topic in those countries, CSA is suddenly an issue that people can use to further their political causes.

sentences.en: CSA doesn't neatly fit into a narrative.If there's a person committing abuse against a child, usually a family member in their own household, there's no story there.
sentences.zh: CSA 并不完全符合叙事。如果有人虐待儿童（通常是自己家里的家庭成员），那么那里就没有故事。

sentences.en: They (hopefully) get arrested, get a minor mention in the media if that, and life moves on.If, on the other hand, they send CSAM using a platform, device or protocol, then clearly the makers of that technology failed to uphold their sacred duty to protect the child.
sentences.zh: 他们（希望）被逮捕，如果那样的话，会在媒体上得到轻微提及，然后生活继续。另一方面，如果他们使用平台、设备或协议发送 CSAM，那么显然该技术的制造者未能履行保护儿童的神圣职责。

sentences.en: If most CSAM cases happen using a few major technologies (and they will, because societies tend to standardize on what technologies they use for communication), the technologies get blamed for the problem they supposedly enable.If you dislike the technology for other reasons (the distrust of corporations from left-leaning politicians, censorship allegations on the right, a moral panic about the impact of smartphones on children's mental health), you can use people's misunderstanding of statistics to exaggerate the CSAM problem, blame the tech, and gain some notion of control over it in a way that is politically palatable to citizens.It's worth saying explicitly that centralization doesn't have much to do with this.
sentences.zh: 如果大多数 CSAM 案例都是使用几种主要技术发生的（而且确实会发生，因为社会倾向于对用于通信的技术进行标准化），那么这些技术就会因它们所引发的问题而受到指责。如果您出于其他原因（左倾政客对企业的不信任、右翼的审查指控、对智能手机对儿童心理健康影响的道德恐慌）而不喜欢该技术，您可以利用人们对统计数据的误解来夸大 CSAM 问题，将其归咎于技术，并以公民在政治上可接受的方式获得对其控制的概念。值得明确指出的是，集中化与此没有太大关系。

sentences.en: We've seen similar stories play out with bicycles, Walkmans, pagers, AI, heavy metal and Uber rapes.
sentences.zh: 我们已经看到类似的故事在自行车、随身听、寻呼机、人工智能、重金属和优步强奸等方面上演。

sentences.en: They were different moral panics, but the mechanic was roughly the same.
sentences.zh: 它们是不同的道德恐慌，但机制大致相同。

sentences.en: I think the situation wouldn't change much if we all used PGP-encrypted email over personal mail servers to communicate.An exception that proves the rule is the moral panic over CSA in primarily-catholic countries, notably relating to abuse committed by catholic priests, which are statistically no more likely to commit it than anybody else.
sentences.zh: 我认为，如果我们都在个人邮件服务器上使用 PGP 加密的电子邮件进行通信，情况不会有太大变化。证明这一规则的一个例外是在以天主教为主的国家中对 CSA 的道德恐慌，特别是与天主教神父所犯下的虐待行为有关，从统计数据来看，天主教神父犯下这种行为的可能性并不比其他人更高。

sentences.en: As the influence of the Church on government policy is a hotly-debated topic in those countries, CSA is suddenly an issue that people can use to further their political causes.
sentences.zh: 由于教会对政府政策的影响在这些国家是一个热门话题，CSA 突然成为人们可以用来推进其政治事业的一个问题。

blocks.html_en:
CSA doesn't neatly fit into a narrative.

If there's a person committing abuse against a child, usually a family member in their own household, there's no story there. They (hopefully) get arrested, get a minor mention in the media if that, and life moves on.

If, on the other hand, they send CSAM using a platform, device or protocol, then clearly the makers of that technology failed to uphold their sacred duty to protect the child. If most CSAM cases happen using a few major technologies (and they will, because societies tend to standardize on what technologies they use for communication), the technologies get blamed for the problem they supposedly enable.

If you dislike the technology for other reasons (the distrust of corporations from left-leaning politicians, censorship allegations on the right, a moral panic about the impact of smartphones on children's mental health), you can use people's misunderstanding of statistics to exaggerate the CSAM problem, blame the tech, and gain some notion of control over it in a way that is politically palatable to citizens.

It's worth saying explicitly that centralization doesn't have much to do with this. We've seen similar stories play out with bicycles, Walkmans, pagers, AI, heavy metal and Uber rapes. They were different moral panics, but the mechanic was roughly the same. I think the situation wouldn't change much if we all used PGP-encrypted email over personal mail servers to communicate.

An exception that proves the rule is the moral panic over CSA in primarily-catholic countries, notably relating to abuse committed by catholic priests, which are statistically no more likely to commit it than anybody else. As the influence of the Church on government policy is a hotly-debated topic in those countries, CSA is suddenly an issue that people can use to further their political causes.

blocks.html_en:
My thought is that many different stakeholders have reasons to focus on CSAM:

- Prosecutors want to go after it because it is much easier to prove than CSA.

- Authoritarians want to go after it because it gives them a chance to get things like ID checks accepted by the public.

- People who genuinely want to prevent CSA focus on it because a large amount of CSAM is made by serial abusers - so capturing producers can prevent future abuse. Additionally, they believe (with good reasons) that CSAM consumption is a gateway to CSA and CSAM production for many people. Further, finding CSAM can be a way to identify and rescue children from abusive situations.

- CSA survivors may advocate for it often feel traumatized not just by the abuse, but by the fact that people might be continuously viewing that abuse.

- Going after it winds up creating digital forensics specialists and task forces, which creates a special interest group within police departments which wants to continue focusing on CSAM but is not set up to do anything about CSA.

I would recommend the podcast Hunting Warhead to anyone interested in learning more about this.

sentences.en: My thought is that many different stakeholders have reasons to focus on CSAM:- Prosecutors want to go after it because it is much easier to prove than CSA.- Authoritarians want to go after it because it gives them a chance to get things like ID checks accepted by the public.- People who genuinely want to prevent CSA focus on it because a large amount of CSAM is made by serial abusers - so capturing producers can prevent future abuse.
sentences.zh: 我的想法是，许多不同的利益相关者都有理由关注 CSAM：- 检察官想要追查它，因为它比 CSA 更容易证明。- 独裁者想要追查它，因为它让他们有机会获得公众接受的身份检查等东西。- 真正想要阻止 CSA 的人关注它，因为大量 CSAM 是由连环滥用者制造的 - 因此捕获生产者可以防止未来的滥用。

sentences.en: Additionally, they believe (with good reasons) that CSAM consumption is a gateway to CSA and CSAM production for many people.
sentences.zh: 此外，他们相信（有充分的理由）对于许多人来说，CSAM 消费是通往 CSA 和 CSAM 生产的门户。

sentences.en: Further, finding CSAM can be a way to identify and rescue children from abusive situations.- CSA survivors may advocate for it often feel traumatized not just by the abuse, but by the fact that people might be continuously viewing that abuse.- Going after it winds up creating digital forensics specialists and task forces, which creates a special interest group within police departments which wants to continue focusing on CSAM but is not set up to do anything about CSA.I would recommend the podcast Hunting Warhead to anyone interested in learning more about this.
sentences.zh: 此外，找到 CSAM 可以成为识别和营救受虐待儿童的一种方法。- CSA 幸存者可能会经常感到创伤，不仅因为虐待，而且因为人们可能不断地观看虐待行为。- 追踪它最终会创建数字取证专家和工作组，这会在警察部门内创建一个特殊兴趣小组，希望继续关注 CSAM，但不会对 CSA 采取任何行动。我会向任何有兴趣学习的人推荐播客《狩猎弹头》有关此的更多信息。

blocks.html_en: I think it's similar with other liability issues, e.g., when a company happens to "lose" customer data through a breach. They will be on the hook for not having certain audits and certifications at regular intervals. Practically never will anyone be feeling any pain due to absolute disregard for basic common sense precautions to prevent issues in the first place. So usually, the pattern is that issues that can be outsourced to insurance will be handled by compliance departments - which don't care about the actual problems, just that the fallout from them is "managed" accordingly.

sentences.en: I think it's similar with other liability issues, e.g., when a company happens to "lose" customer data through a breach.

sentences.en: They will be on the hook for not having certain audits and certifications at regular intervals.
sentences.zh: 他们将因没有定期进行某些审核和认证而陷入困境。

sentences.zh: 实际上，任何人都不会因为完全无视基本的预防问题的常识性预防措施而感到任何痛苦。

blocks.html_en: That's actually a little funny. I work in compliance and we regularly work with the teams to improve processes that enhance security. I will say though, that the outsourced work that we send to consultants has the same sort of result you describe.

sentences.en: That's actually a little funny.
sentences.zh: 这其实有点好笑。

sentences.en: I work in compliance and we regularly work with the teams to improve processes that enhance security.
sentences.zh: 我遵守法规，我们定期与团队合作，改进流程以增强安全性。

sentences.en: I will say though, that the outsourced work that we send to consultants has the same sort of result you describe.
sentences.zh: 不过，我要说的是，我们发送给顾问的外包工作与您所描述的结果相同。

blocks.html_en:
> yet almost nothing seems to be done to prevent CSA

    > On the CSA side, ... [t]here doesn't seem to be any real push for educating and protecting kids before it happens.

This is a mighty wide brush you are painting with. When I was growing up, from very early (elementary school), we had "health class" where the teacher would teach you things about your body and health. This also included who is allowed to see you naked and/or touch your private parts. They also explained how to get help if someone what touching you inappropriately. That effort seems pretty active to me.

    > I personally know of 1 who took a leak across from a playground at 2am walking home from the bars and was put on the sex offender registry because it was within 500'.

Urinating in public late at night is an interesting category of inprobably CSA-labeled behaviour. It should be treated with kid gloves -- the context matters.

sentences.en: > yet almost nothing seems to be done to prevent CSA > On the CSA side, ... [t]here doesn't seem to be any real push for educating and protecting kids before it happens.
sentences.zh: > 但似乎几乎没有采取任何措施来阻止 CSA > 在 CSA 方面，...似乎没有在这种情况发生之前真正推动教育和保护儿童。

sentences.en: This is a mighty wide brush you are painting with.
sentences.zh: 这是您用来绘画的强大的宽画笔。

sentences.en: When I was growing up, from very early (elementary school), we had "health class" where the teacher would teach you things about your body and health.
sentences.zh: 在我成长的过程中，从很早（小学）开始，我们就有“健康课”，老师会教你有关身体和健康的知识。

sentences.en: This also included who is allowed to see you naked and/or touch your private parts.
sentences.zh: 这还包括谁可以看到您的裸体和/或触摸您的私处。

sentences.en: They also explained how to get help if someone what touching you inappropriately.
sentences.zh: 他们还解释了如果有人不恰当地触摸您，如何寻求帮助。

sentences.en: That effort seems pretty active to me. > I personally know of 1 who took a leak across from a playground at 2am walking home from the bars and was put on the sex offender registry because it was within 500'.
sentences.zh: 这种努力对我来说似乎相当积极。 > 我个人认识一个人，凌晨 2 点从酒吧步行回家，在操场对面撒尿，并被列入性犯罪者登记册，因为距离在 500 英尺之内。

sentences.en: Urinating in public late at night is an interesting category of inprobably CSA-labeled behaviour.
sentences.zh: 深夜在公共场合小便是一种有趣的、不太可能被贴上 CSA 标签的行为。

sentences.en: It should be treated with kid gloves -- the context matters.
sentences.zh: 应该谨慎对待它——上下文很重要。

sentences.zh: 这还包括谁可以看到您的裸体和/或触摸您的私处。

sentences.en: They also explained how to get help if someone what touching you inappropriately.
sentences.zh: 他们还解释了如果有人不恰当地触摸您，如何寻求帮助。

sentences.en: That effort seems pretty active to me. > I personally know of 1 who took a leak across from a playground at 2am walking home from the bars and was put on the sex offender registry because it was within 500'.
sentences.zh: 这种努力对我来说似乎相当积极。 > 我个人认识一个人，凌晨 2 点从酒吧步行回家，在操场对面撒尿，并被列入性犯罪者登记册，因为距离在 500 英尺之内。

sentences.en: Urinating in public late at night is an interesting category of inprobably CSA-labeled behaviour.
sentences.zh: 深夜在公共场合小便是一种有趣的、不太可能被贴上 CSA 标签的行为。

sentences.en: It should be treated with kid gloves -- the context matters.
sentences.zh: 应该谨慎对待它——上下文很重要。

blocks.html_en: This is a mighty wide brush you are painting with. When I was growing up, from very early (elementary school), we had "health class" where the teacher would teach you things about your body and health. This also included who is allowed to see you naked and/or touch your private parts. They also explained how to get help if someone what touching you inappropriately. That effort seems pretty active to me.

sentences.en: This is a mighty wide brush you are painting with.
sentences.zh: 这是您用来绘画的强大的宽画笔。

blocks.html_en:
First, I think it’s likely that you hear more about one than the other because you read websites like Hacker News and are not (I assume) an FBI agent or someone else who works on this stuff. A public defender I know would say that child abuse of all sorts is ever-present and relevant to their work, unfortunately.

Second, detecting CSAM leads to its producers who are by definition abusers. Here’s a nice article in Wired about the digital forensics of cracking down on a CSAM ring with some interesting details about the role played by crypto, and abusers’ misconceptions about it: https://www.wired.com/story/tracers-in-the-dark-welcome-to-v...

With that said, I agree that a lot of political concern for this is a smokescreen for a creating more surveillance. “think of the children!” has the flavor of a rhetorical trump card.

sentences.en: First, I think it’s likely that you hear more about one than the other because you read websites like Hacker News and are not (I assume) an FBI agent or someone else who works on this stuff.
sentences.zh: 首先，我认为你可能对其中一个比另一个了解得更多，因为你阅读像黑客新闻这样的网站，并且不是（我认为）联邦调查局特工或从事这方面工作的其他人。

sentences.en: A public defender I know would say that child abuse of all sorts is ever-present and relevant to their work, unfortunately.Second, detecting CSAM leads to its producers who are by definition abusers.
sentences.zh: 我认识的一位公设辩护人会说，不幸的是，各种形式的儿童虐待现象始终存在，并且与他们的工作相关。其次，检测 CSAM 会导致其生产者根据定义是虐待者。

sentences.en: Here’s a nice article in Wired about the digital forensics of cracking down on a CSAM ring with some interesting details about the role played by crypto, and abusers’ misconceptions about it: https://www.wired.com/story/tracers-in-the-dark-welcome-to-v...With that said, I agree that a lot of political concern for this is a smokescreen for a creating more surveillance.
sentences.zh: 《连线》杂志上有一篇关于打击 CSAM 环的数字取证的好文章，其中包含一些关于加密货币所扮演的角色的有趣细节，以及滥用者对此的误解：https://www.wired.com/story/tracers-in-the-dark-welcome-to-v ...话虽如此，我同意对此的许多政治担忧只是创建更多监视的烟幕弹。

sentences.en: “think of the children!”
sentences.zh: “想想孩子们吧！”

sentences.en: has the flavor of a rhetorical trump card.
sentences.zh: 有修辞王牌的味道。

blocks.html_en: CSAM laws in many jurisdictions include computer generated or hand crafted media that do not pre-require CSA at all. The idea is that deviance leads to CSA, and by cracking down on CSAM, we are preventing CSA.

sentences.en: CSAM laws in many jurisdictions include computer generated or hand crafted media that do not pre-require CSA at all.
sentences.zh: 许多司法管辖区的 CSAM 法律包括计算机生成或手工制作的媒体，根本不需要 CSA。

sentences.en: The idea is that deviance leads to CSA, and by cracking down on CSAM, we are preventing CSA.
sentences.zh: 我们的想法是，越轨行为会导致 CSA，而通过打击 CSAM，我们正在防止 CSA。

blocks.html_en: This is such a slippery slope. It amounts to thought police. You did not actually cause any harm, but thought of doing something. Where do we stop ? If I fantasize about my pretty coworker without her consent, am I a rapist now? What if I make a sketch of her naked? Is that a crime? What if I ask AI to remove her clothes from a real picture? Even if I just immediately delete it?? I guess that there’s different levels to each “offense”, and at one level it does become criminal, but it’s hard to unequivocally decide where.

sentences.en: This is such a slippery slope.
sentences.zh: 这真是一个滑坡。

sentences.en: It amounts to thought police.
sentences.zh: 这相当于思想警察。

sentences.en: You did not actually cause any harm, but thought of doing something.
sentences.zh: 你实际上并没有造成任何伤害，只是想做点什么。

sentences.en: Where do we stop ?
sentences.zh: 我们在哪里停下来？

sentences.en: If I fantasize about my pretty coworker without her consent, am I a rapist now?
sentences.zh: 如果我在未经我漂亮同事同意的情况下对她产生幻想，我现在是强奸犯吗？

sentences.en: What if I make a sketch of her naked?
sentences.zh: 如果我给她画一张裸体素描怎么办？

sentences.en: Is that a crime?
sentences.zh: 这是犯罪吗？

sentences.en: What if I ask AI to remove her clothes from a real picture?
sentences.zh: 如果我要求人工智能从真实照片中去掉她的衣服会怎么样？

sentences.en: Even if I just immediately delete it??
sentences.zh: 即使我立即删除它？

sentences.en: I guess that there’s different levels to each “offense”, and at one level it does become criminal, but it’s hard to unequivocally decide where.
sentences.zh: 我想每种“犯罪”都有不同的级别，在某个级别上它确实成为犯罪，但很难明确决定在哪里。

blocks.html_en:
> On the CSA side, you rarely hear about arrests (they happen but less than CSAM).

Because it is nearly always someone we know. Someone who we just cannot imagine would ever do such a thing, even when the evidence is glaringly obvious. And I suspect that the fraction of the population diddling kids in real life is breathtaking, and nobody really wants to face that head on. Too uncomfortable.

I could just be overly cynical today. But given my own experiences and other people I've known throughout my life, I really believe it is very common.

sentences.en: > On the CSA side, you rarely hear about arrests (they happen but less than CSAM).Because it is nearly always someone we know.
sentences.zh: > 在 CSA 方面，您很少听到逮捕的消息（这种情况发生，但比 CSAM 少）。因为几乎都是我们认识的人。

sentences.en: Someone who we just cannot imagine would ever do such a thing, even when the evidence is glaringly obvious.
sentences.zh: 我们无法想象有人会做出这样的事情，即使证据是显而易见的。

sentences.en: And I suspect that the fraction of the population diddling kids in real life is breathtaking, and nobody really wants to face that head on.
sentences.zh: 我怀疑现实生活中欺骗孩子的人比例是惊人的，没有人真正愿意面对这个问题。

sentences.en: Too uncomfortable.I could just be overly cynical today.
sentences.zh: 太不舒服了。我今天可能过于愤世嫉俗了。

sentences.en: But given my own experiences and other people I've known throughout my life, I really believe it is very common.
sentences.zh: 但考虑到我自己的经历和我一生中认识的其他人，我真的相信这是很常见的。

blocks.html_en: Because it is nearly always someone we know. Someone who we just cannot imagine would ever do such a thing, even when the evidence is glaringly obvious. And I suspect that the fraction of the population diddling kids in real life is breathtaking, and nobody really wants to face that head on. Too uncomfortable.

sentences.en: Because it is nearly always someone we know.
sentences.zh: 因为几乎总是我们认识的人。

sentences.en: Too uncomfortable.
sentences.zh: 太不舒服了。

blocks.html_en: I could just be overly cynical today. But given my own experiences and other people I've known throughout my life, I really believe it is very common.

sentences.en: I could just be overly cynical today.
sentences.zh: 我今天可能过于愤世嫉俗了。

sentences.en: But given my own experiences and other people I've known throughout my life, I really believe it is very common.
sentences.zh: 但考虑到我自己的经历和我一生中认识的其他人，我真的相信这是很常见的。

blocks.html_en: I thought it had never happened around me in my whole life, but my sister recently told me that when she just in primary school, a friend of my dad gave her a lift and while she was in the car, inserted his fingers on her. Like WTF! I really had no idea this could have happened in my environment. I guess it probably happened more than I know to others too uncomfortable to say anything, took my sister 30 years to say something.

blocks.html_en: Theory besides what others have said: remove the supply and you affect the demand. That's the theory anyway, I'm not sure it works like that; to make a parallel, punishing drug users didn't affect dealers/supply networks.

blocks.html_en:
It's not about children. It was never about children. How many politicians have gotten caught in Epstein's island by now?

It's all about surveilling the masses and keeping them under control. Children are merely one of the political weapons they use to make the masses accept any proposed solution, no matter how Orwellian. You're not against protecting children from drug trafficking, money laundering, child molesting terrorists, are you?

blocks.html_en:
I'd like to know the link between availability of csam and CSA. There seems to be an assumption that the former causes the latter.

That's not to say it's acceptable if illegally produced.

We have issues in the UK. So far they've banned 'rape' porn. And now they're talking about 'barely legal'

None of this seems to be based on any statistics showing this is actually harmful in any way.

sentences.en: I'd like to know the link between availability of csam and CSA.
sentences.zh: 我想知道 csam 和 CSA 可用性之间的联系。

sentences.en: There seems to be an assumption that the former causes the latter.That's not to say it's acceptable if illegally produced.We have issues in the UK.
sentences.zh: 似乎有一种假设是前者导致了后者。这并不是说非法生产是可以接受的。我们在英国遇到了问题。

sentences.en: So far they've banned 'rape' porn.
sentences.zh: 到目前为止，他们已经禁止“强奸”色情内容。

sentences.en: And now they're talking about 'barely legal'None of this seems to be based on any statistics showing this is actually harmful in any way.
sentences.zh: 现在他们正在谈论“几乎不合法”，这一切似乎都没有任何统计数据表明这实际上是有害的。

blocks.html_en:
It’s nothing to do with CSAM and all about surveilling who has which files and when, and who they send them to.

Even ADP in iCloud sends the hashes of the plaintext to Apple, non-e2ee. This allows them to see who has unique files, and when, and the networks of users to which they spread, and when.

sentences.en: It’s nothing to do with CSAM and all about surveilling who has which files and when, and who they send them to.Even ADP in iCloud sends the hashes of the plaintext to Apple, non-e2ee.
sentences.zh: 这与 CSAM 无关，而是监视谁拥有哪些文件、何时以及将这些文件发送给谁。甚至 iCloud 中的 ADP 也会将明文的哈希值发送给 Apple（非 e2ee）。

sentences.en: This allows them to see who has unique files, and when, and the networks of users to which they spread, and when.
sentences.zh: 这使他们能够看到谁拥有独特的文件，以及何时、以及这些文件传播到的用户网络以及何时。

blocks.html_en:
It’s nothing to do with CSAM and all about surveilling who has which files and when, and who they send them to.

Even ADP in iCloud sends the hashes of the plaintext to Apple, non-e2ee. This allows them to see who has unique files, and when, and the networks of users to which they spread, and when.

blocks.html_en:
> ones who have been involved in abuse scandals in the past (Churches, Scouts, etc)

And those groups still have lower rates of abuse than Schools which do a lot less training and enforcement of youth protection policies.

Children are still safer in Scouts and churches than in schools.

Just about every kid goes to school though, so people just prefer to sweep that under the rug and focus on targeting organizations they are not a part of or disagree with because they're easier to demonize and make fun of.

I work with kids, and I've had to take the Scouts and Catholic Church's youth protection training. They are both free to take online if anyone wants to check it out.

sentences.en: > ones who have been involved in abuse scandals in the past (Churches, Scouts, etc)And those groups still have lower rates of abuse than Schools which do a lot less training and enforcement of youth protection policies.Children are still safer in Scouts and churches than in schools.Just about every kid goes to school though, so people just prefer to sweep that under the rug and focus on targeting organizations they are not a part of or disagree with because they're easier to demonize and make fun of.I work with kids, and I've had to take the Scouts and Catholic Church's youth protection training.
sentences.zh: > 过去曾卷入虐待丑闻的组织（教堂、童子军等）这些团体的虐待率仍然低于学校，学校的培训和青少年保护政策的执行要少得多。孩子们在童子军和教堂里仍然比在学校更安全。尽管几乎每个孩子都会上学，所以人们只是更愿意将其掩盖起来，并专注于针对他们不属于或不同意的组织，因为他们更容易被妖魔化和取笑。我与孩子们一起工作，并且我必须参加童子军和天主教会的青少年保护培训。

sentences.en: They are both free to take online if anyone wants to check it out.
sentences.zh: 如果有人想查看的话，它们都可以免费在线观看。

"Rate" is an ambiguous word. Molested children per year is a rate just as molested children per total children is.

>something like 1 per 100,000

What is this? What number are you citing, and where are you getting it from?

sentences.zh: “率”是一个含糊不清的词。

sentences.zh: 您引用的数字是多少？从哪里得到的？

blocks.html_en:
A rate is just a ratio between two quantities. You've never heard speed defined as the rate of movement?

I might have interpreted your example as such if not for the "one too many" comment. It's strange to throw out a completely fictitious figure and then lament it as if it's real.

sentences.en: A rate is just a ratio between two quantities.
sentences.zh: 比率只是两个数量之间的比率。

sentences.en: You've never heard speed defined as the rate of movement?I might have interpreted your example as such if not for the "one too many" comment.
sentences.zh: 你从来没有听说过速度被定义为运动速率？如果不是因为“太多”的评论，我可能会这样解释你的例子。

sentences.en: It's strange to throw out a completely fictitious figure and then lament it as if it's real.
sentences.zh: 扔掉一个完全虚构的人物，然后哀叹它就好像它是真实的一样，这很奇怪。

> he will never produce an actual figure.

> It's strange to throw out a completely fictitious figure and then lament it as if it's real.

You two are insufferable.

Anyway:

If you do an online search, the first results you may get, say how hard it is to compare. Here is one comparison that I did find:

https://actheologian.com/2025/04/22/comparing-child-abuse-ra...

Please note, this is not where I got my figures years ago, as I no longer have access to that source.

> Public Schools ~450 per 100,000

> Boy Scouts ~100–200 per 100,000

> Protestant Churches Likely 1–5 per 100,000

> Catholic Church (Priests Only) ~1 per 100,000

Also note, something like 90% of the Boy Scout sexual abuse cases were prior to the 1970s before they started mandating adults to take youth protection training. Today, the Boy Scouts and Catholic Church have some of the most comprehensive youth protection traing around, way better than any schools' youth protection training according to educators that I've talked with.

Good riddance. Go sharpen your ideological axes somewhere else.

blocks.html_en:
No, you said explicitly that kids are sexually assaulted at a higher rate in schools than they are in churches or scouts.

You clarified that you mean per 100,000 children more are likely to be sexually assaulted in schools.

Usually when someone has a belief like this, it’s grounded by something that they read or learned somewhere. Where did you learn this? Why aren’t you sharing the source with everyone else so they can learn it too?

But there’s still nuance there that you’re ignoring as well. We could say that rate should be examined per hour because a kid will spend 30 hours per week at school vs a few hours per week at scouts or church. Without your source though, we have no idea what the rates are, if they are what you say, if there’s even a significant difference, etc.

Now you’re trying to act like a victim because people want clarity on what exactly you mean and where you learned it.

Nobody is “gaslighting” you, just give the receipts.

sentences.en: No, you said explicitly that kids are sexually assaulted at a higher rate in schools than they are in churches or scouts.You clarified that you mean per 100,000 children more are likely to be sexually assaulted in schools.Usually when someone has a belief like this, it’s grounded by something that they read or learned somewhere.
sentences.zh: 不，你明确表示，孩子在学校里遭受性侵犯的比例比在教堂或童子军中的比例要高。你澄清说，你的意思是每 100,000 个儿童中，有更多的孩子在学校里遭受性侵犯的可能性更大。通常，当某人有这样的信念时，它是基于他们在某处读到或学到的东西。

sentences.en: Where did you learn this?
sentences.zh: 你从哪里学到这个？

sentences.en: Why aren’t you sharing the source with everyone else so they can learn it too?But there’s still nuance there that you’re ignoring as well.
sentences.zh: 您为什么不与其他人共享源代码，以便他们也可以学习？但是您仍然忽略了其中的细微差别。

sentences.en: We could say that rate should be examined per hour because a kid will spend 30 hours per week at school vs a few hours per week at scouts or church.
sentences.zh: 我们可以说应该按小时检查费率，因为孩子每周会在学校度过 30 个小时，而每周在童子军或教堂度过几个小时。

sentences.en: Without your source though, we have no idea what the rates are, if they are what you say, if there’s even a significant difference, etc.Now you’re trying to act like a victim because people want clarity on what exactly you mean and where you learned it.Nobody is “gaslighting” you, just give the receipts.
sentences.zh: 但是，如果没有您的消息来源，我们不知道费率是多少，是否如您所说，是否存在显着差异等等。现在您试图表现得像受害者，因为人们想清楚您的意思以及您从哪里学到的。没有人“煤气灯”您，只需提供收据即可。

"Don't worry Timmy, even though you were molested at school, because you're forced to go there every (week)day and spend more time there than church, Boy Scouts, etc, you statistically had less of a chance per hour of being molested."

Per hour makes zero sense.

Do you have something against churches or scouts? I can't imagine why you are being ao convoluted otherwise.

> Nobody is “gaslighting” you, just give the receipts.

I already gave the "receipts". Stop trying to gaslight me.

...

FYI everyone else: the Boy Scouts has a "two deep" leadership policy, meaning no private one-on-one contact between youth and adults. That's why 90+% of cases were from over 50 years ago. And don't believe everything you read on reddit, because I saw one comment where someone claimed they were abused as a kid by their troop manager... No one who actually was a Scout would call their Scoutmaster a "troop manager". It almost certainly was someone who was ideologically opposed to Boy Scouts. (If someone can teach me how to use reddit's aweful search function to find that comment, I'll gladly provide that "receipt".)

blocks.html_en:
I'm not a Catholic (nor religious at all), and do question whether schools truly are worse than churches in terms of how children are treated.

But that's not the point. You made up a quote I didn't say ("demonization"), and accused the former poster of doing something they weren't: defending the church.

Saying that schools are worse yet ignored is not defending the church. It may also be incorrect, but that's a separate assertion.

blocks.html_en:
That was in the comment that started this chain. Are you using an alt? I'm not sure why this is so hard for you to follow. If I were you I would take a breath and read the chain again.

Here I'll help you:

> Just about every kid goes to school though, so people just prefer to sweep that under the rug and focus on targeting organizations they are not a part of or disagree with because they're easier to demonize and make fun of.

In what world is that useful to your sanitized claim? Is it not an obviously underhanded apology for the conduct of the church? Is the aggrieved nature of this really lost on you?

blocks.html_en:
Some HN users probably are, but not all, and I doubt not a majority. A majority might not be religious, but not being religious does not automatically mean anti-religion. There are occasionally comments that show an espousal of a god that do get upvotes.

Calling someone's god, an "imaginary friend" is probably a trait of someone very anti-religious though. I haven't read all the different religious texts, but I highly doubt any of them call their god a friend. Perhaps Jesus could be considered friendly, but I don't think the Bible says that a Christian must consider Jesus as their friend. I think it's more like how parents should be parents and not friends. I imagine in most if not all relgions, gods should be gods and not friends.

Please tell me which part of your brain is engaged when you converse with your imaginary friend?

Is that really the best reply you could conjure? Maybe you can produce some evidence absolving the Catholic Church here? Or are you also an apologist for institutions that call themselves sacred engaging in systemic sex abuse?

blocks.html_en:
The problem is, the things that would actually prevent CSA require things like "giving children rights", "widely mandating effective age-appropriate sex education even for young children", and "admitting that it's mostly not scary strangers doing it."

These are utterly anathema to huge chunks of society, especially American society. It's more and more clear, from the scope of the Epstein Files, just how much of American society and government has been influenced specifically to enable easy access to children by rich white men. Even beyond that, the entire right wing would instantly catch fire if we actually required effective sex education. Especially age-appropriate sex education going right down to kindergarten (yes, kindergarten: the better children that age understand what's normal and safe, the better they can communicate when someone is doing things to them that are not that...and I only stop at kindergarten because AFAIK that's the earliest mandated schooling still). Similarly, the right wing desperately wants to have absolute control over their children—treat them as property—so giving children rights that even parents have to respect will get them to oppose absolutely everything.

Plus, as other sibling comments have already noted, the real desire here is for ubiquitous surveillance. CSAM is just the excuse they use.

sentences.en: The problem is, the things that would actually prevent CSA require things like "giving children rights", "widely mandating effective age-appropriate sex education even for young children", and "admitting that it's mostly not scary strangers doing it."These are utterly anathema to huge chunks of society, especially American society.
sentences.zh: 问题是，真正阻止CSA的事情需要诸如“给予儿童权利”、“广泛强制要求即使对幼儿也进行有效的适龄性教育”以及“承认这样做并不可怕的陌生人”。这些对于社会的大部分人，尤其是美国社会来说是完全令人厌恶的。

sentences.en: It's more and more clear, from the scope of the Epstein Files, just how much of American society and government has been influenced specifically to enable easy access to children by rich white men.
sentences.zh: 从爱泼斯坦档案的范围来看，越来越清楚的是，美国社会和政府在多大程度上受到了专门的影响，以使得富有的白人能够轻松接触到孩子。

sentences.en: Even beyond that, the entire right wing would instantly catch fire if we actually required effective sex education.
sentences.zh: 即使除此之外，如果我们确实需要有效的性教育，整个右翼也会立即着火。

sentences.en: Especially age-appropriate sex education going right down to kindergarten (yes, kindergarten: the better children that age understand what's normal and safe, the better they can communicate when someone is doing things to them that are not that...and I only stop at kindergarten because AFAIK that's the earliest mandated schooling still).
sentences.zh: 特别是适合年龄的性教育一直延续到幼儿园（是的，幼儿园：同龄的孩子越了解什么是正常和安全的，当有人对他们做不正常的事情时，他们就能更好地沟通……我只在幼儿园停止，因为据我所知，这仍然是最早的强制学校教育）。

sentences.en: Similarly, the right wing desperately wants to have absolute control over their children—treat them as property—so giving children rights that even parents have to respect will get them to oppose absolutely everything.Plus, as other sibling comments have already noted, the real desire here is for ubiquitous surveillance.

sentences.en: CSAM is just the excuse they use.

blocks.html_en:
The problem is, the things that would actually prevent CSA require things like "giving children rights", "widely mandating effective age-appropriate sex education even for young children", and "admitting that it's mostly not scary strangers doing it."

These are utterly anathema to huge chunks of society, especially American society. It's more and more clear, from the scope of the Epstein Files, just how much of American society and government has been influenced specifically to enable easy access to children by rich white men. Even beyond that, the entire right wing would instantly catch fire if we actually required effective sex education. Especially age-appropriate sex education going right down to kindergarten (yes, kindergarten: the better children that age understand what's normal and safe, the better they can communicate when someone is doing things to them that are not that...and I only stop at kindergarten because AFAIK that's the earliest mandated schooling still). Similarly, the right wing desperately wants to have absolute control over their children—treat them as property—so giving children rights that even parents have to respect will get them to oppose absolutely everything.

Plus, as other sibling comments have already noted, the real desire here is for ubiquitous surveillance. CSAM is just the excuse they use.

blocks.html_en:
To be clear, I recognize that there is zero chance of genuine effective age-appropriate sex education being mandated in the foreseeable future in the US, nor has there been any such chance at any time in the past. I'm merely noting some of the things that would actually be helpful measures in combating CSA if it were possible to implement them.

You're absolutely right that, even if such a mandate had already existed, it would've been erased or co-opted by right-wing groups by now.

To be even clearer, genuine effective age-appropriate sex education absolutely includes education on what it means to be trans, gay, bi, ace, and every other part of the queer rainbow, and that all of these things are just as good and normal as being straight, cis, etc.

Take your bigotry and stuff it.

blocks.html_en: Arresting for CSAM is easy and brings in a lot of money. It’s a slam dunk case for just possession. Investigating and prosecuting people for CSA takes a lot of manpower and time for single case and not nearly as profitable. As usual, follow the money.

sentences.en: Arresting for CSAM is easy and brings in a lot of money.
sentences.zh: 逮捕 CSAM 很容易，而且可以赚很多钱。

sentences.en: It’s a slam dunk case for just possession.
sentences.zh: 对于控球而言，这就是一个灌篮高手。

sentences.en: Investigating and prosecuting people for CSA takes a lot of manpower and time for single case and not nearly as profitable.
sentences.zh: 对单个案件进行调查和起诉 CSA 需要花费大量的人力和时间，而且利润并不高。

sentences.en: As usual, follow the money.
sentences.zh: 像往常一样，跟着钱走。

It is crazy people think apple isnt on the side of privacy. Are they perfect? Not even close, but compared to the rest of big tech theyre simply on another level.

Apple could easily not do this stuff and it may even be easier to not.

blocks.html_en:
> It is crazy people think apple isnt on the side of privacy.

> It also ensured pressure from governments and plaintiffs, including CSAM victims, who preferred Apple’s more interventionist approaches, which Apple had voluntarily demonstrated it was willing to do.

I feel that Apple open pandora's box with the client-side scanning. It proved that it was technically feasible, and was "privacy preserving". I use scare quotes there because I don't think that political or religious dissidents would find that the same or similar technology used to discover and persecute them is "privacy preserving". And that's really the problem with Apple here. They provided a model for scanning for any kind of message or material while purportedly maintaining privacy.

sentences.en: > It is crazy people think apple isnt on the side of privacy.> It also ensured pressure from governments and plaintiffs, including CSAM victims, who preferred Apple’s more interventionist approaches, which Apple had voluntarily demonstrated it was willing to do.I feel that Apple open pandora's box with the client-side scanning.
sentences.zh: > 人们认为苹果不站在隐私一边，这太疯狂了。> 它还确保了来自政府和原告的压力，包括 CSAM 受害者，他们更喜欢苹果更具干预性的方法，苹果已经自愿表明它愿意这样做。我觉得苹果通过客户端扫描打开了潘多拉魔盒。

sentences.zh: 事实证明，这在技术上是可行的，并且是“保护隐私的”。

sentences.en: And that's really the problem with Apple here.
sentences.zh: 这确实是苹果的问题所在。

sentences.en: It proved that it was technically feasible, and was "privacy preserving".
sentences.zh: 事实证明，这在技术上是可行的，并且是“保护隐私的”。

sentences.en: I use scare quotes there because I don't think that political or religious dissidents would find that the same or similar technology used to discover and persecute them is "privacy preserving".
sentences.zh: 我在那里使用恐吓引语是因为我不认为政治或宗教异见者会发现用于发现和迫害他们的相同或类似技术是“隐私保护”。

sentences.en: And that's really the problem with Apple here.
sentences.zh: 这确实是苹果的问题所在。

sentences.en: They provided a model for scanning for any kind of message or material while purportedly maintaining privacy.
sentences.zh: 他们提供了一种扫描任何类型的消息或材料的模型，同时据称还能保护隐私。

I use scare quotes there because I don't think that political or religious dissidents would find that the same or similar technology used to discover and persecute them is "privacy preserving". And that's really the problem with Apple here. They provided a model for scanning for any kind of message or material while purportedly maintaining privacy.

blocks.html_en:
> It proved that it was technically feasible, and was "privacy preserving".

Didn't their paper disproved by reversing the perceptual hashes to reveal blurred version of the images being hashed, and Apple basically said "that's fair, it's not as robust as we wanted, let's visit this later"?

If not, I'll happily stand corrected, but please share sources.

Addenda:

- Apple's original paper: https://web.archive.org/web/20210807165030/https://www.apple...

- Paper breaking the hash: https://arxiv.org/abs/2111.06628

Edit: The second one is the wrong paper. I’ll find and link the correct one tomorrow. Keeping the link for transparency.

blocks.html_en: The paper you linked doesn’t reveal blurred versions of the images being hashed. It does train a classifier to determine which of 1,000 ImageNet classes an image belongs to, which “achieved a top-1 test accuracy of 4.34%”.

blocks.html_en:
> to reveal blurred version of the images being hashed

Skimmed your linked paper. It seems they were able to classify hashes up to ~8% top-1 accuracy and ~30% top-10. Not exactly a blurred version, or any images at all.

So for example, they can say that you probably have images of trees, or images of buildings, but without much other data & very low accuracy.

I'd still be a lot more concerned about them simply flagging political images rather than trying to get a broad understanding of what type of photos I have

sentences.en: > to reveal blurred version of the images being hashedSkimmed your linked paper.
sentences.zh: > 显示正在散列的图像的模糊版本略读您链接的论文。

sentences.en: It seems they were able to classify hashes up to ~8% top-1 accuracy and ~30% top-10.
sentences.zh: 看来他们能够对哈希进行分类，top-1 准确率高达约 8%，top-10 准确率约 30%。

sentences.en: Not exactly a blurred version, or any images at all.So for example, they can say that you probably have images of trees, or images of buildings, but without much other data & very low accuracy.I'd still be a lot more concerned about them simply flagging political images rather than trying to get a broad understanding of what type of photos I have
sentences.zh: 不完全是模糊版本，或根本不是任何图像。例如，他们可以说您可能有树木的图像或建筑物的图像，但没有太多其他数据且准确性非常低。我仍然更关心他们只是简单地标记政治图像，而不是试图广泛了解我拥有什么类型的照片

blocks.html_en: This is not client side. Images just sitting on your Android phone are probably safe (although Google could push an update at any time). Your images get scanned when you back them up, send them over RCS, etc. I have even seen criminal cases originating from reverse image search - anything that touche the servers of the big tech companies, except apparently Apple, will be scanned using questionable AI and against a secret list to Protect the Children.

sentences.en: This is not client side.
sentences.zh: 这不是客户端。

sentences.en: Images just sitting on your Android phone are probably safe (although Google could push an update at any time).
sentences.zh: 放在 Android 手机上的图像可能是安全的（尽管 Google 可以随时推送更新）。

sentences.en: Your images get scanned when you back them up, send them over RCS, etc. I have even seen criminal cases originating from reverse image search - anything that touche the servers of the big tech companies, except apparently Apple, will be scanned using questionable AI and against a secret list to Protect the Children.
sentences.zh: 当你备份图像、通过 RCS 发送图像等时，你的图像会被扫描。我什至见过源自反向图像搜索的刑事案件——任何触及大型科技公司服务器的东西，显然苹果除外，都将使用可疑的人工智能进行扫描，并根据保护儿童的秘密名单进行扫描。

blocks.html_en:
This is an image that he did not send off of his device to anyone except his doctor's office, yet Google reached into his private data and scanned it anyway.

Google reported him to the police based on a single false positive.

To add insult to injury, even after the police contacted Google to tell them that they had cleared him of wrongdoing, Google refused to restore access to his account.

sentences.en: This is an image that he did not send off of his device to anyone except his doctor's office, yet Google reached into his private data and scanned it anyway.Google reported him to the police based on a single false positive.To add insult to injury, even after the police contacted Google to tell them that they had cleared him of wrongdoing, Google refused to restore access to his account.
sentences.zh: 这是一张他没有从他的设备上发送给除了他的医生办公室之外的任何人的图像，但谷歌仍然访问了他的私人数据并扫描了它。谷歌基于一个误报向警方举报了他。更雪上加霜的是，即使在警方联系谷歌告诉他们他们已经清除了他的不当行为之后，谷歌仍然拒绝恢复对他帐户的访问。

blocks.html_en: Informed consent would require Google to inform you that their AI server will scan every photo you take with your device and report you to the police if it should detect (or hallucinate) something it doesn't like.

sentences.en: Informed consent would require Google to inform you that their AI server will scan every photo you take with your device and report you to the police if it should detect (or hallucinate) something it doesn't like.
sentences.zh: 知情同意将要求谷歌通知你，他们的人工智能服务器将扫描你用你的设备拍摄的每张照片，并在检测到（或产生幻觉）它不喜欢的东西时向警方报告。

blocks.html_en:
On page 83 of subsection 14 of paragraph 3 of the consent document you agree to by using "google" as a verb, or by viewing any website they have a tracking pixel on there is a link to a policy which mentions that they retain the right to scan any and all of your photos for advertising targeting purposes, and further, if you read page 27 of the sub agreement mentioned in upside down white on white text in the main TOS they describe advertising purposes as: "Anything we want to do, in perpetuity, for any reason, and without any right of redress".

It's right there plain as day in the TOS. I don't know how people can use these products without understanding the contract they locked themselves into. /s

sentences.en: On page 83 of subsection 14 of paragraph 3 of the consent document you agree to by using "google" as a verb, or by viewing any website they have a tracking pixel on there is a link to a policy which mentions that they retain the right to scan any and all of your photos for advertising targeting purposes, and further, if you read page 27 of the sub agreement mentioned in upside down white on white text in the main TOS they describe advertising purposes as: "Anything we want to do, in perpetuity, for any reason, and without any right of redress".It's right there plain as day in the TOS.
sentences.zh: 在同意文件第 3 段第 14 小节第 83 页上，您同意使用“google”作为动词，或者通过查看他们在其上有跟踪像素的任何网站，其中有一个政策链接，其中提到他们保留出于广告定位目的扫描您的任何和所有照片的权利，此外，如果您阅读主要 TOS 中以白字颠倒的白字文本提到的子协议的第 27 页，他们将广告目的描述为：“我们想做的任何事情，出于任何原因，永久有效，并且没有任何补救权利”。 TOS 中的条款一目了然。

sentences.en: I don't know how people can use these products without understanding the contract they locked themselves into. /s
sentences.zh: 我不知道人们如何在不了解他们所锁定的合同的情况下使用这些产品。 /秒

blocks.html_en:
You do know that we are discussing something that Apple talked about doing, but never did vs. something that Google has been doing for years?

And no, this particular article about Google attempting to have innocent parents arrested was published before Apple even discussed scanning images users manually uploaded to their publicly accessible web album on iCloud.

sentences.en: You do know that we are discussing something that Apple talked about doing, but never did vs. something that Google has been doing for years?And no, this particular article about Google attempting to have innocent parents arrested was published before Apple even discussed scanning images users manually uploaded to their publicly accessible web album on iCloud.
sentences.zh: 你知道我们正在讨论苹果说过要做但从未做过的事情，而谷歌多年来一直在做的事情吗？不，这篇关于谷歌试图逮捕无辜父母的特别文章是在苹果讨论扫描用户手动上传到 iCloud 上可公开访问的网络相册的图像之前发表的。

blocks.html_en: Engineers tend to be too people-pleasing. When someone from management asks "can you scan devices for CSAM without violating privacy?" the answer should not be "hmm... well... maybe if we did it this way it could work". The answer you should give us "no" or to hedge your bets "I don't think so". Same to the government and the court. In particular a court isn't asking for a statement of objective reality whether it's possible, they're asking whether you think you could do it.

sentences.en: Engineers tend to be too people-pleasing.
sentences.zh: 工程师往往太讨人喜欢了。

sentences.en: When someone from management asks "can you scan devices for CSAM without violating privacy?"
sentences.zh: 当管理层有人问“你能在不侵犯隐私的情况下扫描设备中的 CSAM 吗？”

sentences.en: the answer should not be "hmm...
sentences.zh: 答案不应该是“嗯...

sentences.en: well...
sentences.zh: 出色地...

sentences.en: maybe if we did it this way it could work".
sentences.zh: 如果我们这样做的话，也许它会起作用”。

sentences.en: The answer you should give us "no" or to hedge your bets "I don't think so".
sentences.zh: 您应该给我们“不”的答案，或者对冲您的赌注“我不这么认为”。

sentences.en: Same to the government and the court.
sentences.zh: 政府和法院也是如此。

sentences.en: In particular a court isn't asking for a statement of objective reality whether it's possible, they're asking whether you think you could do it.
sentences.zh: 特别是，法院不会要求您提供客观现实的陈述是否可能，而是询问您是否认为您可以做到。

blocks.html_en:
I thought the client side scanning was to protect children? If it suspects an image is bad, it blurs it and pops up a warning including a link to resources to go to for help.

Very different than trying to narc out users to the authorities.

sentences.en: I thought the client side scanning was to protect children?
sentences.zh: 我以为客户端扫描是为了保护儿童？

blocks.html_en:
> The other is detection of images that may contain nudity, whether sent or received, when the owner/admin/parent enables the feature. It is relatively uncontroversial [...]

That's a new version. The one that as announced the same time as client side scanning to block uploading CSAM to iCloud worked like this.

1. It could be enabled on a child's device by the parents. It was not on be default.

2. If the child received a sexual image (not necessarily just CSAM...if an adult sends a dick pic to a child that is not CSAM but would have been flagged) the image is blocked, the child is notified, told their parents are worried the image may harm them, and asked if they still want to see it.

3. If the child says no, they do not want to see it, that is the end of the matter.

4. If the child says that they do want to see it and they are at least 13 they are shown the image and that is the end of the matter.

5. If the child says that they do want to see it and they are under 13, they are again told that they parents are concerned, and that if they view it their parents will be notified, and asked if they still want to view it.

6. If they say no that is the end of the matter.

7. If they say yes they see it but the parents also are notified and will be able to see it.

This should have been pretty uncontroversial, but there were objections on the grounds that if someone say sends their dick pic to your under 13 child and the child goes all the way through to step 7 and decides to view it, that is a violation of the sender's privacy because that message was only intended for the child.

sentences.en: > The other is detection of images that may contain nudity, whether sent or received, when the owner/admin/parent enables the feature.
sentences.zh: > 另一个是当所有者/管理员/家长启用该功能时，检测可能包含裸露的图像，无论是发送还是接收。

sentences.en: It is relatively uncontroversial [...]That's a new version.
sentences.zh: 这是相对没有争议的[...]这是一个新版本。

sentences.en: The one that as announced the same time as client side scanning to block uploading CSAM to iCloud worked like this.1.
sentences.zh: 与客户端扫描同时宣布的阻止将 CSAM 上传到 iCloud 的操作如下。1。

sentences.en: It could be enabled on a child's device by the parents.
sentences.zh: 父母可以在孩子的设备上启用它。

sentences.en: It was not on be default.2.
sentences.zh: 它不是默认的。2。

sentences.en: If the child received a sexual image (not necessarily just CSAM...if an adult sends a dick pic to a child that is not CSAM but would have been flagged) the image is blocked, the child is notified, told their parents are worried the image may harm them, and asked if they still want to see it.3.
sentences.zh: 如果孩子收到色情图片（不一定只是 CSAM...如果成人向孩子发送不是 CSAM 的鸡巴图片，但会被标记），该图片将被屏蔽，孩子会收到通知，告诉他们的父母担心该图片可能会伤害他们，并询问他们是否仍然想看。3。

sentences.en: If the child says no, they do not want to see it, that is the end of the matter.4.
sentences.zh: 如果孩子说不，他们不想看到，那就结束了。 4．

sentences.en: If the child says that they do want to see it and they are at least 13 they are shown the image and that is the end of the matter.5.
sentences.zh: 如果孩子说他们确实想看它并且他们至少 13 岁，他们就会看到图像，事情就结束了。5。

sentences.en: If the child says that they do want to see it and they are under 13, they are again told that they parents are concerned, and that if they view it their parents will be notified, and asked if they still want to view it.6.
sentences.zh: 如果孩子说他们确实想看，而且他们未满 13 岁，他们会再次被告知他们的父母很担心，如果他们观看，他们的父母会收到通知，并询问他们是否仍然想看。6。

sentences.en: If they say no that is the end of the matter.7.
sentences.zh: 如果他们说不，事情就结束了。7。

sentences.en: If they say yes they see it but the parents also are notified and will be able to see it.This should have been pretty uncontroversial, but there were objections on the grounds that if someone say sends their dick pic to your under 13 child and the child goes all the way through to step 7 and decides to view it, that is a violation of the sender's privacy because that message was only intended for the child.
sentences.zh: 如果他们同意，他们会看到它，但父母也会收到通知，并且能够看到它。这本来应该是相当没有争议的，但有人反对，因为如果有人说将他们的阴茎照片发送给您 13 岁以下的孩子，而孩子一直走到第 7 步并决定查看它，那就侵犯了发件人的隐私，因为该消息仅供孩子使用。

blocks.html_en:
One of the more recent changes is that anyone can now enable a version of this functionality, even if they aren't set up with a child account. (It's under Settings > Messages > Sensitive Content Warning.) In this mode, it behaves similarly to the over-13 mode described above; no one is notified, whether the user chooses to view the image or not.

This mode is probably best understood as "if someone texts me a dick pic, please blur it". I don't think there's any reasonable objection to this.

sentences.en: One of the more recent changes is that anyone can now enable a version of this functionality, even if they aren't set up with a child account.
sentences.zh: 最近的变化之一是，任何人现在都可以启用此功能的某个版本，即使他们没有设置儿童帐户。

sentences.en: (It's under Settings > Messages > Sensitive Content Warning.)
sentences.zh: （位于“设置”>“消息”>“敏感内容警告”下。）

sentences.en: In this mode, it behaves similarly to the over-13 mode described above; no one is notified, whether the user chooses to view the image or not.This mode is probably best understood as "if someone texts me a dick pic, please blur it".
sentences.zh: 在此模式下，其行为与上述 over-13 模式类似；无论用户是否选择查看图像，都不会通知任何人。这种模式最好的理解可能是“如果有人给我发了一张鸡巴照片，请对其进行模糊处理”。

sentences.en: I don't think there's any reasonable objection to this.
sentences.zh: 我认为对此没有任何合理的反对意见。

blocks.html_en:
I have a hard time imagining any significant number of people objecting based on the sender's privacy. An adult sending explicit images to a child is a crime in most jurisdictions. A child doing it with a recipient under 13 is a concerning behavior the parents need to address.

Notifying the parents even requires that the child acknowledge that's what's going to happen.

sentences.en: I have a hard time imagining any significant number of people objecting based on the sender's privacy.
sentences.zh: 我很难想象会有多少人因为发件人的隐私而提出反对。

sentences.en: An adult sending explicit images to a child is a crime in most jurisdictions.
sentences.zh: 在大多数司法管辖区，成人向儿童发送露骨图像均属犯罪行为。

sentences.en: A child doing it with a recipient under 13 is a concerning behavior the parents need to address.Notifying the parents even requires that the child acknowledge that's what's going to happen.
sentences.zh: 孩子与 13 岁以下的接受者一起这样做是一种令人担忧的行为，父母需要解决这一问题。通知父母甚至要求孩子承认将会发生这种情况。

blocks.html_en:
The above proposed system could get ugly...

Family A enables system.

Kid B sends dick pic to Kid A.

Kid A decides to view it, Apple notifies and makes it viewable to Parents A.

Parents A are now guilty of possessing CSAM. (Distributed by Apple for extra fun)

sentences.en: The above proposed system could get ugly...Family A enables system.Kid B sends dick pic to Kid A.Kid A decides to view it, Apple notifies and makes it viewable to Parents A.Parents A are now guilty of possessing CSAM.
sentences.zh: 上述提议的系统可能会变得丑陋......家庭 A 启用系统。孩子 B 向孩子 A 发送迪克照片。孩子 A 决定查看它，Apple 通知并使其可供父母 A 查看。父母 A 现在因拥有 CSAM 而有罪。

sentences.en: (Distributed by Apple for extra fun)
