blocks.html_en: The controversial part is having the system enabled by default with age verification required to turn it off, and having the system impact non-Apple/Google apps. The UK for example wants Apple and Google to forcibly enable nudity blocking on all devices in the UK, and they want the system to bypass app/DRM security to scan all content visible on a device.

blocks.html_en:
While I didn’t agree with Apple’s approach, there is a subtle but IMHO critical nuance that is often forgotten about the client side scanning. It was only generating fingerprint material which Apple could decrypt and scan, alongside original images sent E2E encrypted to Apple servers. If the image wasn’t sent to Apple, the fingerprint wasn’t either.

And the reporting was still gated through Apple employees; there was no mechanism for the government to expand the scope without Apple’s knowledge.

I understand that people don’t like the idea of their personal hardware being complicit in treachery, but when considered from a purely functional perspective, apple’s proposal was no different to what Google and others were already doing.

sentences.en: While I didn’t agree with Apple’s approach, there is a subtle but IMHO critical nuance that is often forgotten about the client side scanning.
sentences.zh: 虽然我不同意苹果的做法，但在客户端扫描方面，有一个微妙但至关重要的细微差别经常被遗忘。

sentences.en: It was only generating fingerprint material which Apple could decrypt and scan, alongside original images sent E2E encrypted to Apple servers.
sentences.zh: 它只是生成苹果可以解密和扫描的指纹材料，以及将 E2E 加密发送到苹果服务器的原始图像。

sentences.en: If the image wasn’t sent to Apple, the fingerprint wasn’t either.And the reporting was still gated through Apple employees; there was no mechanism for the government to expand the scope without Apple’s knowledge.I understand that people don’t like the idea of their personal hardware being complicit in treachery, but when considered from a purely functional perspective, apple’s proposal was no different to what Google and others were already doing.
sentences.zh: 如果图像没有发送给苹果，那么指纹也没有发送给苹果。而且报告仍然是通过苹果员工进行的；政府没有机制可以在苹果不知情的情况下扩大范围。我知道人们不喜欢他们的个人硬件参与背叛的想法，但从纯粹的功能角度考虑时，苹果的提议与谷歌和其他公司已经在做的没有什么不同。

blocks.html_en:
The original proposal was to use a visual hash designed to identify known bad CSAM images even if cropped or otherwise edited. Your computer would scan all your stuff for these images and report you to apple who would report you to the cops. This presented a number of issues.

Accidental false positives could lead to horrific outcomes up to and including oh look bob got shot by the cops for resisting.

It was possible to produce apparently matching innocuous images and then poison people's machines with them.Oops did you click on that picture of a tree have fun with the cops. Like an advanced form of swatting.

Although inspired by a desire to find CSAM Apple could be forced to scan for ANYTHING by repressive regimes including America and China.

Although initially targeting images client side scanning of messages is a pretty obvious next step. Again obvious good motivation exists and is completely justifiable who doesn't want to stop the next mass shooting or terrorist attack... and then we can basically use it to find people critical of the regime. Do remember we are presently prosecuting a political figure for a picture of sea shells and a guy in texas is rotting in prison for distributing political literature.

sentences.en: The original proposal was to use a visual hash designed to identify known bad CSAM images even if cropped or otherwise edited.
sentences.zh: 最初的提议是使用视觉哈希，旨在识别已知的不良 CSAM 图像，即使经过裁剪或以其他方式编辑也是如此。

sentences.en: Your computer would scan all your stuff for these images and report you to apple who would report you to the cops.
sentences.zh: 你的电脑会扫描你所有的东西，寻找这些图像，然后将你报告给苹果公司，苹果公司再将你报告给警察。

sentences.en: This presented a number of issues.Accidental false positives could lead to horrific outcomes up to and including oh look bob got shot by the cops for resisting.It was possible to produce apparently matching innocuous images and then poison people's machines with them.Oops did you click on that picture of a tree have fun with the cops.
sentences.zh: 这提出了许多问题。意外的误报可能会导致可怕的结果，甚至包括哦，看鲍勃因抵抗而被警察开枪射击。有可能产生明显匹配的无害图像，然后用它们毒害人们的机器。哎呀，你点击那棵树的图片与警察玩得开心吗？

sentences.en: Like an advanced form of swatting.Although inspired by a desire to find CSAM Apple could be forced to scan for ANYTHING by repressive regimes including America and China.Although initially targeting images client side scanning of messages is a pretty obvious next step.
sentences.zh: 就像一种高级形式的打击。尽管受到寻找 CSAM 的渴望的启发，苹果可能会被迫扫描包括美国和中国在内的专制政权的任何东西。尽管最初针对图像，但客户端扫描消息是非常明显的下一步。

sentences.en: Again obvious good motivation exists and is completely justifiable who doesn't want to stop the next mass shooting or terrorist attack...
sentences.zh: 同样，明显的良好动机是存在的，并且是完全合理的，谁不想阻止下一次大规模枪击或恐怖袭击......

sentences.en: and then we can basically use it to find people critical of the regime.
sentences.zh: 然后我们基本上可以用它来寻找批评该政权的人。

sentences.en: Do remember we are presently prosecuting a political figure for a picture of sea shells and a guy in texas is rotting in prison for distributing political literature.
sentences.zh: 请记住，我们目前正在因一张贝壳图片而起诉一名政治人物，德克萨斯州的一名男子因散发政治文学而在监狱里腐烂。

sentences.zh: > 苹果可能会被迫扫描美国和中国等专制政权的任何内容。

blocks.html_en: Including Europe. Europe is ruled by people who think 1984 was an instruction manual.

sentences.en: Including Europe.
sentences.zh: 包括欧洲。

sentences.en: Europe is ruled by people who think 1984 was an instruction manual.
sentences.zh: 统治欧洲的人认为 1984 年是一本指导手册。

blocks.html_en:
Europe is a fractal mix of polities ruled by a constant flux of elected representatives, and consequently exhibits political behaviour from a wide range of constituencies. Each citizen agrees, presumably, with some policies and disagrees with others, then they vote as best as they are able - which is not very, since every large electorate in the world falls short of adequate political competence. Some of those people vote for authoritarians, which i consider a shame but which is also true in your closest democratic nation.

European citizen surveillance is something i oppose, but at least it's balanced by a robust bill of human rights. America's surveillance state does not have that balance, because America wrote a bill of rights but didn't bother with the follow-through.

Kindly don't use my continent as a punching bag, thanks.

sentences.en: Europe is a fractal mix of polities ruled by a constant flux of elected representatives, and consequently exhibits political behaviour from a wide range of constituencies.
sentences.zh: 欧洲是一个由不断变化的民选代表统治的政体的分形混合体，因此表现出来自广泛选区的政治行为。

sentences.en: Each citizen agrees, presumably, with some policies and disagrees with others, then they vote as best as they are able - which is not very, since every large electorate in the world falls short of adequate political competence.
sentences.zh: 据推测，每个公民都同意某些政策，不同意其他政策，然后他们会尽其所能地投票——这并不是很好，因为世界上每个大选民都缺乏足够的政治能力。

sentences.en: Some of those people vote for authoritarians, which i consider a shame but which is also true in your closest democratic nation.European citizen surveillance is something i oppose, but at least it's balanced by a robust bill of human rights.
sentences.zh: 其中一些人投票支持独裁者，我认为这是一种耻辱，但在你们最接近的民主国家也是如此。我反对欧洲公民监视，但至少它被强有力的人权法案所平衡。

sentences.en: America's surveillance state does not have that balance, because America wrote a bill of rights but didn't bother with the follow-through.Kindly don't use my continent as a punching bag, thanks.
sentences.zh: 美国的监视国家没有这种平衡，因为美国写了一份权利法案，但没有费心去跟进。请不要用我的大陆作为出气筒，谢谢。

blocks.html_en:
I still also totally don't get their policy.

Trying to avoid false positives by not firing until a threshold was hit (was it 20 images?) seemed insane from a PR position... rightly or wrongly, all it would take would be the wrong court case and you can see the headlines:

"Apple says users can have up to 20 CSAM images on their phone before they'll tell police"

sentences.zh: 从公关职位来看似乎很疯狂......

blocks.html_en:
Imagine you have pictures of someones baptism and the kid was nude. Is it CSAM? I think the program would have to say use but morally I'd say no. The issue with client scanning is it has to assume the worst, or they are than liable. If its the person has 20+ different baptism of nude babys well huh that actually might be CSAM because the context of how that concentrated photos implies but even than its hard what if that person actually has 20 God Children its less crazy than one thinks... Especially if they have multiply phones from a single baptism.

You might not like pictures that way but honestly I think more important in procescuting CSAM is to go after the large sources of CSAM generation. Its trafficing in East Asia, and in Europe. I think weirdly America actually produces less CSAM in general because Americans are lot more off put by Sex than most other cultures. Abuse definitely happens in the US but making policy decisions like this produces bad policy.

Does iCloud rehost the photos to other people I don't really know because I use andriod tbh. If they are being rehosted (I assume to members of your contacts) that can be problematic but I think honestly the issue a lot more complex than just protect the children which the source of critic is a lot attacks against apples are coming from

sentences.en: Imagine you have pictures of someones baptism and the kid was nude.
sentences.zh: 想象一下，你有某人受洗的照片，而那个孩子是裸体的。

sentences.en: Is it CSAM?
sentences.zh: 是CSAM吗？

sentences.en: I think the program would have to say use but morally I'd say no.
sentences.zh: 我认为该程序必须说使用，但从道德上我会说不。

sentences.en: The issue with client scanning is it has to assume the worst, or they are than liable.
sentences.zh: 客户端扫描的问题是它必须假设最坏的情况，否则他们就要承担责任。

sentences.en: If its the person has 20+ different baptism of nude babys well huh that actually might be CSAM because the context of how that concentrated photos implies but even than its hard what if that person actually has 20 God Children its less crazy than one thinks...
sentences.zh: 如果这个人有 20 多个不同的裸体婴儿洗礼，嗯，那实际上可能是 CSAM，因为那张集中照片所暗示的背景，但即使比这更难，如果这个人实际上有 20 个上帝之子，它也没有人们想象的那么疯狂......

sentences.en: Especially if they have multiply phones from a single baptism.You might not like pictures that way but honestly I think more important in procescuting CSAM is to go after the large sources of CSAM generation.
sentences.zh: 特别是如果他们在一次洗礼中拥有多个手机。您可能不喜欢那样的图片，但老实说，我认为在执行 CSAM 过程中更重要的是追踪 CSAM 生成的大量来源。

sentences.en: Its trafficing in East Asia, and in Europe.
sentences.zh: 它在东亚和欧洲贩运。

sentences.en: I think weirdly America actually produces less CSAM in general because Americans are lot more off put by Sex than most other cultures.
sentences.zh: 我认为奇怪的是，美国实际上总体上产生的 CSAM 较少，因为美国人比大多数其他文化更对性感到反感。

sentences.en: Abuse definitely happens in the US but making policy decisions like this produces bad policy.Does iCloud rehost the photos to other people I don't really know because I use andriod tbh.
sentences.zh: 滥用行为肯定会在美国发生，但做出这样的政策决定会产生糟糕的政策。 iCloud 是否会将照片重新托管给其他人，我真的不知道，因为我使用的是 android tbh。

sentences.en: If they are being rehosted (I assume to members of your contacts) that can be problematic but I think honestly the issue a lot more complex than just protect the children which the source of critic is a lot attacks against apples are coming from
sentences.zh: 如果他们被重新托管（我假设是你的联系人），这可能会出现问题，但老实说，我认为这个问题比仅仅保护孩子要复杂得多，批评者的来源是对苹果的很多攻击都来自于此

blocks.html_en:
> America actually produces less CSAM in general because Americans are lot more off put by Sex than most other cultures

The US has the largest pornography industry in the world by a massive margin, and the largest consumption of online pornography per capita

Meanwhile should we be surprised that CSAM production is higher in countries like the Philippines that have very weak digital policing, abject poverty, high numbers of street children etc?

blocks.html_en:
Texas is particularly wild to me as a midwesterner. All the bibles and megachurches and all that and... They have strip clubs freaking everywhere! Really weird zoning laws as well - I recall seeing a strip club across the street from an Office Depot, next to a deli. The suburbs where I live, you have to go to a seedy part of town so you feel good and ashamed about it. lol

Some TV shows rightfully take aim at this contradiction (recently: The Hunting Wives).

blocks.html_en: The age of consent in the PI was like 14 until a couple years ago when they changed it to roughly match international norms, in their culture it was considered consensual rather than abuse until very recently.

sentences.en: The age of consent in the PI was like 14 until a couple years ago when they changed it to roughly match international norms, in their culture it was considered consensual rather than abuse until very recently.
sentences.zh: PI 中的同意年龄约为 14 岁，直到几年前他们将其更改为大致符合国际规范，在他们的文化中，直到最近才认为这是自愿的而不是滥用的。

blocks.html_en:
You can get married with parental or a judge consent at any age in Mississippi, New Mexico, and Oklahoma.

You can get married at 15 in Hawaii and Kansas.

You are "grandfathered" into sexual consent (not as in "marital rape" but "no longer statutory rape") when you do so.

So we may not want to be lording it too much on "international norms", particularly given that most of the marriages that happen at those ages are not "young couple got pregnant" but "older man in conservative/religious community".

sentences.en: You can get married with parental or a judge consent at any age in Mississippi, New Mexico, and Oklahoma.You can get married at 15 in Hawaii and Kansas.You are "grandfathered" into sexual consent (not as in "marital rape" but "no longer statutory rape") when you do so.So we may not want to be lording it too much on "international norms", particularly given that most of the marriages that happen at those ages are not "young couple got pregnant" but "older man in conservative/religious community".
sentences.zh: 在密西西比州、新墨西哥州和俄克拉荷马州，你可以在任何年龄在父母或法官同意的情况下结婚。在夏威夷和堪萨斯州，你可以在 15 岁结婚。当你这样做时，你就被“祖父”性同意（不是“婚内强奸”，而是“不再法定强奸”）。因此，我们可能不想过多地依赖“国际规范”，特别是考虑到大多数在这些年龄发生的婚姻都不是“年轻的”夫妇怀孕了”，但“保守/宗教社区的年长男子”。

Yes, in the sense that you have a legal doctor-patient privilege that binds what they can share with whom. There's not really an Apple cloud user privilege.

No, in the sense that your therapist is still required to report you to the police in various situations where you pose an immediate threat to yourself or others, etc.

blocks.html_en: A better analogy is a storage locker. AFAIK police need a warrant to search "your" storage locker even though it's on someone else's property. I don't see why data in the cloud should be any different. Pre-emptively scanning everyone's data is equivalent to officers rummaging through all the storage lockers in a facility "just in case" they find something illegal.

sentences.en: I don't see why data in the cloud should be any different.
sentences.zh: 我不明白为什么云中的数据应该有什么不同。

sentences.en: Pre-emptively scanning everyone's data is equivalent to officers rummaging through all the storage lockers in a facility "just in case" they find something illegal.

blocks.html_en: They are mandated to report child abuse. It is the same story with doctors, if an abusive parent brings in a child for care they learn never to give the kid healthcare again after the doctor reports it. It is rooted in good intentions but the effect is it means abused children never get to see doctors, therapists, get a half-ass minimal homeschool instead of going to school, etc so that mandated reporters never enter the picture.

sentences.en: They are mandated to report child abuse.
sentences.zh: 他们的任务是举报虐待儿童行为。

sentences.en: It is the same story with doctors, if an abusive parent brings in a child for care they learn never to give the kid healthcare again after the doctor reports it.
sentences.zh: 医生的情况也是如此，如果有虐待行为的父母带孩子来接受护理，他们就会知道在医生报告后不再为孩子提供医疗服务。

sentences.en: It is rooted in good intentions but the effect is it means abused children never get to see doctors, therapists, get a half-ass minimal homeschool instead of going to school, etc so that mandated reporters never enter the picture.
sentences.zh: 它植根于良好的意图，但结果是，它意味着受虐待的儿童永远无法去看医生、治疗师，无法接受最低限度的家庭教育而不是上学，等等，因此强制记者永远不会进入画面。

blocks.html_en:
> That's different from hearing their client saw a picture of the abuse of a total stranger.

I get it, actually. It's totally possible the picture in question was not known to authorities prior. That's called due diligence to look into it.

Adults not looking into things or following up on things are how the system fails children if you read some accounts of people who were abused by their guardians. Horrifying stuff.

sentences.en: Horrifying stuff.
sentences.zh: 可怕的东西。

blocks.html_en:
It could theoretically help to flag those images. But that kind of submission should be anonymous. It shouldn't ruin the ability for someone to get therapy.

Mandatory reporting makes sense for situations you are connected to. And even then there's presumably good reasons not everyone is a mandatory reporter. This goes way beyond that, mandatory reporting once removed for someone that doesn't know a single person involved.

Hell, reporting someone for that doesn't even guarantee the images get looked into! If they didn't save history they're probably not feeling like going back to the site to demonstrate. Similar if it was sent against their will and they deleted it right away.

sentences.en: It could theoretically help to flag those images.
sentences.zh: 从理论上讲，它可以帮助标记这些图像。

sentences.en: But that kind of submission should be anonymous.
sentences.zh: 但这种提交应该是匿名的。

sentences.en: It shouldn't ruin the ability for someone to get therapy.Mandatory reporting makes sense for situations you are connected to.
sentences.zh: 它不应该破坏某人获得治疗的能力。强制报告对于您所涉及的情况是有意义的。

sentences.en: And even then there's presumably good reasons not everyone is a mandatory reporter.
sentences.zh: 即便如此，也可能有充分的理由表明并非每个人都是强制性记者。

sentences.en: This goes way beyond that, mandatory reporting once removed for someone that doesn't know a single person involved.Hell, reporting someone for that doesn't even guarantee the images get looked into!
sentences.zh: 这远远超出了这个范围，一旦删除了对一个不认识任何人的人的强制报告。见鬼，报告某人甚至不能保证图像得到调查！

sentences.en: If they didn't save history they're probably not feeling like going back to the site to demonstrate.
sentences.zh: 如果他们没有保存历史记录，他们可能不想回到现场进行演示。

sentences.en: Similar if it was sent against their will and they deleted it right away.
sentences.zh: 如果是违背他们的意愿发送的，他们会立即将其删除，情况也是如此。

blocks.html_en:
Have you considered the implications of what you're saying?

A whistleblower goes to a therapist, stressed out over their pending decision to reveal official misconduct. They've been investigating ways to post something on the internet that can't be immediately taken down by the corrupt government officials they want to expose. They express their discomfort, in confidence, to their therapist, about using something they've discovered is also used for CSAM.

You think it's a good thing for the therapist to be required to report this? Should they report that the patient admitted to viewing CSAM with no context so the whistleblower gets investigated and arrested, or should they provide the context -- that the patient is about to expose the corruption of the government receiving the report?

For that matter, consider what it does when someone is actually a pedophile. They find out that if they try to seek therapy to address their perverse attraction to kids, the therapist isn't allowed to keep their confidence and they'll be arrested, so instead of seeking professional help, they keep abusing kids. Is that the result we wanted? There is a reason doctor-patient confidentiality was a thing.

sentences.en: Have you considered the implications of what you're saying?A whistleblower goes to a therapist, stressed out over their pending decision to reveal official misconduct.
sentences.zh: 你有没有考虑过你所说的话的含义？一名举报人去找治疗师，对他们即将做出的揭发官方不当行为的决定感到压力重重。

sentences.en: They've been investigating ways to post something on the internet that can't be immediately taken down by the corrupt government officials they want to expose.
sentences.zh: 他们一直在研究如何在互联网上发布一些内容，而这些内容不能被他们想要揭露的腐败政府官员立即删除。

sentences.en: They express their discomfort, in confidence, to their therapist, about using something they've discovered is also used for CSAM.You think it's a good thing for the therapist to be required to report this?
sentences.zh: 他们私下向治疗师表达了他们对使用他们发现也用于 CSAM 的东西的不安。您认为要求治疗师报告这一点是一件好事吗？

sentences.en: Should they report that the patient admitted to viewing CSAM with no context so the whistleblower gets investigated and arrested, or should they provide the context -- that the patient is about to expose the corruption of the government receiving the report?For that matter, consider what it does when someone is actually a pedophile.
sentences.zh: 他们应该在没有背景的情况下报告患者承认观看了 CSAM，以便举报者受到调查和逮捕，还是应该提供背景信息——患者即将揭露收到报告的政府的腐败行为？就此而言，考虑一下当某人实际上是恋童癖时它会做什么。

sentences.en: They find out that if they try to seek therapy to address their perverse attraction to kids, the therapist isn't allowed to keep their confidence and they'll be arrested, so instead of seeking professional help, they keep abusing kids.
sentences.zh: 他们发现，如果他们试图寻求治疗来解决对孩子的不正当吸引力，治疗师就不允许他们保持信心，他们会被逮捕，所以他们没有寻求专业帮助，而是继续虐待孩子。

sentences.en: Is that the result we wanted?
sentences.zh: 这就是我们想要的结果吗？

sentences.en: There is a reason doctor-patient confidentiality was a thing.
sentences.zh: 医患保密是有原因的。

blocks.html_en: I used to have a good friend that was a stripper (I promise, I wasn't the client...). Some of her biggest customers were people that wanted therapy without the paper trail of going to a licensed therapist. This is a big thing for pilots as well, since their health records and mental care are intensely scrutinized. A stripper will provide comfort, verbal relief, and physical love for $100 hour you can tell them anything and their reputation is bad enough no one will bother to believe them if they say something bad about you.

sentences.en: I used to have a good friend that was a stripper (I promise, I wasn't the client...).

sentences.en: Some of her biggest customers were people that wanted therapy without the paper trail of going to a licensed therapist.
sentences.zh: 她的一些最大的客户是那些想要接受治疗而不需要去找有执照的治疗师的书面记录的人。

sentences.zh: 这对飞行员来说也是一件大事，因为他们的健康记录和心理护理都受到严格审查。

sentences.en: A stripper will provide comfort, verbal relief, and physical love for $100 hour you can tell them anything and their reputation is bad enough no one will bother to believe them if they say something bad about you.
sentences.zh: 脱衣舞娘会以 100 美元的小时来提供安慰、言语上的安慰和身体上的爱，你可以告诉他们任何事情，而且他们的名声已经够糟糕了，如果他们说你的坏话，没有人会费心去相信他们。

Well, in the jurisdictions that I care about the courts and lawmakers have already decided this and the legal requirement is to report.

If its your license to practice on the line you know what choice you're going to make.

blocks.html_en:
That's not generally true. From [0]

> Across most states, viewing CSEM alone is generally not a mandated-reporting trigger; reporting becomes obligatory when disclosures involve an identifiable child being abused or used to produce material.

> California’s CANRA imposes a distinct duty to report electronic access (download/stream) with identifying patient information, upheld against privacy challenges based on compelling state interest.

[0] https://www.psychiatrictimes.com/view/mandatory-reporting-ch...

sentences.en: That's not generally true.
sentences.zh: 这通常不是真的。

sentences.en: From [0]> Across most states, viewing CSEM alone is generally not a mandated-reporting trigger; reporting becomes obligatory when disclosures involve an identifiable child being abused or used to produce material.> California’s CANRA imposes a distinct duty to report electronic access (download/stream) with identifying patient information, upheld against privacy challenges based on compelling state interest.[0] https://www.psychiatrictimes.com/view/mandatory-reporting-ch...
sentences.zh: 来自 [0]> 在大多数州，单独查看 CSEM 通常并不是强制报告的触发因素；当披露涉及可识别的儿童被虐待或用于制作材料时，报告就成为强制性的。> 加利福尼亚州的 CANRA 规定了报告电子访问（下载/流媒体）和识别患者信息的明确义务，并基于令人信服的国家利益来应对隐私挑战。 [0] https://www.psychiatrictimes.com/view/mandatory-reporting-ch...

blocks.html_en:
Apple is on the side of privacy if it serves their marketing. Which is why they would rather build and normalize CLIENT SIDE CONTENT SCANNING so they can continue to market iCloud as "secure and private".

If Apple's interests sometimes align with ours then great. I'll take it. But don't attribute to this ~5 trillion dollar company some kind of altruism.

sentences.en: Apple is on the side of privacy if it serves their marketing.
sentences.zh: 如果苹果为他们的营销服务，那么它就站在隐私一边。

sentences.en: Which is why they would rather build and normalize CLIENT SIDE CONTENT SCANNING so they can continue to market iCloud as "secure and private".If Apple's interests sometimes align with ours then great.
sentences.zh: 这就是为什么他们宁愿构建客户端内容扫描并使之标准化，这样他们就可以继续将 iCloud 推销为“安全和私密”。如果 Apple 的利益有时与我们的利益一致那就太好了。

sentences.en: I'll take it.
sentences.zh: 我要买它。

sentences.en: But don't attribute to this ~5 trillion dollar company some kind of altruism.
sentences.zh: 但不要将这家市值约 5 万亿美元的公司归结为某种利他主义。

blocks.html_en:
Apple is very much like WhatsApp. Yes you cant perfectly trust their E2EE against state actors, but both in fact put some effort into making world have little bit more privacy.

At least on Desktop we have usable Linux, but on the phones there is literally nothing usable because thanks to Google efforts switching to GrapheneOS mean tons of apps either not working or break every few months.

Yes its possible to make Andoid spy on you a little less, but even for tech savvy person its damn inconvinient and Google making platform worse with every single release.

Thanks to Google "security" I can use my banking apps on 9 years old device with 6 years outdated firmware, but not on GrapheneOS.

sentences.en: Apple is very much like WhatsApp.
sentences.zh: 苹果很像 WhatsApp。

sentences.en: Yes you cant perfectly trust their E2EE against state actors, but both in fact put some effort into making world have little bit more privacy.At least on Desktop we have usable Linux, but on the phones there is literally nothing usable because thanks to Google efforts switching to GrapheneOS mean tons of apps either not working or break every few months.Yes its possible to make Andoid spy on you a little less, but even for tech savvy person its damn inconvinient and Google making platform worse with every single release.Thanks to Google "security" I can use my banking apps on 9 years old device with 6 years outdated firmware, but not on GrapheneOS.
sentences.zh: 是的，你不能完全相信他们的 E2EE 对抗国家行为者，但事实上，两者都付出了一些努力，让世界拥有更多的隐私。至少在桌面上，我们有可用的 Linux，但在手机上，几乎没有任何可用的东西，因为感谢谷歌的努力，切换到 GrapheneOS 意味着大量的应用程序要么无法工作，要么每隔几个月就会崩溃。是的，有可能让 Andoid 监视你的情况少一点，但即使对于精通技术的人来说，这也太不方便了，谷歌的每一个版本都让平台变得更糟。感谢谷歌“安全性” 我可以在 9 年前、固件已过时 6 年前的设备上使用我的银行应用程序，但不能在 GrapheneOS 上使用。

blocks.html_en:
> hanks to Google efforts switching to GrapheneOS mean tons of apps either not working or break every few months.

I've been using GrapheneOS for years and that hasn't been my experience. There are two financial apps that don't work for me, and that's it. Pretty much everything else I use is fine. But, to be fair, I'm very scrupulous about my apps and tend to avoid installing an app for every little thing that wants me to.

sentences.zh: > 感谢 Google 努力转向 GrapheneOS，这意味着每隔几个月就会有大量应用程序无法运行或崩溃。我已经使用 GrapheneOS 多年了，但这并不是我的经验。

sentences.en: There are two financial apps that don't work for me, and that's it.
sentences.zh: 有两个金融应用程序不适合我，仅此而已。

sentences.en: Pretty much everything else I use is fine.
sentences.zh: 我用的其他东西几乎都很好。

sentences.en: But, to be fair, I'm very scrupulous about my apps and tend to avoid installing an app for every little thing that wants me to.
sentences.zh: 但是，公平地说，我对我的应用程序非常谨慎，并且倾向于避免为所有需要我安装的小东西安装应用程序。

blocks.html_en:
> thanks to Google efforts switching to GrapheneOS mean tons of apps either not working or break every few months.

Sometimes, all you need is a Web browser. I personally don't want any "apps" on my phone that aren't basic utilities.

I am aware some banks in Europe require 2FA on a mobile device. Short of switching banks, my answer to that is a cheap or e-waste Googled Android phone that stays at home and serves that sole purpose.

sentences.zh: > 感谢 Google 努力转向 GrapheneOS，这意味着每隔几个月就有大量应用程序无法运行或崩溃。有时，您所需要的只是一个 Web 浏览器。

blocks.html_en:
> thanks to Google efforts switching to GrapheneOS mean tons of apps either not working or break every few months.

Sometimes, all you need is a Web browser. I personally don't want any "apps" on my phone that aren't basic utilities.

I am aware some banks in Europe require 2FA on a mobile device. Short of switching banks, my answer to that is a cheap or e-waste Googled Android phone that stays at home and serves that sole purpose.

blocks.html_en:
It's sadly becoming harder. I've been playing that game for quite long and hope to stick to web apps, but still.

Some banks limit functionality on web apps, which is annoying.

More importantly, many refuse to provide a decent 2FA other than push notifications inside the app or SMS, which is insecure and EU has mandated its phaseout.

The thing that works for me is to pretend to be clueless and get an old hardware OTP generator, but those are susceptible to impersonation attacks on the bank side.

sentences.en: It's sadly becoming harder.
sentences.zh: 可悲的是它变得越来越难。

sentences.en: I've been playing that game for quite long and hope to stick to web apps, but still.Some banks limit functionality on web apps, which is annoying.More importantly, many refuse to provide a decent 2FA other than push notifications inside the app or SMS, which is insecure and EU has mandated its phaseout.The thing that works for me is to pretend to be clueless and get an old hardware OTP generator, but those are susceptible to impersonation attacks on the bank side.
sentences.zh: 我玩这个游戏已经很长时间了，希望坚持使用网络应用程序，但仍然如此。一些银行限制网络应用程序的功能，这很烦人。更重要的是，除了应用程序或短信内的推送通知之外，许多银行拒绝提供像样的 2FA，这是不安全的，欧盟已强制其逐步淘汰。对我有用的方法是假装一无所知并获得旧的硬件 OTP 生成器，但这些很容易受到银行方面的假冒攻击。

blocks.html_en:
> Apple is very much like WhatsApp. Yes you cant perfectly trust their E2EE against state actors, but both in fact put some effort into making world have little bit more privacy.

They probably use E2EE just so they don't have to respond to court orders and such.

sentences.en: > Apple is very much like WhatsApp.
sentences.zh: > Apple 很像 WhatsApp。

sentences.en: Yes you cant perfectly trust their E2EE against state actors, but both in fact put some effort into making world have little bit more privacy.They probably use E2EE just so they don't have to respond to court orders and such.
sentences.zh: 是的，你不能完全信任他们的 E2EE 来对抗国家行为者，但事实上，两者都付出了一些努力，让世界拥有更多的隐私。他们可能只是使用 E2EE，这样他们就不必回应法院命令等。

blocks.html_en:
To me it is crazy people go out of their way to defend apple in this area.

They might be slightly better than some others (horray!) but given their ecosystem it is still the worst platform if you value any form of freedom. Depending on apple for your privacy is ignorance at best.

sentences.zh: 依赖苹果保护你的隐私充其量只是无知。

blocks.html_en:
Apple has degraded privacy online through Safari's cookie-preferential storage partitioning (i.e. site capabilities are penalized for using private localStorage instead of cookies), requiring sites to occasionally leak data over the network for multi-subdomain same-site web applications. These applications can privately share local state offline in Firefox and Chrome but usually serve the lowest common denominator, so many webapps use cookies to support Safari.

They're pro-privacy when it serves them financially.

sentences.en: Apple has degraded privacy online through Safari's cookie-preferential storage partitioning (i.e. site capabilities are penalized for using private localStorage instead of cookies), requiring sites to occasionally leak data over the network for multi-subdomain same-site web applications.
sentences.zh: Apple 通过 Safari 的 cookie 优先存储分区（即站点功能因使用私有 localStorage 而不是 cookie 而受到惩罚）降低了在线隐私性，要求站点偶尔通过网络泄露多子域同站点 Web 应用程序的数据。

sentences.en: These applications can privately share local state offline in Firefox and Chrome but usually serve the lowest common denominator, so many webapps use cookies to support Safari.They're pro-privacy when it serves them financially.
sentences.zh: 这些应用程序可以在 Firefox 和 Chrome 中离线私下共享本地状态，但通常服务于最低公分母，因此许多 Web 应用程序使用 Cookie 来支持 Safari。当为它们提供经济服务时，它们是支持隐私的。

blocks.html_en:
I was an engineer at both MS and Apple. At Apple, privacy was baked into every new feature from the start. At MS, the privacy component was glued on at the very end, if ever.

Like OP said, Apple isn't perfect nor will they ever be, but they do prioritize privacy better than most.

blocks.html_en:
Privacy is a natural fit for Apple in that they make money on discrete devices, but services have grown tremendously. That’s where the erosion of privacy happens.

So once there’s a profit motive for violating your privacy, the justification for eroding your privacy will proceed. It’s really the inertia of Apple starting out as privacy-compatible that makes them hesitant to throw that away.

sentences.en: Privacy is a natural fit for Apple in that they make money on discrete devices, but services have grown tremendously.
sentences.zh: 隐私对于苹果来说是天然的选择，因为他们通过离散设备赚钱，但服务却有了巨大的增长。

sentences.en: That’s where the erosion of privacy happens.So once there’s a profit motive for violating your privacy, the justification for eroding your privacy will proceed.
sentences.zh: 这就是侵犯隐私的地方。因此，一旦侵犯您的隐私是出于盈利动机，那么侵犯您的隐私的理由就会继续存在。

sentences.en: It’s really the inertia of Apple starting out as privacy-compatible that makes them hesitant to throw that away.
sentences.zh: 事实上，苹果一开始就注重隐私兼容，这让他们犹豫是否要放弃这一点。

blocks.html_en:
I said this in another thread a while ago, and one of these people who thinks Apple isn’t on the side of privacy cited a lawsuit they settled around Siri listened to conversations: https://www.scientificamerican.com/article/apple-settles-cla...

People understood this settlement to mean Apple was spying on their conversations and selling them to advertisers, when it seems to have more to do with people accidentally triggering Siri. But people don’t care about this kind of nuance or actually tallying up all the ways Apple is pro privacy against rare issues like this one. It’s all just tribalism at the end of the day.

sentences.en: I said this in another thread a while ago, and one of these people who thinks Apple isn’t on the side of privacy cited a lawsuit they settled around Siri listened to conversations: https://www.scientificamerican.com/article/apple-settles-cla...People understood this settlement to mean Apple was spying on their conversations and selling them to advertisers, when it seems to have more to do with people accidentally triggering Siri.
sentences.zh: 我不久前在另一条帖子中说过这一点，其中一位认为苹果不站在隐私一边的人引用了他们围绕 Siri 达成的诉讼，听了对话：https://www.scientificamerican.com/article/apple-settles-cla...人们理解这一和解意味着苹果正在监视他们的对话并将其出售给广告商，而这似乎与人们意外触发 Siri 有关。

sentences.en: But people don’t care about this kind of nuance or actually tallying up all the ways Apple is pro privacy against rare issues like this one.
sentences.zh: 但人们并不关心这种细微差别，也不关心苹果公司在保护隐私方面采取的所有措施，以应对像这样的罕见问题。

sentences.en: It’s all just tribalism at the end of the day.
sentences.zh: 归根结底，这只是部落主义。

blocks.html_en:
This is also a misunderstanding of the case, though. The suit wasn't filed because of accidental Siri triggers, it was filed because Apple never informed users that third-party contractors would be listening to retained recordings of accidental invocations. From the original Guardian report:

> Although Apple does not explicitly disclose it in its consumer-facing privacy documentation, a small proportion of Siri recordings are passed on to contractors working for the company around the world. https://www.theguardian.com/technology/2019/jul/26/apple-con...

Regardless of how you feel towards Apple, this sort of data should be siloed in a way that makes it impossible to share with undisclosed third-parties. It also should not be shared anywhere until Apple can confirm that PII and other sensitive information was redacted from the data, which they did not. It generally points to a laissez-faire attitude towards personal data that is hard to abdicate without seeing the Siri server-side code or retention architecture, which is why Apple settled to avoid revealing the extent to which they retain and share data in a class-action discovery process. The settlement is a mea-culpa without admitting to wrongdoing or proving fundamental security.

The lawsuit was entirely avoidable if Apple didn't play fast-and-loose with production databases. It'll be a black eye for anyone that points to Apple's whitepapers as an example of their commitment to security - some retention simply doesn't get documented by Apple.

sentences.en: This is also a misunderstanding of the case, though.
sentences.zh: 但这也是对案件的误解。

sentences.en: The suit wasn't filed because of accidental Siri triggers, it was filed because Apple never informed users that third-party contractors would be listening to retained recordings of accidental invocations.
sentences.zh: 该诉讼并不是因为 Siri 意外触发而提起，而是因为苹果从未告知用户第三方承包商将监听意外调用的保留录音。

sentences.en: From the original Guardian report:> Although Apple does not explicitly disclose it in its consumer-facing privacy documentation, a small proportion of Siri recordings are passed on to contractors working for the company around the world.
sentences.zh: 摘自《卫报》原始报道：> 尽管苹果没有在其面向消费者的隐私文档中明确披露，但一小部分 Siri 录音会被传递给为该公司在世界各地工作的承包商。

sentences.en: https://www.theguardian.com/technology/2019/jul/26/apple-con...Regardless of how you feel towards Apple, this sort of data should be siloed in a way that makes it impossible to share with undisclosed third-parties.
sentences.zh: https://www.theguardian.com/technology/2019/jul/26/apple-con...无论您对苹果有何看法，此类数据都应该以某种方式进行隔离，使其无法与未公开的第三方共享。

sentences.en: It also should not be shared anywhere until Apple can confirm that PII and other sensitive information was redacted from the data, which they did not.
sentences.zh: 在 Apple 确认 PII 和其他敏感信息已从数据中删除之前，也不应在任何地方共享这些信息，但他们并没有这样做。

sentences.en: It generally points to a laissez-faire attitude towards personal data that is hard to abdicate without seeing the Siri server-side code or retention architecture, which is why Apple settled to avoid revealing the extent to which they retain and share data in a class-action discovery process.
sentences.zh: 它通常表明对个人数据采取自由放任的态度，如果没有看到 Siri 服务器端代码或保留架构，就很难放弃这种态度，这就是为什么苹果决定避免在集体诉讼发现过程中透露他们保留和共享数据的程度。

sentences.en: The settlement is a mea-culpa without admitting to wrongdoing or proving fundamental security.The lawsuit was entirely avoidable if Apple didn't play fast-and-loose with production databases.
sentences.zh: 该和解协议是一种道歉，既没有承认错误行为，也没有证明基本安全。如果苹果公司不在生产数据库方面采取快速而宽松的做法，这场诉讼是完全可以避免的。

sentences.en: It'll be a black eye for anyone that points to Apple's whitepapers as an example of their commitment to security - some retention simply doesn't get documented by Apple.
sentences.zh: 对于任何将苹果公司的白皮书作为其安全承诺的例子的人来说，这都会是一个打击——苹果公司根本没有记录某些保留情况。

It also should not be shared anywhere until Apple can confirm that PII and other sensitive information was redacted from the data, which they did not. It generally points to a laissez-faire attitude towards personal data that is hard to abdicate without seeing the Siri server-side code or retention architecture, which is why Apple settled to avoid revealing the extent to which they retain and share data in a class-action discovery process. The settlement is a mea-culpa without admitting to wrongdoing or proving fundamental security.

sentences.en: Regardless of how you feel towards Apple, this sort of data should be siloed in a way that makes it impossible to share with undisclosed third-parties.
sentences.zh: 无论你对苹果的看法如何，此类数据都应该以某种方式进行隔离，使其无法与未公开的第三方共享。

sentences.en: It also should not be shared anywhere until Apple can confirm that PII and other sensitive information was redacted from the data, which they did not.
sentences.zh: 在 Apple 确认 PII 和其他敏感信息已从数据中删除之前，也不应在任何地方共享这些信息，但他们并没有这样做。

sentences.en: It generally points to a laissez-faire attitude towards personal data that is hard to abdicate without seeing the Siri server-side code or retention architecture, which is why Apple settled to avoid revealing the extent to which they retain and share data in a class-action discovery process.
sentences.zh: 它通常表明对个人数据采取自由放任的态度，如果没有看到 Siri 服务器端代码或保留架构，就很难放弃这种态度，这就是为什么苹果决定避免在集体诉讼发现过程中透露他们保留和共享数据的程度。

sentences.en: The settlement is a mea-culpa without admitting to wrongdoing or proving fundamental security.
sentences.zh: 该和解协议是一种道歉，既没有承认错误行为，也没有证明基本安全。

blocks.html_en: The lawsuit was entirely avoidable if Apple didn't play fast-and-loose with production databases. It'll be a black eye for anyone that points to Apple's whitepapers as an example of their commitment to security - some retention simply doesn't get documented by Apple.

sentences.en: The lawsuit was entirely avoidable if Apple didn't play fast-and-loose with production databases.
sentences.zh: 如果苹果公司不对生产数据库采取快速而宽松的做法，这场诉讼是完全可以避免的。

sentences.en: It'll be a black eye for anyone that points to Apple's whitepapers as an example of their commitment to security - some retention simply doesn't get documented by Apple.
sentences.zh: 对于任何将苹果公司的白皮书作为其安全承诺的例子的人来说，这都会是一个打击——苹果公司根本没有记录某些保留情况。

blocks.html_en: Apple may be on the side of privacy, but since they are competing everybody out of the market with their slick consumer products they actually form a threat to privacy since now the government has to only implement a backdoor at one vendor.

sentences.en: Apple may be on the side of privacy, but since they are competing everybody out of the market with their slick consumer products they actually form a threat to privacy since now the government has to only implement a backdoor at one vendor.
sentences.zh: 苹果可能站在隐私一边，但由于他们正在用其精致的消费产品将市场上的所有人竞争出去，因此实际上对隐私构成了威胁，因为现在政府只能在一个供应商处实施后门。

blocks.html_en:
> It is crazy people think apple isnt on the side of privacy.

Look up the "iCloud Keychain" API:

For years Apple has let and helped Facebook, TikTok, Tinder etc. track users even after you delete an app, even ACROSS DEVICES and DEVICE RESETS.

There's no way to even SEE what data the apps have stored on your device & iCloud account on iOS, only through the macOS Keychain Access app. Even then you can't be sure that that's all that being stored.

They temporarily changed course and wiped iCloud Keychain data when deleting apps, but only during a single beta of iOS some years ago, and then reverted to the way it is now.

This scores so many points in favor of privacy intruding corporations that it puts Apple far from being the paragon of privacy they pretend to parade as.

sentences.en: > It is crazy people think apple isnt on the side of privacy.Look up the "iCloud Keychain" API:For years Apple has let and helped Facebook, TikTok, Tinder etc. track users even after you delete an app, even ACROSS DEVICES and DEVICE RESETS.There's no way to even SEE what data the apps have stored on your device & iCloud account on iOS, only through the macOS Keychain Access app.
sentences.zh: > 人们认为苹果不站在隐私一边，这太疯狂了。查找“iCloud Keychain”API：多年来，即使您删除应用程序，即使跨设备和设备重置，苹果也允许并帮助 Facebook、TikTok、Tinder 等跟踪用户。甚至无法查看这些应用程序在您的设备和 iOS 上的 iCloud 帐户上存储了哪些数据，只能通过 macOS Keychain Access 应用程序。

sentences.en: Even then you can't be sure that that's all that being stored.They temporarily changed course and wiped iCloud Keychain data when deleting apps, but only during a single beta of iOS some years ago, and then reverted to the way it is now.This scores so many points in favor of privacy intruding corporations that it puts Apple far from being the paragon of privacy they pretend to parade as.
sentences.zh: 即便如此，你也不能确定这就是被存储的全部内容。他们在删除应用程序时临时改变了做法并擦除了 iCloud 钥匙串数据，但只是在几年前的 iOS 测试版期间，然后又恢复到现在的样子。这为侵犯隐私的公司赢得了很多分，以至于苹果远没有成为他们假装标榜的隐私典范。

blocks.html_en:
It's misleading to claim that Apple has "let and helped Facebook, TikTok, Tinder etc. track users [...]" using the keychain API. The persistence you're talking about, which those companies exploit, is a side effect of how the API works, not an intentional surveillance feature. Furthermore, it's not some special API that those big companies made a deal with Apple to get access to – any iOS dev can use it.

It's a real flaw that they should've fixed a long time ago, but your conspiratorial framing makes it seem like Apple colluded with Facebook et al. to end run their own privacy protections, while ignoring the fact that Apple's App Tracking Transparency feature has cost Facebook billions.

sentences.en: It's misleading to claim that Apple has "let and helped Facebook, TikTok, Tinder etc. track users [...]" using the keychain API.
sentences.zh: 声称苹果“让并帮助 Facebook、TikTok、Tinder 等使用钥匙串 API 跟踪用户 [...]”的说法具有误导性。

sentences.en: The persistence you're talking about, which those companies exploit, is a side effect of how the API works, not an intentional surveillance feature.
sentences.zh: 你所说的那些公司利用的持久性是 API 工作方式的副作用，而不是有意的监视功能。

sentences.en: Furthermore, it's not some special API that those big companies made a deal with Apple to get access to – any iOS dev can use it.It's a real flaw that they should've fixed a long time ago, but your conspiratorial framing makes it seem like Apple colluded with Facebook et al. to end run their own privacy protections, while ignoring the fact that Apple's App Tracking Transparency feature has cost Facebook billions.
sentences.zh: 此外，这并不是那些大公司与 Apple 达成协议才能访问的特殊 API，任何 iOS 开发人员都可以使用它。这是一个真正的缺陷，他们早就应该修复了，但你的阴谋框架让它看起来像是 Apple 与 Facebook 等人勾结。结束自己的隐私保护，同时忽视了苹果的应用程序跟踪透明度功能已经让 Facebook 损失了数十亿美元的事实。

blocks.html_en:
It's telling that these come from people trying to implement tracking and high visibility into user behavior, and complaining that Apple won't let them even though Apple conceptually could.

Except ublock, which can't do what it does the way it normally does, for the same reason you can't have any plugin inspecting realtime activity and doing scriptlet injection.

You can have ad blocking. You can't have plugins with that kind of low level access to your browser activity.

You can prefer something that allows dangerous behavior as a trade-off for greater capabilities, but you can't deny it's a safety trade-off where Apple picked what's safer.

sentences.en: It's telling that these come from people trying to implement tracking and high visibility into user behavior, and complaining that Apple won't let them even though Apple conceptually could.Except ublock, which can't do what it does the way it normally does, for the same reason you can't have any plugin inspecting realtime activity and doing scriptlet injection.You can have ad blocking.
sentences.zh: 这说明这些来自于试图实现对用户行为的跟踪和高可见性的人，并抱怨苹果不会让他们这样做，尽管苹果在概念上可以。除了 ublock，它不能像通常那样做事情，出于同样的原因，你不能让任何插件检查实时活动并进行 scriptlet 注入。你可以进行广告拦截。

sentences.en: You can't have plugins with that kind of low level access to your browser activity.You can prefer something that allows dangerous behavior as a trade-off for greater capabilities, but you can't deny it's a safety trade-off where Apple picked what's safer.
sentences.zh: 你不能让插件对你的浏览器活动进行低级别的访问。你可以更喜欢允许危险行为的插件，作为对更大功能的权衡，但你不能否认这是一种安全权衡，苹果选择了更安全的东西。

blocks.html_en:
It's telling that these come from people trying to implement tracking and high visibility into user behavior, and complaining that Apple won't let them even though Apple conceptually could.

Except ublock, which can't do what it does the way it normally does, for the same reason you can't have any plugin inspecting realtime activity and doing scriptlet injection.

You can have ad blocking. You can't have plugins with that kind of low level access to your browser activity.

You can prefer something that allows dangerous behavior as a trade-off for greater capabilities, but you can't deny it's a safety trade-off where Apple picked what's safer.

blocks.html_en:
You mean the company that does hundreds of assessments per year of companies all throughout the supply chain, including surprise spot checks, and forces those it catches violating labor standards to fix the problem and also provide remedies to the affected workers?

Quite a lot of the labor issues in Apple's supply chain we found out because Apple found them and included them in their annual report on these and other supply chain issues.

sentences.zh: 你的意思是，该公司每年对整个供应链上的公司进行数百次评估，包括突击抽查，并迫使那些违反劳工标准的公司解决问题，并为受影响的工人提供补救措施。我们发现了苹果供应链中的很多劳工问题，因为苹果发现了这些问题，并将它们纳入了有关这些问题和其他供应链问题的年度报告中。

blocks.html_en:
> It is crazy people think apple isnt on the side of privacy.

Apple is on the side of making money, and the privacy claims are mostly marketing. The entire stack is closed source, which means it is difficult and expensive to independently verify any of the claims made. What's more, the "auto update" universal backdoor means that Apple can forcibly push a user-hostile "feature" like client-side scanning when it wants or is compelled to by a state actor.

sentences.en: > It is crazy people think apple isnt on the side of privacy.Apple is on the side of making money, and the privacy claims are mostly marketing.
sentences.zh: > 人们认为苹果不站在隐私一边，这太疯狂了。苹果站在赚钱的一边，而隐私声明主要是营销。

sentences.en: The entire stack is closed source, which means it is difficult and expensive to independently verify any of the claims made.
sentences.zh: 整个堆栈是闭源的，这意味着独立验证任何声明都是困难且昂贵的。

sentences.en: What's more, the "auto update" universal backdoor means that Apple can forcibly push a user-hostile "feature" like client-side scanning when it wants or is compelled to by a state actor.
sentences.zh: 更重要的是，“自动更新”通用后门意味着苹果可以在国家行为者想要或被迫时强行推送对用户不利的“功能”，例如客户端扫描。

blocks.html_en:
I am not a lawyer.

There is something ironic about US laws that attempt to prevent crime A by outlawing action B. For example:

  * A: physical sexual abuse of children. B: possession or distribution of CSAM
  * A: drug trafficking or tax evasion. B: structured cash withdrawals

The irony is that the more B is prevented, the less A can be detected and the less B can be used as evidence of A.

It's my understanding that conviction of CSAM-related crimes do not require any physical act to have ever occurred to any real person: one can be convicted of CSAM-related crimes related to paintings/drawings/created_art of fictional people.

It's my understanding that one can be convicted of structured withdrawals that are not driven by, linked to, or in any way related to anything nefarious.

sentences.en: I am not a lawyer.There is something ironic about US laws that attempt to prevent crime A by outlawing action B.
sentences.zh: 我不是律师。美国法律试图通过禁止行为 B 来防止犯罪 A，这有点讽刺。

sentences.en: For example: * A: physical sexual abuse of children.
sentences.zh: 例如： * A：对儿童进行身体性虐待。

sentences.en: B: possession or distribution of CSAM * A: drug trafficking or tax evasion.
sentences.zh: B：拥有或分发 CSAM * A：贩毒或逃税。

sentences.en: B: structured cash withdrawals The irony is that the more B is prevented, the less A can be detected and the less B can be used as evidence of A.It's my understanding that conviction of CSAM-related crimes do not require any physical act to have ever occurred to any real person: one can be convicted of CSAM-related crimes related to paintings/drawings/created_art of fictional people.It's my understanding that one can be convicted of structured withdrawals that are not driven by, linked to, or in any way related to anything nefarious.
sentences.zh: B：结构化现金提取 具有讽刺意味的是，B 被阻止越多，A 被检测到的次数就越少，B 可以用作 A 证据的次数就越少。据我了解，对 CSAM 相关犯罪的定罪不需要任何真实的人曾经发生过任何身体行为：一个人可以因与虚构人物的绘画/绘图/创作的艺术相关的 CSAM 相关犯罪而被定罪。据我所知，一个人可以因与虚构人物的绘画/绘图/创作艺术相关的 CSAM 相关犯罪而被定罪。据我所知，一个人可以因与虚构人物无关的结构化提款而被定罪。或以任何方式与任何邪恶的事物相关。

There is something ironic about US laws that attempt to prevent crime A by outlawing action B. For example:

  * A: physical sexual abuse of children. B: possession or distribution of CSAM

The irony is that the more B is prevented, the less A can be detected and the less B can be used as evidence of A.

It's my understanding that conviction of CSAM-related crimes do not require any physical act to have ever occurred to any real person: one can be convicted of CSAM-related crimes related to paintings/drawings/created_art of fictional people.

It's my understanding that one can be convicted of structured withdrawals that are not driven by, linked to, or in any way related to anything nefarious.

blocks.html_en:
> one can be convicted of CSAM-related crimes related to paintings/drawings/created_art of fictional people.

This isn't necessarily the case in the US, though I believe only for drawings. AI-generated CSAM probably wouldn't fly in a court of law.

Regardless, it's a naive conception of a system of law to think of it as a utilitarian system of restitution in contexts of "this individual harmed this individual". In fact, that would fall under the category of a "tort" rather than a "crime". The law is just as much about enforcing social mores and norms as it is about dealing with individuals harming each other. Hence why locales like Canada outlaw all forms CSAM, even fictional ones. The victim taken is to be society itself. The possession of this material, implicitly entailing enjoyment of it, is so gross a violation of society's norms and mores that it becomes elevated to a legal matter.

sentences.en: > one can be convicted of CSAM-related crimes related to paintings/drawings/created_art of fictional people.This isn't necessarily the case in the US, though I believe only for drawings.
sentences.zh: > 与虚构人物的绘画/图画/创作艺术相关的 CSAM 相关犯罪可能被定罪。在美国，情况不一定如此，尽管我相信仅适用于图画。

sentences.en: AI-generated CSAM probably wouldn't fly in a court of law.Regardless, it's a naive conception of a system of law to think of it as a utilitarian system of restitution in contexts of "this individual harmed this individual".
sentences.zh: 人工智能生成的 CSAM 可能不会在法庭上生效。无论如何，在“这个人伤害了这个人”的背景下将其视为功利主义的赔偿制度，这是一种天真的法律体系概念。

sentences.en: In fact, that would fall under the category of a "tort" rather than a "crime".
sentences.zh: 事实上，这属于“侵权”而不是“犯罪”的范畴。

sentences.en: The law is just as much about enforcing social mores and norms as it is about dealing with individuals harming each other.
sentences.zh: 法律不仅是为了处理个人互相伤害的问题，也是为了执行社会道德和规范。

sentences.en: Hence why locales like Canada outlaw all forms CSAM, even fictional ones.
sentences.zh: 因此，为什么像加拿大这样的地区禁止所有形式的 CSAM，甚至是虚构的形式。

sentences.en: The victim taken is to be society itself.
sentences.zh: 被带走的受害者就是社会本身。

sentences.en: The possession of this material, implicitly entailing enjoyment of it, is so gross a violation of society's norms and mores that it becomes elevated to a legal matter.
sentences.zh: 拥有这种材料，隐含地意味着享受它，是对社会规范和道德的严重违反，以至于它上升到了法律问题。

blocks.html_en:
> one can be convicted of CSAM-related crimes related to paintings/drawings/created_art of fictional people.

This isn't necessarily the case in the US, though I believe only for drawings. AI-generated CSAM probably wouldn't fly in a court of law.

Regardless, it's a naive conception of a system of law to think of it as a utilitarian system of restitution in contexts of "this individual harmed this individual". In fact, that would fall under the category of a "tort" rather than a "crime". The law is just as much about enforcing social mores and norms as it is about dealing with individuals harming each other. Hence why locales like Canada outlaw all forms CSAM, even fictional ones. The victim taken is to be society itself. The possession of this material, implicitly entailing enjoyment of it, is so gross a violation of society's norms and mores that it becomes elevated to a legal matter.

blocks.html_en: For drawings it has to additionally be "obscene" (since obscenity isn't protected by the first amendment). And there is also a specific law that criminalizes even non-obscene realistic computer generated imagery.

sentences.zh: 还有一项具体法律将计算机生成的非淫秽现实图像定为犯罪。

blocks.html_en:
Not quite. There can be more restrictions on the distribution or promotion of obscene material, but mere possession of obscene material is protected by the first Amendment: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Stanley_v._Georgia

The reason why the Supreme Court upheld bans on possessing CSAM is not because it's obscene, but because it incentivizes abuse of children to produce it.

sentences.en: Not quite.
sentences.zh: 不完全是。

sentences.en: There can be more restrictions on the distribution or promotion of obscene material, but mere possession of obscene material is protected by the first Amendment: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Stanley_v._GeorgiaThe reason why the Supreme Court upheld bans on possessing CSAM is not because it's obscene, but because it incentivizes abuse of children to produce it.
sentences.zh: 对淫秽材料的传播或推广可能有更多限制，但仅仅拥有淫秽材料就受到第一修正案的保护：https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Stanley_v._Georgia最高法院维持禁止拥有 CSAM 的原因不是因为它是淫秽的，而是因为它鼓励虐待儿童来生产它。

blocks.html_en:
This is an extremely important point to understand. At face value, it can feel like CSAM should be protected speech, however repulsive. There are no laws against gore videos, though one might argue that death is worse than SA. However, we have substantial empirical evidence that CSAM directly contributes to offending behavior.

We also acknowledge that participating in pornography requires consent, and that the continued distribution of nonconsensual pornography constitutes a continuing crime against the unwilling subject. Because children have zero legal capacity to consent, CSAM is de facto illegal.

It misses the point to think that CSAM is illegal because it is "obscene". It isn't illegal because it's disgusting; it's illegal because it's egregiously harmful to children. It's like thinking the bad thing a murderer did was make a mess.

sentences.en: This is an extremely important point to understand.
sentences.zh: 这是非常重要的一点。

sentences.en: At face value, it can feel like CSAM should be protected speech, however repulsive.
sentences.zh: 从表面上看，CSAM 应该是受保护的言论，无论多么令人厌恶。

sentences.en: There are no laws against gore videos, though one might argue that death is worse than SA.
sentences.zh: 没有针对血腥视频的法律，尽管有人可能会说死亡比 SA 更糟糕。

sentences.en: However, we have substantial empirical evidence that CSAM directly contributes to offending behavior.We also acknowledge that participating in pornography requires consent, and that the continued distribution of nonconsensual pornography constitutes a continuing crime against the unwilling subject.
sentences.zh: 然而，我们有大量的经验证据表明 CSAM 直接导致犯罪行为。我们也承认参与色情内容需要同意，并且持续传播未经同意的色情内容构成针对不自愿主体的持续犯罪。

sentences.en: Because children have zero legal capacity to consent, CSAM is de facto illegal.It misses the point to think that CSAM is illegal because it is "obscene".
sentences.zh: 由于儿童的同意行为能力为零，CSAM事实上是非法的。如果认为CSAM是非法的，因为它是“淫秽的”，那就没有抓住要点。

sentences.en: It isn't illegal because it's disgusting; it's illegal because it's egregiously harmful to children.
sentences.zh: 这并不违法，因为它令人恶心；而是因为它令人厌恶。这是非法的，因为它对儿童极其有害。

sentences.en: It's like thinking the bad thing a murderer did was make a mess.
sentences.zh: 这就像认为杀人犯所做的坏事就是造成混乱。

blocks.html_en:
> It misses the point to think that CSAM is illegal because it is "obscene".

Are you sure you're not the one missing the point here? You're replying to the tail end of branch of discussion about AI-generated imagery. Obscene is the only argument that works against it.

blocks.html_en: Our society is pretty aligned that distribution is another kind of harm. Non-consented distribution of sexual images (eg: revenge porn) is also a crime. Children don’t need to be the ones to press charges in child porn unlike with adults. That’s a good thing.

sentences.zh: 这是一件好事。

> Our society is pretty aligned that distribution is another kind of harm.

As well as possession. I don't actually know if those are different for CSAM, but I would assume so because they are for drugs.

blocks.html_en:
Meh, I assume "possession" (of drugs) is how the law is worded because otherwise law enforcement would have to catch someone in the act of using or distributing, which must be much harder to do.

Mere possession of a substance is surely not what society cares about.

sentences.en: Meh, I assume "possession" (of drugs) is how the law is worded because otherwise law enforcement would have to catch someone in the act of using or distributing, which must be much harder to do.Mere possession of a substance is surely not what society cares about.
sentences.zh: 呃，我认为法律的措辞是“拥有”（毒品），因为否则执法部门就必须抓获使用或分发毒品的人，而这一定很难做到。仅仅拥有某种物质肯定不是社会关心的。

blocks.html_en:
It surely is, but that doesn't change the fact that many cases are not about distribution and the harm is frequently more of a legal fiction, unless by harm you mean something other than suffering inflicted on the victims. I don't necessarily think it's wrong for society to ban certain acts on purely moral grounds (another example: incest between siblings), but let's not pretend it's something else going on.

> Non-consented distribution of sexual images (eg: revenge porn) is also a crime.

There is very compelling empirical evidence that this causes actual harm (suicide ideation in a very big fraction of the victims), even if it is fictional, so here there is no question about the harm.

sentences.en: It surely is, but that doesn't change the fact that many cases are not about distribution and the harm is frequently more of a legal fiction, unless by harm you mean something other than suffering inflicted on the victims.
sentences.zh: 确实如此，但这并不能改变这样一个事实：许多案件与分配无关，而且伤害往往更多地是法律虚构的，除非你所说的伤害不是指受害者遭受的痛苦。

sentences.en: I don't necessarily think it's wrong for society to ban certain acts on purely moral grounds (another example: incest between siblings), but let's not pretend it's something else going on.> Non-consented distribution of sexual images (eg: revenge porn) is also a crime.There is very compelling empirical evidence that this causes actual harm (suicide ideation in a very big fraction of the victims), even if it is fictional, so here there is no question about the harm.
sentences.zh: 我并不认为社会纯粹出于道德原因禁止某些行为（另一个例子：兄弟姐妹之间的乱伦）是错误的，但我们不要假装这是其他事情。>未经同意传播性图像（例如：复仇色情）也是一种犯罪。有非常令人信服的经验证据表明，这会造成实际伤害（很大一部分受害者有自杀意念），即使它是虚构的，所以这里毫无疑问会造成伤害。

blocks.html_en: I don't think these are the same. Outlawing CSAM gives law enforcement the ability to shutdown markets and prevent commercial distribution of CSAM. Sexually abusing children is heinous, but sexually abusing children for financial gain is even worse.

sentences.en: I don't think these are the same.
sentences.zh: 我不认为这些是相同的。

sentences.en: Outlawing CSAM gives law enforcement the ability to shutdown markets and prevent commercial distribution of CSAM.
sentences.zh: 取缔 CSAM 使执法部门能够关闭市场并阻止 CSAM 的商业分销。

sentences.en: Sexually abusing children is heinous, but sexually abusing children for financial gain is even worse.
sentences.zh: 性侵儿童固然令人发指，但为了经济利益而性侵儿童则更恶劣。

blocks.html_en:
There are even people involved in commercial distribution of it that claim to not even be interested in children, just in profit or even allegedly “for a sense of community” (someone actually said this after getting caught, he was in his 20s but I can’t remember his name — he might have been one of the red room guys).

On top of that, while there are different types of child abusers, the worst ones almost invariantly collect CSAM to the point of hoarding. So it really isn’t that bad of a proxy.

The root comment is implying that legalizing or decriminalizing csam would somehow help with prosecution of child abuse? I’m kind of speechless. Csam IS child abuse. The fact that there are consumers encourages producers to, well, produce!

sentences.en: There are even people involved in commercial distribution of it that claim to not even be interested in children, just in profit or even allegedly “for a sense of community” (someone actually said this after getting caught, he was in his 20s but I can’t remember his name — he might have been one of the red room guys).On top of that, while there are different types of child abusers, the worst ones almost invariantly collect CSAM to the point of hoarding.
sentences.zh: 甚至还有参与商业发行的人声称对儿童根本不感兴趣，只是为了盈利，甚至据称是“为了社区意识”（实际上有人在被抓后这么说，他20多岁，但我不记得他的名字了——他可能是红房里的人之一）。最重要的是，虽然虐待儿童的人有不同类型，但最糟糕的人几乎总是会收集 CSAM 到囤积的地步。

sentences.en: So it really isn’t that bad of a proxy.The root comment is implying that legalizing or decriminalizing csam would somehow help with prosecution of child abuse?
sentences.zh: 所以，代理确实没那么糟糕。根本评论是暗示 csam 合法化或非刑事化会以某种方式帮助起诉虐待儿童吗？

sentences.en: I’m kind of speechless.
sentences.zh: 我有点无语了。

sentences.en: Csam IS child abuse.
sentences.zh: Csam 是虐待儿童行为。

blocks.html_en: There's also the argument that CSAM can act as a gateway leading people from just being a pedophile in their head, to going out and doing something to some child.

blocks.html_en:
Yeah, that old canard is ridiculous!

I mean, if there were any truth to it, surely our nation would have seen an uptick, in the past 30–40 years, of new generations picking up guns and just mercilessly mowing down soft targets as if playing GTA.

Thankfully, that is all confined to fantasy in cyberspace!

sentences.en: Yeah, that old canard is ridiculous!I mean, if there were any truth to it, surely our nation would have seen an uptick, in the past 30–40 years, of new generations picking up guns and just mercilessly mowing down soft targets as if playing GTA.Thankfully, that is all confined to fantasy in cyberspace!
sentences.zh: 是的，那个老谣言太荒谬了！我的意思是，如果有任何事实的话，在过去的 30-40 年里，我们国家肯定会看到越来越多的新一代拿起枪，就像玩 GTA 一样无情地砍倒软目标。值得庆幸的是，这一切都仅限于网络空间的幻想！

blocks.html_en:
school shootings happen when lonely boys with mental health issues a) have access to guns and b) feel like society failed them and they need to take revenge.

the first one is the reason why it happens it america more than any other place. other countries also have youth unemployment and social isolation but they show up as bullying in schools or votes for fascist parties instead of mass murder.

the actual problem is people feeling hopeless, like theres no way they could ever be successful in this world so they want to burn it down. thats why some of them turn violent, when you feel like nothing you can do will make life better the only thing left is making it worse for those you think are responsible, like your classmates or gay/queer people or Jews.

none of this comes down to video games, feminism, rap music, violent movies, atheism or other cultural "problems" conservatives always like to blame. its a social issue that needs social solutions.

sentences.en: school shootings happen when lonely boys with mental health issues a) have access to guns and b) feel like society failed them and they need to take revenge.the first one is the reason why it happens it america more than any other place.
sentences.zh: 当患有心理健康问题的孤独男孩 a) 能够获得枪支 b) 觉得社会辜负了他们并且他们需要报复时，学校枪击事件就会发生。第一个原因是美国比其他任何地方都更容易发生校园枪击事件。

sentences.en: other countries also have youth unemployment and social isolation but they show up as bullying in schools or votes for fascist parties instead of mass murder.the actual problem is people feeling hopeless, like theres no way they could ever be successful in this world so they want to burn it down.
sentences.zh: 其他国家也存在青年失业和社会孤立的问题，但它们表现为学校欺凌或为法西斯政党投票，而不是大规模谋杀。真正的问题是人们感到绝望，好像他们不可能在这个世界上取得成功，所以他们想把它烧毁。

sentences.en: thats why some of them turn violent, when you feel like nothing you can do will make life better the only thing left is making it worse for those you think are responsible, like your classmates or gay/queer people or Jews.none of this comes down to video games, feminism, rap music, violent movies, atheism or other cultural "problems" conservatives always like to blame.

blocks.html_en:
Yes, it’s an argument but there’s zero data to support it, in fact the opposite.

If this were true then widespread availability of pornography on the internet would have resulted in a massive increase in rape of adult females. When in fact, assault numbers have been on a steady decline for decades.

sentences.en: Yes, it’s an argument but there’s zero data to support it, in fact the opposite.If this were true then widespread availability of pornography on the internet would have resulted in a massive increase in rape of adult females.
sentences.zh: 是的，这是一个论点，但支持它的数据为零，事实上恰恰相反。如果这是真的，那么互联网上色情内容的广泛传播将导致强奸成年女性的案件大幅增加。

sentences.en: When in fact, assault numbers have been on a steady decline for decades.
sentences.zh: 事实上，数十年来，袭击事件的数量一直在稳步下降。

blocks.html_en:
Why would general pornography lead to rape? Most pornography is not rape pornography. Would people watching a bunch of rape pornography lead to more rapes? I don't know, but that seems more likely than general pornography leading to rapes.

60% of respondents who were found looking for CSAM on the dark web stated that they were fearful that consuming CSAM would lead them to do something to a child in real life: https://doi.org/10.54501/jots.v1i2.29

sentences.en: Why would general pornography lead to rape?
sentences.zh: 为什么一般色情内容会导致强奸？

sentences.en: Most pornography is not rape pornography.
sentences.zh: 大多数色情内容不是强奸色情内容。

sentences.en: Would people watching a bunch of rape pornography lead to more rapes?
sentences.zh: 人们观看一堆强奸色情内容会导致更多的强奸吗？

sentences.en: I don't know, but that seems more likely than general pornography leading to rapes.60% of respondents who were found looking for CSAM on the dark web stated that they were fearful that consuming CSAM would lead them to do something to a child in real life: https://doi.org/10.54501/jots.v1i2.29
sentences.zh: 我不知道，但这似乎比一般色情内容更有可能导致强奸。在暗网上寻找 CSAM 的受访者中有 60% 表示，他们担心消费 CSAM 会导致他们在现实生活中对孩子做一些事情：https://doi.org/10.54501/jots.v1i2.29

blocks.html_en:
> Most pornography is not rape pornography.

I'm not taking a position here, but the total amount of pornography is irrelevant to the argument. If the amount of rape pornography available has increased 500x as the total amount of pornography has increased 5000x, the proportion of pornography that was rape pornography has been reduced by 90%.

> 60% of respondents who were found looking for CSAM on the dark web stated that they were fearful that consuming CSAM would lead them to do something to a child in real life

Someone looking for child pornography is somebody looking for child pornography, so their opinion about what the search might lead to seems a bit worthless. There's no reason to assume that they have any insight into what will happen next, but we know for a fact what has already happened.

That out of the way, though, and assuming that child pornography leads to child abuse, the good pro-pornography case that has been made is that when pornography is suppressed rather than regulated, the 95% of people who are exclusively interested in adult pornography will have to go through the same underground channels that child pornography flows through, thus having the perverse effect of exposing more people to child pornography (or at least obtaining access to it.) If exposure to child pornography causes child abuse, then the suppression of adult pornography would then lead to more child abuse.

sentences.en: > Most pornography is not rape pornography.I'm not taking a position here, but the total amount of pornography is irrelevant to the argument.
sentences.zh: > 大多数色情内容不是强奸色情内容。我在这里不表明立场，但色情内容的总量与该论点无关。

sentences.en: If the amount of rape pornography available has increased 500x as the total amount of pornography has increased 5000x, the proportion of pornography that was rape pornography has been reduced by 90%.> 60% of respondents who were found looking for CSAM on the dark web stated that they were fearful that consuming CSAM would lead them to do something to a child in real lifeSomeone looking for child pornography is somebody looking for child pornography, so their opinion about what the search might lead to seems a bit worthless.
sentences.zh: 如果可用的强奸色情内容的数量增加了 500 倍，而色情内容的总量增加了 5000 倍，那么强奸色情内容的比例就会减少 90%。> 在暗网上寻找 CSAM 的受访者中，有 60% 的受访者表示，他们担心消费 CSAM 会导致他们在现实生活中对儿童做某事。寻找儿童色情内容的人就是寻找儿童色情内容的人，因此他们对搜索可能导致的结果的看法似乎有点不值钱。

sentences.en: There's no reason to assume that they have any insight into what will happen next, but we know for a fact what has already happened.That out of the way, though, and assuming that child pornography leads to child abuse, the good pro-pornography case that has been made is that when pornography is suppressed rather than regulated, the 95% of people who are exclusively interested in adult pornography will have to go through the same underground channels that child pornography flows through, thus having the perverse effect of exposing more people to child pornography (or at least obtaining access to it.)
sentences.zh: 没有理由假设他们对接下来会发生什么有任何洞察力，但我们知道已经发生的事实。不过，假设儿童色情制品会导致虐待儿童，已经提出的支持色情制品的良好理由是，当色情制品受到压制而不是受到监管时，95％只对成人色情制品感兴趣的人将不得不通过与儿童色情制品相同的地下渠道，从而产生使更多的人接触儿童的不良影响色情内容（或至少获得访问权限。）

sentences.en: If exposure to child pornography causes child abuse, then the suppression of adult pornography would then lead to more child abuse.
sentences.zh: 如果接触儿童色情制品导致虐待儿童，那么压制成人色情制品就会导致更多的儿童虐待。

Someone looking for child pornography is somebody looking for child pornography, so their opinion about what the search might lead to seems a bit worthless. There's no reason to assume that they have any insight into what will happen next, but we know for a fact what has already happened.

That out of the way, though, and assuming that child pornography leads to child abuse, the good pro-pornography case that has been made is that when pornography is suppressed rather than regulated, the 95% of people who are exclusively interested in adult pornography will have to go through the same underground channels that child pornography flows through, thus having the perverse effect of exposing more people to child pornography (or at least obtaining access to it.) If exposure to child pornography causes child abuse, then the suppression of adult pornography would then lead to more child abuse.

sentences.en: > Most pornography is not rape pornography.I'm not taking a position here, but the total amount of pornography is irrelevant to the argument.
sentences.zh: > 大多数色情内容不是强奸色情内容。我在这里不表明立场，但色情内容的总量与该论点无关。

sentences.en: If the amount of rape pornography available has increased 500x as the total amount of pornography has increased 5000x, the proportion of pornography that was rape pornography has been reduced by 90%.> 60% of respondents who were found looking for CSAM on the dark web stated that they were fearful that consuming CSAM would lead them to do something to a child in real lifeSomeone looking for child pornography is somebody looking for child pornography, so their opinion about what the search might lead to seems a bit worthless.

sentences.zh: 没有理由假设他们对接下来会发生什么有任何洞察力，但我们知道已经发生的事实。不过，假设儿童色情制品会导致虐待儿童，已经提出的支持色情制品的良好理由是，当色情制品受到压制而不是受到监管时，95％只对成人色情制品感兴趣的人将不得不通过与儿童色情制品相同的地下渠道，从而产生使更多的人接触儿童的不良影响色情内容（或至少获得访问权限。）

Does it need to be said that by making all pornography easier to access the internet obviously makes rape pornography more prevalent and easier to access as well?

Hence your theory should be easily visible in rape statistics, yet it’s the opposite.

blocks.html_en: It would be easily visible or not visible in the rape statistics if the world had a single variable. In any case, even if the world was extremely simple, 'rape porn' could increase rapes, and we could still observe a decrease in rapes if 'general porn' decreased the odds of rape to a larger degree than rape porn increased them relative to the population that consumed each.

blocks.html_en:
Clicking on a page linked in your article, the PROTECT Act of 2003[1] (passed a year later), I see:

> The PROTECT Act includes prohibitions against obscene illustrations depicting child pornography, including computer-generated illustrations, also known as virtual child pornography. Previous provisions outlawing virtual child pornography... had been ruled unconstitutional... The PROTECT ACT attached an obscenity requirement under the Miller test or the variant test noted above to overcome this limitation.

Which, if I'm reading it right, means that GP was correct in saying "conviction of CSAM-related crimes do not require any physical act to have ever occurred to any real person"

[1] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/PROTECT_Act_of_2003

sentences.en: Clicking on a page linked in your article, the PROTECT Act of 2003[1] (passed a year later), I see:> The PROTECT Act includes prohibitions against obscene illustrations depicting child pornography, including computer-generated illustrations, also known as virtual child pornography.
sentences.zh: 点击您文章中链接的页面，2003 年《保护法案》[1]（一年后通过），我看到：>《保护法案》包括禁止描绘儿童色情内容的淫秽插图，包括计算机生成的插图，也称为虚拟儿童色情内容。

sentences.en: Previous provisions outlawing virtual child pornography...
sentences.zh: 先前的规定取缔虚拟儿童色情内容...

sentences.en: had been ruled unconstitutional...
sentences.zh: 被裁定违宪...

sentences.en: The PROTECT ACT attached an obscenity requirement under the Miller test or the variant test noted above to overcome this limitation.Which, if I'm reading it right, means that GP was correct in saying "conviction of CSAM-related crimes do not require any physical act to have ever occurred to any real person"[1] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/PROTECT_Act_of_2003
sentences.zh: 《保护法案》在米勒测试或上述变体测试下附加了淫秽要求，以克服这一限制。如果我没理解错的话，这意味着 GP 的说法是正确的：“对 CSAM 相关犯罪的定罪不需要任何真实的人发生过任何身体行为”[1] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/PROTECT_Act_of_2003

As for the third prong, I’m sure some porn out there has literary merit, but I would guess the vast, vast majority would not.

sentences.zh: 至于第三个方面，我确信一些色情内容具有文学价值，但我猜绝大多数、绝大多数都没有。

Currently it is explicitly against the law[0]:

  (a)In General.—Any person who, in a circumstance described in subsection (d), knowingly produces, distributes, receives, or possesses with intent to distribute, a visual depiction of any kind, including a drawing, cartoon, sculpture, or painting, that—
    (1)
      (A)depicts a minor engaging in sexually explicit conduct; and
      (B)is obscene; or ...
  (b)...
  (c)Nonrequired Element of Offense.—
  It is not a required element of any offense under this section that the minor depicted actually exist.

It is not a required element of any offense under this section that the minor depicted actually exist.

[0]https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/18/1466A

sentences.en: Currently it is explicitly against the law[0]: (a)In General.—Any person who, in a circumstance described in subsection (d), knowingly produces, distributes, receives, or possesses with intent to distribute, a visual depiction of any kind, including a drawing, cartoon, sculpture, or painting, that— (1) (A)depicts a minor engaging in sexually explicit conduct; and (B)is obscene; or ...
sentences.zh: 目前，这种行为明确违法[0]： (a) 一般而言。——任何人在 (d) 小节所述的情况下，故意制作、分发、接收或意图分发任何类型的视觉描绘，包括图画、卡通、雕塑或绘画，且 —— (1) (A) 描绘未成年人从事露骨的性行为； (B) 淫秽；或者 ...

sentences.en: (c)Nonrequired Element of Offense.— It is not a required element of any offense under this section that the minor depicted actually exist.
sentences.zh: (c) 犯罪的非必要要素——所描述的未成年人实际存在并不是本节规定的任何犯罪的必要要素。
