blocks.html_en:
I wonder how you'd actually go about prosecuting that. What's the line between crass and illegal?

People have infantilization fetishes - where they wear diapers and shit. You can certainly imagine someone making a cartoon of that in a sexual way.

Adults often don't look their ages. John Mulaney famously had a set about resembling a child when he was 29.

What if someone generated an image that looked like a teenager, but there was a driver's license in the frame that said the person was an adult?

Real people have ages. Imaginary people are imaginary.

What about art from the antiquity when what we would call a teenager would have been treated as an adult? Surely someone painted people having sex before "the age of consent" was a well-defined term. Is it illegal to own that painting?

sentences.en: I wonder how you'd actually go about prosecuting that.
sentences.zh: 我想知道你实际上会如何起诉这件事。

sentences.en: What's the line between crass and illegal?People have infantilization fetishes - where they wear diapers and shit.
sentences.zh: 粗鲁和非法之间的界限是什么？人们有婴儿化的迷恋——他们穿着尿布和拉屎。

sentences.en: You can certainly imagine someone making a cartoon of that in a sexual way.Adults often don't look their ages.
sentences.zh: 你当然可以想象有人以性的方式制作这样的漫画。成年人通常不会看起来像他们的年龄。

sentences.en: John Mulaney famously had a set about resembling a child when he was 29.What if someone generated an image that looked like a teenager, but there was a driver's license in the frame that said the person was an adult?Real people have ages.
sentences.zh: 约翰·穆拉尼 (John Mulaney) 在 29 岁时制作了一张关于孩子的照片。如果有人生成了一张看起来像青少年的图像，但框架中的驾驶执照显示该人是成年人，该怎么办？真实的人有年龄。

sentences.en: Imaginary people are imaginary.What about art from the antiquity when what we would call a teenager would have been treated as an adult?
sentences.zh: 想象中的人就是想象中的。当我们所说的青少年被当作成年人对待时，古代的艺术又如何呢？

sentences.en: Surely someone painted people having sex before "the age of consent" was a well-defined term.
sentences.zh: 肯定有人在“同意年龄”这个定义明确的术语之前描绘了人们发生性行为的情况。

sentences.en: Is it illegal to own that painting?
sentences.zh: 拥有那幅画违法吗？

blocks.html_en:
It's even worse than that. If you make withdrawals with the intent of evading currency reporting requirements you've committed a crime even if the withdrawals don't constitute structuring.

Also, in regard to the fictional CSAM depictions that stuff is still wending its way through the courts.

sentences.en: It's even worse than that.
sentences.zh: 甚至比那更糟糕。

sentences.en: If you make withdrawals with the intent of evading currency reporting requirements you've committed a crime even if the withdrawals don't constitute structuring.Also, in regard to the fictional CSAM depictions that stuff is still wending its way through the courts.
sentences.zh: 如果您出于逃避货币报告要求的目的而提款，即使提款不构成结构化，您也犯下了犯罪行为。此外，关于虚构的 CSAM 描述，这些内容仍在法庭上审理。

blocks.html_en:
> CSAM (“see-sam”) refers to any visual content—photos, videos, livestreams, or AI-generated images—that shows a child being sexually abused or exploited. Child sexual abuse material (CSAM) is not “child pornography.” It’s evidence of child sexual abuse [1]

I can't wrap my head around how AI-generated imagery is evidence of child sexual abuse (CAS). How are you abusing a real child by generating an image of a fake one?

[1] https://rainn.org/get-the-facts-about-csam-child-sexual-abus...

sentences.en: > CSAM (“see-sam”) refers to any visual content—photos, videos, livestreams, or AI-generated images—that shows a child being sexually abused or exploited.
sentences.zh: > CSAM（“see-sam”）是指任何显示儿童遭受性虐待或性剥削的视觉内容（照片、视频、直播或人工智能生成的图像）​​。

sentences.en: Child sexual abuse material (CSAM) is not “child pornography.”
sentences.zh: 儿童性虐待材料 (CSAM) 不是“儿童色情制品”。

sentences.en: It’s evidence of child sexual abuse [1]I can't wrap my head around how AI-generated imagery is evidence of child sexual abuse (CAS).
sentences.zh: 这是儿童性虐待的证据 [1] 我无法理解人工智能生成的图像如何成为儿童性虐待 (CAS) 的证据。

sentences.en: How are you abusing a real child by generating an image of a fake one?[1] https://rainn.org/get-the-facts-about-csam-child-sexual-abus...
sentences.zh: 你如何通过生成假孩子的图像来虐待真正的孩子？[1] https://rainn.org/get-the-facts-about-csam-child- Sex-abus ...

blocks.html_en:
I don't think there's a particular connection between indirect enforcement mechanisms and inability to detect the crime, though:

- Structured transactions are illegal because we put a minimum on the amount of cash that has to move before government financial surveillance applies. The alternative (at least, one acceptable to the state) would be that the government knows every transaction you make[0] no matter the size. Since we don't want that, it has to be illegal to lie about the size of a transaction. Furthermore, the harder it is to get away with structuring your transactions, the more legible the financial system becomes and the easier it is to catch drug dealers.

- Pedophiles have not stopped possessing or distributing CSAM to reduce their legal liability. Actually, this argument ignores the main reason why pedophiles store and trade CSAM around in the first place: it's specifically to scare victims into silence and revictimize those who tell the cops. In fact, this is why we stopped calling it "child porn" and started calling it "child sexual abuse material" - because it is specifically material designed to sexually abuse children by way of it's mere existence.

If you're a "no touch" pedophile (they do exist!) that's still trading real CSAM around, well... Congratulations, Nobuhiro Watsuki, award-winning author of the hit samurai manga Rurouni Kenshin, you're still doing the dirty work for the full-contact pedo who recorded the damned thing.

As for drawn child porn, involving fictional characters (i.e. not CSAM), it is legal in certain jurisdictions. Notably, America, where the 1st Amendment errs on the side of creative expression[1]; and Japan, the thinking man's Epstein Island, where... I actually don't know why the fuck Japan is so weirdly tolerant of all this sick lolicon trash. Hell, Watsuki didn't even get cancelled when it came out he had 100 DVDs worth of actual CSAM.

There's an additional layer to this, though, in that for all the crimes you brought up, there's been a history of active state complicity in the crime:

- The CIA is a drug trafficking gang that happens to moonlight as a government intelligence agency

- A good chunk of elected officials and heads of state in multiple countries were compromised by notorious child trafficker Jeffrey Epstein

- The government doesn't pay taxes. I mean, obviously, they're the ones levying them.

We like to think of law enforcement as a cat-and-mouse game: criminals do a thing and law enforcement tries to hunt them down within the bounds of 4A/5A. The reality is more complicated. There are cases in which governments actively collaborate with organized crime, either because the government is corrupt as sin, or because the criminals are offering the state a way out.

[0] Fun fact: if you use Bitcoin, you're automatically opting into this.

[1] To be clear, while I agree with the American argument, you still shouldn't actually expose yourself to this kind of porn, because you're training yourself to get horny around kids. I shouldn't have to say this, but just because it's not illegal doesn't mean it's safe to use.

sentences.en: I don't think there's a particular connection between indirect enforcement mechanisms and inability to detect the crime, though:- Structured transactions are illegal because we put a minimum on the amount of cash that has to move before government financial surveillance applies.
sentences.zh: 不过，我不认为间接执行机制与无法侦查犯罪之间存在特殊联系： - 结构性交易是非法的，因为我们对政府金融监管实施之前必须流动的现金数量设定了最低限度。

sentences.en: The alternative (at least, one acceptable to the state) would be that the government knows every transaction you make[0] no matter the size.
sentences.zh: 另一种选择（至少是国家可以接受的一种）是政府知道您进行的每笔交易[0]，无论规模大小。

sentences.en: Since we don't want that, it has to be illegal to lie about the size of a transaction.
sentences.zh: 既然我们不希望这样，那么谎报交易规模就一定是违法的。

sentences.en: Furthermore, the harder it is to get away with structuring your transactions, the more legible the financial system becomes and the easier it is to catch drug dealers.- Pedophiles have not stopped possessing or distributing CSAM to reduce their legal liability.
sentences.zh: 此外，构建交易越困难，金融体系就越清晰，毒贩就越容易被抓获。 - 恋童癖者并没有停止拥有或分发 CSAM 以减少他们的法律责任。

sentences.en: Actually, this argument ignores the main reason why pedophiles store and trade CSAM around in the first place: it's specifically to scare victims into silence and revictimize those who tell the cops.
sentences.zh: 事实上，这种论点首先忽略了恋童癖者储存和交易 CSAM 的主要原因：它专门是为了吓唬受害者让他们保持沉默，并让那些向警察举报的人再次受害。

sentences.en: In fact, this is why we stopped calling it "child porn" and started calling it "child sexual abuse material" - because it is specifically material designed to sexually abuse children by way of it's mere existence.If you're a "no touch" pedophile (they do exist!)
sentences.zh: 事实上，这就是为什么我们不再称其为“儿童色情内容”，而是开始称其为“儿童性虐待材料”——因为它是专门为通过其存在而对儿童进行性虐待的材料。如果您是“无接触”恋童癖者（他们确实存在！）

sentences.en: that's still trading real CSAM around, well...
sentences.zh: 仍然在交易真正的 CSAM，嗯......

sentences.en: Congratulations, Nobuhiro Watsuki, award-winning author of the hit samurai manga Rurouni Kenshin, you're still doing the dirty work for the full-contact pedo who recorded the damned thing.As for drawn child porn, involving fictional characters (i.e. not CSAM), it is legal in certain jurisdictions.
sentences.zh: 恭喜，Nobuhiro Watsuki，热门武士漫画《浪客剑心》的获奖作者，你仍然在为记录这该死的事情的全接触恋童做肮脏的工作。至于绘制儿童色情内容，涉及虚构人物（即不是 CSAM），在某些司法管辖区是合法的。

sentences.en: Notably, America, where the 1st Amendment errs on the side of creative expression[1]; and Japan, the thinking man's Epstein Island, where...
sentences.zh: 值得注意的是，在美国，第一修正案在创造性表达方面犯了错误[1]；而日本，有思想的人的爱泼斯坦岛，那里……

sentences.en: I actually don't know why the fuck Japan is so weirdly tolerant of all this sick lolicon trash.
sentences.zh: 我真的不知道为什么他妈的日本对所有这些恶心的萝莉控垃圾如此奇怪地容忍。

sentences.zh: 见鬼，Watsuki 甚至没有被取消，因为他拥有 100 张 DVD 的实际 CSAM。不过，还有一个额外的层面，对于你提到的所有罪行，都有国家积极参与犯罪的历史： - 中央情报局是一个贩毒团伙，碰巧兼职政府情报机构 - 多个国家的很多民选官员和国家元首都受到臭名昭著的儿童贩运者杰弗里·爱泼斯坦的影响 - 政府不纳税。

sentences.zh: 我的意思是，很明显，他们是征收者。我们喜欢将执法部门视为猫捉老鼠的游戏：犯罪分子做了一件事，执法部门试图在 4A/5A 的范围内追捕他们。

sentences.zh: 不过，这还有一个额外的层面，即对于你提出的所有罪行，都有国家积极参与犯罪的历史：

blocks.html_en: - The CIA is a drug trafficking gang that happens to moonlight as a government intelligence agency

sentences.en: - The CIA is a drug trafficking gang that happens to moonlight as a government intelligence agency
sentences.zh: - 中央情报局是一个贩毒团伙，恰好兼职政府情报机构

blocks.html_en: - A good chunk of elected officials and heads of state in multiple countries were compromised by notorious child trafficker Jeffrey Epstein

sentences.en: - A good chunk of elected officials and heads of state in multiple countries were compromised by notorious child trafficker Jeffrey Epstein
sentences.zh: - 多个国家的大部分民选官员和国家元首都受到臭名昭著的儿童贩运者杰弗里·爱泼斯坦的危害

blocks.html_en: - The government doesn't pay taxes. I mean, obviously, they're the ones levying them.

sentences.en: - The government doesn't pay taxes.
sentences.zh: - 政府不纳税。

sentences.en: I mean, obviously, they're the ones levying them.
sentences.zh: 我的意思是，显然，他们是征收这些费用的人。

blocks.html_en: We like to think of law enforcement as a cat-and-mouse game: criminals do a thing and law enforcement tries to hunt them down within the bounds of 4A/5A. The reality is more complicated. There are cases in which governments actively collaborate with organized crime, either because the government is corrupt as sin, or because the criminals are offering the state a way out.

sentences.en: We like to think of law enforcement as a cat-and-mouse game: criminals do a thing and law enforcement tries to hunt them down within the bounds of 4A/5A.
sentences.zh: 我们喜欢将执法部门视为猫捉老鼠的游戏：犯罪分子做某事，执法部门试图在 4A/5A 的范围内追捕他们。

sentences.en: The reality is more complicated.
sentences.zh: 现实情况要复杂得多。

sentences.en: There are cases in which governments actively collaborate with organized crime, either because the government is corrupt as sin, or because the criminals are offering the state a way out.
sentences.zh: 在某些情况下，政府积极与有组织犯罪合作，要么是因为政府腐败成罪，要么是因为犯罪分子为国家提供了一条出路。

blocks.html_en: [0] Fun fact: if you use Bitcoin, you're automatically opting into this.

sentences.en: [0] Fun fact: if you use Bitcoin, you're automatically opting into this.
sentences.zh: [0] 有趣的事实：如果您使用比特币，您就会自动选择加入。

blocks.html_en: [1] To be clear, while I agree with the American argument, you still shouldn't actually expose yourself to this kind of porn, because you're training yourself to get horny around kids. I shouldn't have to say this, but just because it's not illegal doesn't mean it's safe to use.

sentences.en: [1] To be clear, while I agree with the American argument, you still shouldn't actually expose yourself to this kind of porn, because you're training yourself to get horny around kids.
sentences.zh: [1] 需要明确的是，虽然我同意美国人的观点，但你实际上仍然不应该让自己接触这种色情内容，因为你正在训练自己在孩子面前变得饥渴。

sentences.en: I shouldn't have to say this, but just because it's not illegal doesn't mean it's safe to use.
sentences.zh: 我不应该这么说，但仅仅因为它不违法并不意味着它可以安全使用。

blocks.html_en:
> As for drawn child porn, involving fictional characters (i.e. not CSAM), it is legal in certain jurisdictions. Notably, America, where the 1st Amendment errs on the side of creative expression

As I noted in another comment:

Currently this is explicitly against the law[0]:

  (a)In General.—Any person who, in a circumstance described in subsection (d), knowingly produces, distributes, receives, or possesses with intent to distribute, a visual depiction of any kind, including a drawing, cartoon, sculpture, or painting, that—
    (1)
      (A)depicts a minor engaging in sexually explicit conduct; and
      (B)is obscene; or ...
  (b)...
  (c)Nonrequired Element of Offense.—
  It is not a required element of any offense under this section that the minor depicted actually exist.

It is not a required element of any offense under this section that the minor depicted actually exist.

[0]https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/18/1466A

blocks.html_en:
IMO "end-to-end encryption" simply isn't possible when the application is run by the same company as the servers the data sits on, is closed source, and can at any time, see the decrypted contents of data it downloads from their servers and do whatever they want with it.

Same issue with Proton, MEGA, and any other e2ee app... it's only useful when the company decides not to mess with the data it could always decrypt locally. Also why people are hesitant to use javascript-based e2ee solutions where the site owner can modify the code at will to do what they want.

sentences.en: IMO "end-to-end encryption" simply isn't possible when the application is run by the same company as the servers the data sits on, is closed source, and can at any time, see the decrypted contents of data it downloads from their servers and do whatever they want with it.Same issue with Proton, MEGA, and any other e2ee app...
sentences.zh: 在我看来，当应用程序与数据所在的服务器由同一家公司运行、是闭源的、并且可以随时查看从服务器下载的数据的解密内容并对其执行任何操作时，“端到端加密”根本不可能实现。Proton、MEGA 和任何其他 e2ee 应用程序也存在同样的问题...

sentences.en: it's only useful when the company decides not to mess with the data it could always decrypt locally.
sentences.zh: 仅当公司决定不弄乱其始终可以在本地解密的数据时，它才有用。

sentences.en: Also why people are hesitant to use javascript-based e2ee solutions where the site owner can modify the code at will to do what they want.
sentences.zh: 这也是为什么人们对使用基于 javascript 的 e2ee 解决方案犹豫不决的原因，因为网站所有者可以随意修改代码来执行他们想要的操作。

blocks.html_en:
Beyond the privacy marketing angle, e2e allows companies with global exposure to sidestep any unpleasantness when they get a subpoena from Bumfuck, Nowhere.

Sure, the NSA, GCHQ and Mossad have a way to exfiltrate the unencrypted data but proprietary e2e is a good thing for most people IMO. Shifts the risk from "my messages are theoretically available to most law enforcement in the globe" to "YOU’RE STILL GONNA BE MOSSAD’ED UPON"[0]. This is specially good for me because I know the equivalent to the FBI where is live is too cheap to buy a Cellebrite [1] license.

[0] https://www.usenix.org/system/files/1401_08-12_mickens.pdf [1] https://arstechnica.com/gadgets/2025/10/leaker-reveals-which...

EDIT: I suppose someone could ask about Meta. The reason behind their support for scanning (and removing e2e in facebook msg) is simply regulatory capture. The zucc wishes to have a letter of marque to "protect" your children and remove the "unsafe" competitors.

EDIT2: Used the wrong term, I mixed up exfiltration channel with sidechannel attack.

sentences.en: Beyond the privacy marketing angle, e2e allows companies with global exposure to sidestep any unpleasantness when they get a subpoena from Bumfuck, Nowhere.Sure, the NSA, GCHQ and Mossad have a way to exfiltrate the unencrypted data but proprietary e2e is a good thing for most people IMO.
sentences.zh: 除了隐私营销角度之外，e2e 还允许拥有全球知名度的公司在收到 Bumfuck、Nowhere.Sure、NSA、GCHQ 和 Mossad 的传票时避免任何不愉快的事情。当然，NSA、GCHQ 和 Mossad 有办法泄露未加密的数据，但在我看来，专有的 e2e 对大多数人来说是一件好事。

sentences.en: Shifts the risk from "my messages are theoretically available to most law enforcement in the globe" to "YOU’RE STILL GONNA BE MOSSAD’ED UPON"[0].
sentences.zh: 将风险从“理论上全球大多数执法部门都可以获取我的信息”转移到“你仍然会被摩萨德攻击”[0]。

sentences.en: This is specially good for me because I know the equivalent to the FBI where is live is too cheap to buy a Cellebrite [1] license.[0] https://www.usenix.org/system/files/1401_08-12_mickens.pdf [1] https://arstechnica.com/gadgets/2025/10/leaker-reveals-which...EDIT: I suppose someone could ask about Meta.
sentences.zh: 这对我来说特别好，因为我知道相当于 FBI 的现场版本太便宜，无法购买 Cellebrite [1] 许可证。[0] https://www.usenix.org/system/files/1401_08-12_mickens.pdf [1] https://arstechnica.com/gadgets/2025/10/leaker-reveals-which...编辑：我想有人可能会问有关Meta的问题。

sentences.en: The reason behind their support for scanning (and removing e2e in facebook msg) is simply regulatory capture.
sentences.zh: 他们支持扫描（并删除 Facebook 消息中的 e2e）背后的原因仅仅是监管捕获。

sentences.en: The zucc wishes to have a letter of marque to "protect" your children and remove the "unsafe" competitors.EDIT2: Used the wrong term, I mixed up exfiltration channel with sidechannel attack.
sentences.zh: zucc 希望拥有一个品牌证书来“保护”您的孩子并消除“不安全”的竞争对手。编辑2：使用了错误的术语，我将渗透通道与侧通道攻击混淆了。

blocks.html_en: Beyond the privacy marketing angle, e2e allows companies with global exposure to sidestep any unpleasantness when they get a subpoena from Bumfuck, Nowhere.

sentences.en: Beyond the privacy marketing angle, e2e allows companies with global exposure to sidestep any unpleasantness when they get a subpoena from Bumfuck, Nowhere.
sentences.zh: 除了隐私营销角度之外，e2e 还允许具有全球影响力的公司在收到来自 Bumfuck, Nowhere 的传票时避免任何不愉快的事情。

blocks.html_en: Sure, the NSA, GCHQ and Mossad have a way to exfiltrate the unencrypted data but proprietary e2e is a good thing for most people IMO. Shifts the risk from "my messages are theoretically available to most law enforcement in the globe" to "YOU’RE STILL GONNA BE MOSSAD’ED UPON"[0]. This is specially good for me because I know the equivalent to the FBI where is live is too cheap to buy a Cellebrite [1] license.

sentences.en: Sure, the NSA, GCHQ and Mossad have a way to exfiltrate the unencrypted data but proprietary e2e is a good thing for most people IMO.

blocks.html_en:
Side channel is academic at best.

Watching memory changing on a complex code base without having said code base is near impossible.

1. Run code 2. Watch memory changes 3. Correlate those to real data

If your code is doing anything complicated that's an intense thing to determine. If you're deep enough for a side channel there's likely a lot easier way of getting in.

sentences.en: Side channel is academic at best.Watching memory changing on a complex code base without having said code base is near impossible.1.
sentences.zh: 侧通道充其量只是学术性的。在没有所述代码库的情况下观察复杂代码库上的内存变化几乎是不可能的。1。

sentences.en: Run code 2.
sentences.zh: 运行代码2。

sentences.en: Watch memory changes 3.
sentences.zh: 观察内存变化 3.

sentences.en: Correlate those to real dataIf your code is doing anything complicated that's an intense thing to determine.
sentences.zh: 将它们与真实数据相关联如果您的代码正在执行任何复杂的操作，那么确定它是一件很困难的事情。

sentences.en: If you're deep enough for a side channel there's likely a lot easier way of getting in.
sentences.zh: 如果你的深度足够深，可以使用侧通道，那么进入可能会更容易。

blocks.html_en:
Brainfart on my part. I was referring to what @majorchord was worrying about, the unencrypted messages in the client get exfiltrated and get sent to the spooks using steganography on some benign request, edited my comment.

My mental model is that most competent intelligence agencies have a PRISM 3.0 deal with FAANG, including on E2E products or at least have devs on the payroll. I imagine that any backdoor is only used on important targets, so no intel sharing with Cletus the deputy.

sentences.en: Brainfart on my part.
sentences.zh: 我的脑放屁。

sentences.en: I was referring to what @majorchord was worrying about, the unencrypted messages in the client get exfiltrated and get sent to the spooks using steganography on some benign request, edited my comment.My mental model is that most competent intelligence agencies have a PRISM 3.0 deal with FAANG, including on E2E products or at least have devs on the payroll.
sentences.zh: 我指的是 @majorchord 担心的事情，客户端中未加密的消息被泄露，并根据一些善意的请求使用隐写术发送给间谍，编辑了我的评论。我的心智模型是，大多数有能力的情报机构都与 FAANG 进行 PRISM 3.0 交易，包括 E2E 产品或至少在工资单上有开发人员。

sentences.en: I imagine that any backdoor is only used on important targets, so no intel sharing with Cletus the deputy.
sentences.zh: 我想任何后门都只用于重要目标，因此不会与副手克莱特斯共享情报。

blocks.html_en:
Yeah Cletus and Chud aren't getting many secrets but I get the feeling Apple's incentives here are against this.

What financial gain do they get from this?

A: risk billions in stock value and customer purchases for basically a "thanks" from the gov? One whistleblower would also have the real ability of becoming world famous for "exposing" apple.

B: Get publicity actually resisting the gov and not lying, what is the gov gonna do? I imagine it has and does happen but I also imagine there's a crying tim apple being dragged through it painfully.

Unlike google I just don't see the financial positives for them to do it beyond massive arm twisting. For many companies the risk of destroying their entire value to customers is just not worth it.

The only money in it is mass scale data collection for training data and ads, if they aren't doing that any other method is the opposite of valuable it's a massive liability.

sentences.en: Yeah Cletus and Chud aren't getting many secrets but I get the feeling Apple's incentives here are against this.What financial gain do they get from this?A: risk billions in stock value and customer purchases for basically a "thanks" from the gov?
sentences.zh: 是的，Cletus 和 Chud 并没有得到太多秘密，但我感觉苹果公司的激励措施是反对这一点的。他们能从中获得什么经济收益？ 答：冒着数十亿股票价值和客户购买的风险，基本上是为了得到政府的“感谢”？

sentences.en: One whistleblower would also have the real ability of becoming world famous for "exposing" apple.B: Get publicity actually resisting the gov and not lying, what is the gov gonna do?
sentences.zh: 一名举报人也有真正的能力因“揭露”苹果而闻名于世。 B：让公众真正抵制政府而不是撒谎，政府会做什么？

sentences.en: I imagine it has and does happen but I also imagine there's a crying tim apple being dragged through it painfully.Unlike google I just don't see the financial positives for them to do it beyond massive arm twisting.
sentences.zh: 我想象它已经发生并且确实发生了，但我也想象有一个哭泣的蒂姆苹果被痛苦地拖过它。与谷歌不同，我只是看不到他们这样做除了大规模的手臂扭曲之外的财务积极因素。

sentences.en: For many companies the risk of destroying their entire value to customers is just not worth it.The only money in it is mass scale data collection for training data and ads, if they aren't doing that any other method is the opposite of valuable it's a massive liability.
sentences.zh: 对于许多公司来说，冒着破坏其对客户的全部价值的风险是不值得的。其中唯一的钱是用于培训数据和广告的大规模数据收集，如果他们不这样做，任何其他方法都与价值相反，这是一个巨大的责任。

blocks.html_en: This is based on a faulty understanding of the underlying systems. The risk with this sort of E2E encryption is not that the service provider pinky promises not to decrypt what they have, it's that they promise they will not insert a new key into your circle of trust to subsequently start decrypting things.

sentences.en: This is based on a faulty understanding of the underlying systems.
sentences.zh: 这是基于对底层系统的错误理解。

sentences.en: The risk with this sort of E2E encryption is not that the service provider pinky promises not to decrypt what they have, it's that they promise they will not insert a new key into your circle of trust to subsequently start decrypting things.
sentences.zh: 这种端到端加密的风险并不在于服务提供商小指承诺不解密他们拥有的内容，而是他们承诺不会将新密钥插入您的信任圈以随后开始解密。

sentences.zh: 这种端到端加密的风险并不在于服务提供商小指承诺不解密他们拥有的内容，而是他们承诺不会将新密钥插入您的信任圈以随后开始解密。

blocks.html_en:
You're suggesting they purposely put a backdoor into all their custom methods? Why?

From a liability standpoint that implies a security breach could result in massive loss of customer data and if it did occur would destroy their privacy image to their customers.

I agree with the point that what you actually trust is the company to not insert maliscous code or keys into your protected path but modern systems actually contain ways to truly lock out the company itself from seeing your data.

Security wise it's amazing. If a company's admin cannot take your data it's excedingly hard for a hacker to do so.

sentences.en: You're suggesting they purposely put a backdoor into all their custom methods?
sentences.zh: 您是说他们故意在所有自定义方法中放置后门？

sentences.en: Why?From a liability standpoint that implies a security breach could result in massive loss of customer data and if it did occur would destroy their privacy image to their customers.I agree with the point that what you actually trust is the company to not insert maliscous code or keys into your protected path but modern systems actually contain ways to truly lock out the company itself from seeing your data.Security wise it's amazing.
sentences.zh: 为什么？从责任的角度来看，这意味着安全漏洞可能会导致客户数据大量丢失，如果确实发生，将会破坏他们对客户的隐私形象。我同意这样的观点，即您真正信任的是公司不会将恶意代码或密钥插入到您的受保护路径中，但现代系统实际上包含真正阻止公司本身查看您的数据的方法。安全方面，这是令人惊奇的。

sentences.en: If a company's admin cannot take your data it's excedingly hard for a hacker to do so.
sentences.zh: 如果公司管理员无法获取您的数据，那么黑客就很难获取您的数据。

blocks.html_en:
> You're suggesting they purposely put a backdoor into all their custom methods? Why?

I'm not sure what you mean by "custom methods", but I'm not saying they have bypassed the e2e encryption - I'm just saying that they technically could.

And as for why they would do that, they might get compelled by a government to do it secretly. As far as I know that hasn't happened yet but I see no reason it couldn't and it would take a whistleblower to find out.

> Security wise it's amazing. If a company's admin cannot take your data it's excedingly hard for a hacker to do so.

I agree, it is the best option available. But Apple/Meta are technically lying when they say it's impossible for them to read your messages.

blocks.html_en:
I disagree because these systems are amoungst the most abused in the world. The only way a backdoor realistically exists is if they have code that is prebuilt backdoor that they serve to inviduals upon request.

As a company you'd be asking for an internal implosion of your company if everything had an additional backdoor in it.

Any backdoor added is a backdoor the thousands to tens of thousands of advanced hackers are always actively trying to breach. So if they do it they'd be doing it very selectively via special served code.

It's also the only way they'd stop whistle blowers.

Now that I say it. That's 100% what they would do. But again it's a crazy high risk almost zero reward action for them. Is the CIA paying apple a bajillion dollars for phones? No so why unless their arm is twisted would they risk billions of dollars for basically no gain?

sentences.en: I disagree because these systems are amoungst the most abused in the world.
sentences.zh: 我不同意，因为这些系统是世界上滥用最严重的系统之一。

sentences.en: The only way a backdoor realistically exists is if they have code that is prebuilt backdoor that they serve to inviduals upon request.As a company you'd be asking for an internal implosion of your company if everything had an additional backdoor in it.Any backdoor added is a backdoor the thousands to tens of thousands of advanced hackers are always actively trying to breach.
sentences.zh: 后门真正存在的唯一方式是，如果他们有预先构建的后门代码，并根据要求向个人提供服务。作为一家公司，如果所有东西都有一个额外的后门，那么你就会要求公司内部崩溃。任何添加的后门都是成千上万的高级黑客总是积极尝试突破的后门。

sentences.en: So if they do it they'd be doing it very selectively via special served code.It's also the only way they'd stop whistle blowers.Now that I say it.
sentences.zh: 因此，如果他们这样做，他们会通过特殊服务代码非常有选择性地这样做。这也是他们阻止举报者的唯一方法。现在我这么说了。

sentences.en: That's 100% what they would do.
sentences.zh: 他们100%会这么做。

sentences.en: But again it's a crazy high risk almost zero reward action for them.
sentences.zh: 但这对他们来说又是一次疯狂的高风险几乎零回报的行动。

sentences.en: Is the CIA paying apple a bajillion dollars for phones?
sentences.zh: 中央情报局是否向苹果公司支付了数十亿美元的手机费用？

sentences.en: No so why unless their arm is twisted would they risk billions of dollars for basically no gain?
sentences.zh: 不，那么为什么除非他们的手臂被扭曲，否则他们会冒数十亿美元的风险而基本上没有收益呢？

blocks.html_en:
They are not lying when they say that they cannot read your messages at rest or in transit without actively changing the code so that you start trusting a new key. The keys sync between devices in ways that Apple cannot read.

This is not bulletproof, because they can potentially change the code to do this - this is what the FBI vs Apple thing was a few years ago was all about.

sentences.en: They are not lying when they say that they cannot read your messages at rest or in transit without actively changing the code so that you start trusting a new key.
sentences.zh: 当他们说如果不主动更改代码以便您开始信任新密钥时，他们无法在休息或传输中读取您的消息时，他们并不是在撒谎。

sentences.en: The keys sync between devices in ways that Apple cannot read.This is not bulletproof, because they can potentially change the code to do this - this is what the FBI vs Apple thing was a few years ago was all about.
sentences.zh: 设备之间的密钥同步以苹果无法读取的方式进行。这并不是万无一失的，因为他们可能会更改代码来做到这一点——这就是几年前联邦调查局与苹果之争的全部内容。

sentences.en: The keys sync between devices in ways that Apple cannot read.This is not bulletproof, because they can potentially change the code to do this - this is what the FBI vs Apple thing was a few years ago was all about.
sentences.zh: 设备之间的密钥同步以苹果无法读取的方式进行。这并不是万无一失的，因为他们可能会更改代码来做到这一点——这就是几年前联邦调查局与苹果之争的全部内容。

blocks.html_en:
> ...without actively changing the code so that you start trusting a new key.

Yes but the problem is there's nothing stopping them from doing that. If Apple/Meta rely on the argument that they technically cannot read people's messages then they will eventually lose because it's simply not true.

blocks.html_en:
In the US at least, companies can and do receive secret demands from the government to add whatever kind of backdoor they want, and you're not allowed to disclose it in any way, they even order you to preserve any warrant canaries you have implemented.

This is for example why Lavabit chose to go out of business instead of giving up their keys.

sentences.en: In the US at least, companies can and do receive secret demands from the government to add whatever kind of backdoor they want, and you're not allowed to disclose it in any way, they even order you to preserve any warrant canaries you have implemented.This is for example why Lavabit chose to go out of business instead of giving up their keys.
sentences.zh: 至少在美国，公司可以而且确实收到政府的秘密要求，添加他们想要的任何类型的后门，而且你不被允许以任何方式披露它，他们甚至命令你保留你已经实施的任何权证金丝雀。这就是为什么Lavabit选择停业而不是放弃他们的密钥。

blocks.html_en:
The company can provide secure enclave and allow the architecture to be audited by third parties.

Which apple does.

It's largely academic though, as almost nobody opts-in to escalated e2e posture in apple services unless they're a high risk person (journalist, dissident, etc).

The headaches that come from e2e everything are too great for most people.

Yes that's exactly his point. E2E is often sold as preventing the owners of the server from being able to read the messages at all, even if they are evil and misleading you.

That's obviously only the case if they aren't also the sole providers of the "ends".

blocks.html_en:
There are actual methods to do this though just not sure anyone does it yet.

1. 3rd party audit of a current repo hash 2. Public hosting of hash 3. Modern attested compute can check the current startup and running code hash and return to the user for their own checks. 4. User encrypts the last known hash they used or trust a 3rd party to perform the check like azure's methods.

Another way is to open source it and repeat 2/3/4

The way around that requires either a backdoor in attested hardware which would be wild if discovered because it's the same tech protecting companies and governments most sensitive info so they're all incentivised to audit that.

sentences.en: There are actual methods to do this though just not sure anyone does it yet.1.
sentences.zh: 有一些实际的方法可以做到这一点，但不确定是否有人这样做。1。

sentences.en: 3rd party audit of a current repo hash 2.
sentences.zh: 当前存储库哈希值的第 3 方审计 2.

sentences.en: Public hosting of hash 3.
sentences.zh: 哈希 3 的公共托管。

sentences.en: Modern attested compute can check the current startup and running code hash and return to the user for their own checks.
sentences.zh: 现代经过验证的计算可以检查当前启动和运行的代码哈希，并返回给用户进行自己的检查。

sentences.en: User encrypts the last known hash they used or trust a 3rd party to perform the check like azure's methods.Another way is to open source it and repeat 2/3/4The way around that requires either a backdoor in attested hardware which would be wild if discovered because it's the same tech protecting companies and governments most sensitive info so they're all incentivised to audit that.
sentences.zh: 用户加密他们使用的最后一个已知的哈希值，或者信任第三方来执行像 azure 的方法一样的检查。另一种方法是将其开源并重复 2/3/4。这种方法需要经过认证的硬件中的后门，如果被发现，这将是疯狂的，因为它是保护公司和政府最敏感信息的相同技术，因此他们都被激励去审计。

blocks.html_en:
> IMO "end-to-end encryption" simply isn't possible

In a technical sense it's absolutely possible. Owning the servers != transferring keys to the servers. Most E2E apps run both client and servers, it's about if they ever had key access.

sentences.en: > IMO "end-to-end encryption" simply isn't possibleIn a technical sense it's absolutely possible.
sentences.zh: > IMO“端到端加密”根本不可能从技术意义上讲这是绝对可能的。

sentences.en: Owning the servers != transferring keys to the servers.
sentences.zh: 拥有服务器！=将密钥传输到服务器。

sentences.en: Most E2E apps run both client and servers, it's about if they ever had key access.
sentences.zh: 大多数端到端应用程序都运行客户端和服务器，这取决于它们是否具有密钥访问权限。

blocks.html_en:
Right... it's one thing to for example, use a third-party MEGA client to upload files so that there's no chance of the company ever being able to see your key (unlike random javascript they might inject into the web version).

But when Apple etc. control both ends, their app could always see your data locally because it is the one that encrypts it to upload to the server in the first place. And these companies can receive secret orders from the US government to add backdoors into the local app and there's nothing you can do or say about it, except go out of business (like Lavabit).

sentences.en: Right...
sentences.zh: 正确的...

sentences.en: it's one thing to for example, use a third-party MEGA client to upload files so that there's no chance of the company ever being able to see your key (unlike random javascript they might inject into the web version).But when Apple etc. control both ends, their app could always see your data locally because it is the one that encrypts it to upload to the server in the first place.
sentences.zh: 例如，使用第三方 MEGA 客户端上传文件是一回事，这样公司就不可能看到您的密钥（与他们可能会注入到网络版本中的随机 JavaScript 不同）。但是，当 Apple 等控制两端时，他们的应用程序总是可以在本地看到您的数据，因为它是首先对数据进行加密以上传到服务器的应用程序。

sentences.en: And these companies can receive secret orders from the US government to add backdoors into the local app and there's nothing you can do or say about it, except go out of business (like Lavabit).
sentences.zh: 这些公司可以收到美国政府的秘密命令，在本地应用程序中添加后门，除了倒闭（例如 Lavabit）之外，您对此无能为力或无能为力。

blocks.html_en:
Right... it's one thing to for example, use a third-party MEGA client to upload files so that there's no chance of the company ever being able to see your key (unlike random javascript they might inject into the web version).

But when Apple etc. control both ends, their app could always see your data locally because it is the one that encrypts it to upload to the server in the first place. And these companies can receive secret orders from the US government to add backdoors into the local app and there's nothing you can do or say about it, except go out of business (like Lavabit).

sentences.en: Right...
sentences.zh: 正确的...

sentences.en: it's one thing to for example, use a third-party MEGA client to upload files so that there's no chance of the company ever being able to see your key (unlike random javascript they might inject into the web version).But when Apple etc. control both ends, their app could always see your data locally because it is the one that encrypts it to upload to the server in the first place.
sentences.zh: 例如，使用第三方 MEGA 客户端上传文件是一回事，这样公司就不可能看到您的密钥（与他们可能会注入到网络版本中的随机 JavaScript 不同）。但是，当 Apple 等控制两端时，他们的应用程序总是可以在本地看到您的数据，因为它是首先对数据进行加密以上传到服务器的应用程序。

sentences.en: And these companies can receive secret orders from the US government to add backdoors into the local app and there's nothing you can do or say about it, except go out of business (like Lavabit).
sentences.zh: 这些公司可以收到美国政府的秘密命令，在本地应用程序中添加后门，除了倒闭（例如 Lavabit）之外，您对此无能为力或无能为力。

blocks.html_en:
The judge called the outcome disturbing, as it leaves victimized children as "collateral damage" of privacy protections.

As sad as this is, end to end encryption means no CSAM scanning.

As an alternative Apple previously tried to do scanning on the phones locally but caught hell for that too.

This is one of those unfortunate tradeoffs but I see no alternative to privacy taking priority.

sentences.en: The judge called the outcome disturbing, as it leaves victimized children as "collateral damage" of privacy protections.As sad as this is, end to end encryption means no CSAM scanning.As an alternative Apple previously tried to do scanning on the phones locally but caught hell for that too.This is one of those unfortunate tradeoffs but I see no alternative to privacy taking priority.
sentences.zh: 法官称这一结果令人不安，因为它让受害儿童成为隐私保护的“附带损害”。尽管如此，端到端加密意味着没有 CSAM 扫描。作为替代方案，苹果公司之前曾尝试在手机上进行本地扫描，但也因此陷入困境。这是不幸的权衡之一，但我认为除了隐私优先之外别无选择。

blocks.html_en:
People can also distribute heinous things through snail mail, but we are not yet at the point where the government reads all letters looking for wrongthink.

Just because we technically can make a privacy destroying drag net does not mean we should. Had phones existed 250 years ago, I have no doubt the founders would have thought it obvious that a cellphone’s contents were your personal papers which could not be freely searched.

sentences.zh: 人们还可以通过蜗牛邮件传播令人发指的东西，但我们还没有到政府阅读所有信件寻找错误想法的地步。仅仅因为我们在技术上可以建立一个破坏隐私的拖网并不意味着我们应该这样做。

blocks.html_en:
But that's mostly because it's impractical. They do use dogs to sniff for drugs and explosives, so if CSAM smelled or was visible through X-ray then it would probably be a different story. And let's not forget snail mail is by far a more uncommon way to spread that material than the Internet is. The Internet came into broad use just ~15 years after commercial CSAM was openly being sold by mail order in Europe.

Personally, I am on the side of privacy, just to be clear.

blocks.html_en:
More effort should be done to find real-world equivalents of such actions and "think of the children".

An icloud is like a storage locker or a safety deposit box... the owner should go through all your stuff there, just in case you have some CSAM!

Metadata is just tracking info about who, where and with whom... every bartender should take your IDs and log when you came to the bar, who you sat with and how long you talked there.

EU Chat control is like general eavesdropping... every time you sit down and talk with someone, an EU bureaucrat should sit next to you and listen and write down your conversations, just in case.

etc.

Somehow people think that "it's ok if it's on the internet", even when it's stuff they'd never accept in real life.

sentences.en: More effort should be done to find real-world equivalents of such actions and "think of the children".An icloud is like a storage locker or a safety deposit box...
sentences.zh: 应该付出更多的努力来找到现实世界中此类行为的等价物，并“为孩子们着想”。icloud 就像一个储物柜或一个保险箱......

sentences.en: the owner should go through all your stuff there, just in case you have some CSAM!Metadata is just tracking info about who, where and with whom...
sentences.zh: 所有者应该在那里检查你的所有东西，以防万一你有一些 CSAM！元数据只是跟踪有关谁、在哪里以及与谁一起的信息......

sentences.en: every bartender should take your IDs and log when you came to the bar, who you sat with and how long you talked there.EU Chat control is like general eavesdropping...
sentences.zh: 每个调酒师都应该拿走你的身份证并记录你何时来到酒吧、与谁坐在一起以及在那里交谈了多长时间。欧盟聊天控制就像一般的窃听......

sentences.en: every time you sit down and talk with someone, an EU bureaucrat should sit next to you and listen and write down your conversations, just in case.etc.Somehow people think that "it's ok if it's on the internet", even when it's stuff they'd never accept in real life.
sentences.zh: 每次你坐下来与某人交谈时，欧盟官员都应该坐在你旁边，听并写下你的谈话，以防万一等等。不知何故，人们认为“如果是在互联网上就没关系”，即使这是他们在现实生活中永远不会接受的东西。

blocks.html_en:
I agree, so let's make sure that signed warrant is always required for any kind of access to communications, even _if unencrypted_ like snail mail, SMS or plain text chat client. As well as encrypted.

So - political solution, not a tech solution.

blocks.html_en:
While I’m decidedly pro-encryption, I don’t like this argument. If something is the right thing, it would still be the right thing when promoted for the wrong reasons, and if it’s the wrong thing, it’s still the wrong thing even when at present nobody has ulterior motives.

When arguing against surveillance, the arguments should be on its merits, not on whether the current proponents happen to have ulterior motives.

sentences.en: While I’m decidedly pro-encryption, I don’t like this argument.
sentences.zh: 虽然我坚决支持加密，但我不喜欢这个论点。

blocks.html_en:
> not on whether the current proponents happen to have ulterior motives.

Even if the current proponents have no ulterior motive, and in fact live and die having done nothing negative with such power, it does not stop the next group in power from extending and abusing power, don't base laws on temporary trust of politicians.

sentences.en: > not on whether the current proponents happen to have ulterior motives.Even if the current proponents have no ulterior motive, and in fact live and die having done nothing negative with such power, it does not stop the next group in power from extending and abusing power, don't base laws on temporary trust of politicians.

blocks.html_en:
This suffers from the "proves too much" problem. You are essentially arguing that government should only be given powers that cannot possibly ever be abused or be extended to where they can be abused. It is only an argument against government powers against CSAM because it is an argument against pretty much all government powers.

Arguments that have any chance of persuading a government not to do something (and the public not to support the government in doing that thing) have to a lot more specific than that.

sentences.en: This suffers from the "proves too much" problem.
sentences.zh: 这面临着“证明太多”的问题。

sentences.zh: 你本质上是在主张政府只应该被赋予永远不会被滥用的权力，或者延伸到可以滥用的权力。

sentences.zh: 这只是反对政府权力反对 CSAM 的论点，因为它是反对几乎所有政府权力的论点。有机会说服政府不做某事（以及公众不支持政府做那件事）的论点必须比这更具体。

blocks.html_en: One problem with that is that it’s difficult to prove motives. So you’re on shaky and disputable ground. It’s much better to point out how the proposed mechanisms are prone to be misused, which is independent of current motives. Get rid of the shaky ground. Saying “these are disingenuous people proposing this” is exactly an attempt at emotional manipulation, in the sense of an ad hominem fallacy.

sentences.en: One problem with that is that it’s difficult to prove motives.
sentences.zh: 这样做的一个问题是很难证明动机。

sentences.en: So you’re on shaky and disputable ground.
sentences.zh: 所以你的立场不稳定且有争议。

sentences.en: It’s much better to point out how the proposed mechanisms are prone to be misused, which is independent of current motives.
sentences.zh: 最好指出所提出的机制如何容易被滥用，这与当前的动机无关。

sentences.en: Get rid of the shaky ground.
sentences.zh: 摆脱摇摇欲坠的地面。

sentences.zh: 说“这些人提出这个建议是不诚实的”，从人身攻击的意义上来说，这完全是一种情绪操纵的尝试。

blocks.html_en:
So invading the privacy of millions of people, even if it's just automatic scans for some specific thing is a right thing? Does this apply to mandatory drug tests for everyone everywhere? How about drug and weapon seeking drones, doing daily checks in every apartment everywhere? How about mandatory AI powered microphones everywhere that would detect threats, blackmail, any talk about anything illegal, etc.?

If you take a 1000 random people of the street now,how many of them are sharing CSAM via icloud?

If you take a 1000 random politicians, how many of them have corruption scandals? Why not start with them instead, a bodycam and an AI powered microphone that would detect corruption automatically... let them lead as an example, before they apply the laws onto "the rest of us".

sentences.en: So invading the privacy of millions of people, even if it's just automatic scans for some specific thing is a right thing?
sentences.zh: 那么，侵犯数百万人的隐私，即使只是自动扫描某些特定事物，也是正确的事情吗？

sentences.en: Does this apply to mandatory drug tests for everyone everywhere?
sentences.zh: 这是否适用于世界各地每个人的强制药物测试？

sentences.en: How about drug and weapon seeking drones, doing daily checks in every apartment everywhere?
sentences.zh: 寻找毒品和武器的无人机，对各地的每间公寓进行日常检查怎么样？

sentences.en: How about mandatory AI powered microphones everywhere that would detect threats, blackmail, any talk about anything illegal, etc.?If you take a 1000 random people of the street now,how many of them are sharing CSAM via icloud?If you take a 1000 random politicians, how many of them have corruption scandals?
sentences.zh: 强制人工智能驱动的麦克风无处不在，可以检测威胁、勒索、任何非法言论等，怎么样？如果你现在随机抽取 1000 名街上的人，他们中有多少人通过 icloud 共享 CSAM？如果随机抽取 1000 名政客，他们中有多少人有腐败丑闻？

sentences.en: Why not start with them instead, a bodycam and an AI powered microphone that would detect corruption automatically...
sentences.zh: 为什么不从它们开始呢，一个随身摄像头和一个人工智能麦克风，可以自动检测损坏......

sentences.en: let them lead as an example, before they apply the laws onto "the rest of us".
sentences.zh: 在他们将法律适用于“我们其他人”之前，让他们以身作则。

blocks.html_en: If you take a 1000 random politicians, how many of them have corruption scandals? Why not start with them instead, a bodycam and an AI powered microphone that would detect corruption automatically... let them lead as an example, before they apply the laws onto "the rest of us".

sentences.en: If you take a 1000 random politicians, how many of them have corruption scandals?
sentences.zh: 如果随机抽取 1000 名政客，其中有多少人有腐败丑闻？

sentences.en: Why not start with them instead, a bodycam and an AI powered microphone that would detect corruption automatically...
sentences.zh: 为什么不从它们开始呢，一个随身摄像头和一个人工智能麦克风，可以自动检测损坏......

sentences.en: let them lead as an example, before they apply the laws onto "the rest of us".
sentences.zh: 在他们将法律适用于“我们其他人”之前，让他们以身作则。

blocks.html_en: Feels like this has gone completely meta and it's now way more common to see people who don't care about children refusing to support that any proposal that might benefit children under the assumption that nobody cares about children so there must be an ulterior motive.

blocks.html_en:
The judge's comments are extremely disturbing, as she seems to want legislation passed that requires companies to violate user privacy.

And client side scanning is just as bad as encryption backdoors. There's a good reason Apple was attacked for even considering it: https://arxiv.org/abs/2110.07450

sentences.en: The judge's comments are extremely disturbing, as she seems to want legislation passed that requires companies to violate user privacy.And client side scanning is just as bad as encryption backdoors.

sentences.zh: 苹果甚至考虑这样做而受到攻击是有充分理由的：https://arxiv.org/abs/2110.07450

blocks.html_en:
Truly being honest, I think CSAM scanning of private comms is ineffective in the long term anyways. Pedophiles aren't stupid, you'll drag a bunch at first but the networks will be reestablished and sharing will be done via sneakernet.

The primary focus should always in preventing the creation of CSAM.

- Comprehensive Sex Ed starting young so kids can identify grooming and seek help from a trusted adult, even if abuse comes from a family member.

- Fixing schools in general so homeschooling isn't as attractive for parents. Keep a tab on home schooled children and identify social isolation.

- Bigger resources for actual honest to god on the ground investigations.

To be clear I'm not saying that homeschooling = child abuse, simply there's a lack of the mechanisms to detect it in homeschooling settings.

sentences.en: Truly being honest, I think CSAM scanning of private comms is ineffective in the long term anyways.
sentences.zh: 说实话，我认为 CSAM 对私人通信的扫描从长远来看是无效的。

sentences.en: Pedophiles aren't stupid, you'll drag a bunch at first but the networks will be reestablished and sharing will be done via sneakernet.The primary focus should always in preventing the creation of CSAM.- Comprehensive Sex Ed starting young so kids can identify grooming and seek help from a trusted adult, even if abuse comes from a family member.- Fixing schools in general so homeschooling isn't as attractive for parents.
sentences.zh: 恋童癖者并不愚蠢，一开始你会拖累一群人，但网络将被重建，共享将通过运动鞋网完成。主要重点应该始终是防止 CSAM 的产生。-从很小的时候开始进行全面的性教育，这样孩子们就可以识别诱骗行为，并向值得信赖的成年人寻求帮助，即使虐待来自家庭成员。-总体上修复学校，这样在家上学对父母来说就不那么有吸引力了。

sentences.en: Keep a tab on home schooled children and identify social isolation.- Bigger resources for actual honest to god on the ground investigations.To be clear I'm not saying that homeschooling = child abuse, simply there's a lack of the mechanisms to detect it in homeschooling settings.
sentences.zh: 密切关注在家上学的儿童并识别社会隔离。- 为真正诚实的实地调查提供更多资源。需要澄清的是，我并不是说在家上学 = 虐待儿童，只是缺乏在家庭上学环境中检测到这一情况的机制。

blocks.html_en:
> Pedophiles aren't stupid

I'm not entirely convinced that's true. Facebook is a leading reporter of CSAM, much of it sent through Messenger, which only recently got E2EE, and Instagram DM, which briefly had E2EE but no longer does. If I was going to transmit something that could get me in trouble, it certainly wouldn't be via Instagram DM.

Facebook's EU CSAM report is here: https://transparency.meta.com/reports/regulatory-transparenc...

sentences.en: > Pedophiles aren't stupidI'm not entirely convinced that's true.
sentences.zh: > 恋童癖者并不愚蠢我并不完全相信这是真的。

blocks.html_en:
I wonder if the judge would be in favor of companies proactively going into people's houses at random to check on their belongings, if they don't have inappropriate photos somewhere, or whatever.

It's harder to do, but conceptually the same. So sad it's not being done. Very disturbing.

They could do it when people are not at home. There'd no problem, nobody would even notice.

> on par with requiring encryption backdoors

There is no back door if nothing leaves your device

> goes against the purpose of having end to end encryption

Most people would consider the "purpose" is to avoid 3rd parties listening in

blocks.html_en: Banks are probably the most Orwellian surveillance apparatus of all. Almost every time I read an arrest warrant there is a whole section where banking records are used to damn someone. The equivalent level of banking privacy to graphene is roughly walking in to the teller to withdraw a few thousand cash once a month and then paying literally everything with that (or an anonymized crypto).

sentences.en: Banks are probably the most Orwellian surveillance apparatus of all.
sentences.zh: 银行可能是最奥威尔式的监视机构。

sentences.en: Almost every time I read an arrest warrant there is a whole section where banking records are used to damn someone.
sentences.zh: 几乎每次我读到逮捕令时，都会有一整段银行记录被用来诅咒某人。

sentences.en: The equivalent level of banking privacy to graphene is roughly walking in to the teller to withdraw a few thousand cash once a month and then paying literally everything with that (or an anonymized crypto).
sentences.zh: 与石墨烯相当的银行隐私水平大致是每月一次走进出纳员提取几千现金，然后用它（或匿名加密货币）支付几乎所有费用。

blocks.html_en:
The judge’s dicta about protecting children in her pro-privacy ruling upholding existing law “tips their hand” that they are somehow part of a global conspiracy to eliminate privacy?

I think your conspiracy theory needs work, to be perfectly honest with you.

sentences.en: The judge’s dicta about protecting children in her pro-privacy ruling upholding existing law “tips their hand” that they are somehow part of a global conspiracy to eliminate privacy?I think your conspiracy theory needs work, to be perfectly honest with you.

blocks.html_en:
> As sad as this is, end to end encryption means no CSAM scanning.

I think it depends on your definition of e2ee and where the "end"s are.

If the locally running application can decrypt the data, it could always do whatever it wanted. Is that really how you define e2ee?

sentences.en: > As sad as this is, end to end encryption means no CSAM scanning.I think it depends on your definition of e2ee and where the "end"s are.If the locally running application can decrypt the data, it could always do whatever it wanted.
sentences.zh: > 令人遗憾的是，端到端加密意味着没有 CSAM 扫描。我认为这取决于您对 e2ee 的定义以及“末端”在哪里。如果本地运行的应用程序可以解密数据，它总是可以做任何它想做的事情。

sentences.en: Is that really how you define e2ee?
sentences.zh: 这真的是您对 e2ee 的定义吗？

blocks.html_en:
“Apple created its own proprietary alternative, NeuralHash, which apparently wasn’t as good. So Apple U-turned on its efforts to scan for CSAM in its cloud storage. Instead, Apple implemented end-to-end encryption for iCloud files.”

Wasn’t Apple’s design to explicitly NOT scan in its cloud storage, but look at the file on-device at the moment you wish to upload it to iCloud? This method would make it compatible with Advanced Data Protection; so ADP could have always been in the pipeline rather than Apple u-turning. In fact, NeuralHash may have been proposed because Apple wanted to introduce ADP and saw a potential problem here/get concerns from government agencies about it and saw this as a means to an end(-to-end).

The system was designed pretty elegantly and offers far better privacy protections - including guardrails - than what Microsoft and Google do, but the communication from Apple about it was absolutely horrible and generated enormous backlash. (Not saying I agreed with implementing it, just saying the design was infinitely better than competitors.)

sentences.en: “Apple created its own proprietary alternative, NeuralHash, which apparently wasn’t as good.
sentences.zh: “苹果创建了自己的专有替代方案 NeuralHash，但它显然没有那么好。

sentences.en: So Apple U-turned on its efforts to scan for CSAM in its cloud storage.
sentences.zh: 因此，Apple 开始在其云存储中扫描 CSAM。

sentences.en: Instead, Apple implemented end-to-end encryption for iCloud files.”Wasn’t Apple’s design to explicitly NOT scan in its cloud storage, but look at the file on-device at the moment you wish to upload it to iCloud?
sentences.zh: 相反，Apple 为 iCloud 文件实施了端到端加密。”Apple 的设计不是明确不在其云存储中进行扫描，而是在您希望将其上传到 iCloud 时查看设备上的文件吗？

sentences.en: This method would make it compatible with Advanced Data Protection; so ADP could have always been in the pipeline rather than Apple u-turning.
sentences.zh: 此方法将使其与高级数据保护兼容；因此，ADP 可能一直在酝酿之中，而不是苹果掉头。

sentences.en: In fact, NeuralHash may have been proposed because Apple wanted to introduce ADP and saw a potential problem here/get concerns from government agencies about it and saw this as a means to an end(-to-end).The system was designed pretty elegantly and offers far better privacy protections - including guardrails - than what Microsoft and Google do, but the communication from Apple about it was absolutely horrible and generated enormous backlash.
sentences.zh: 事实上，NeuralHash 的提出可能是因为 Apple 想要引入 ADP，并看到了这里的潜在问题/政府机构对此表示担忧，并将其视为达到目的的一种手段。该系统设计得相当优雅，提供了比 Microsoft 和 Google 更好的隐私保护（包括护栏），但 Apple 对此的沟通绝对是可怕的，并产生了巨大的反弹。

sentences.en: (Not saying I agreed with implementing it, just saying the design was infinitely better than competitors.)
sentences.zh: （并不是说我同意实施它，只是说该设计比竞争对手好得多。）

sentences.en: Wasn’t Apple’s design to explicitly NOT scan in its cloud storage, but look at the file on-device at the moment you wish to upload it to iCloud?
sentences.zh: Apple 的设计不是明确不在其云存储中进行扫描，而是在您希望将其上传到 iCloud 时查看设备上的文件吗？

sentences.en: This method would make it compatible with Advanced Data Protection; so ADP could have always been in the pipeline rather than Apple u-turning.
sentences.zh: 此方法将使其与高级数据保护兼容；因此，ADP 可能一直在酝酿之中，而不是苹果掉头。

sentences.en: In fact, NeuralHash may have been proposed because Apple wanted to introduce ADP and saw a potential problem here/get concerns from government agencies about it and saw this as a means to an end(-to-end).
sentences.zh: 事实上，NeuralHash 的提出可能是因为苹果想要引入 ADP，并看到了这里潜在的问题/政府机构对此表示担忧，并将其视为达到目的的一种手段。

blocks.html_en: The system was designed pretty elegantly and offers far better privacy protections - including guardrails - than what Microsoft and Google do, but the communication from Apple about it was absolutely horrible and generated enormous backlash. (Not saying I agreed with implementing it, just saying the design was infinitely better than competitors.)

sentences.en: The system was designed pretty elegantly and offers far better privacy protections - including guardrails - than what Microsoft and Google do, but the communication from Apple about it was absolutely horrible and generated enormous backlash.
sentences.zh: 该系统设计得相当优雅，提供了比微软和谷歌更好的隐私保护——包括护栏——但苹果公司对此的沟通绝对是可怕的，并引起了巨大的强烈反对。

sentences.en: (Not saying I agreed with implementing it, just saying the design was infinitely better than competitors.)
sentences.zh: （并不是说我同意实施它，只是说该设计比竞争对手好得多。）

blocks.html_en: A mandatory client side scanning system with an opaque database filled with unverifiable entries would render many of the protections provided by ADP meaningless. Its was insane that Apple was even considering such an idea in the first place.

blocks.html_en:
As the creator of mediaden.ca[1] I’ve thought about this. Client side scanning is maybe marginally better than server side scanning, but both paths lead to privacy rot.

Governments need to catch criminals, but they shouldn’t do it at everyone else’s expense.

1. https://mediaden.ca

sentences.en: As the creator of mediaden.ca[1] I’ve thought about this.
sentences.zh: 作为 mediaden.ca[1] 的创建者，我思考过这个问题。

sentences.en: Client side scanning is maybe marginally better than server side scanning, but both paths lead to privacy rot.Governments need to catch criminals, but they shouldn’t do it at everyone else’s expense.1.
sentences.zh: 客户端扫描可能比服务器端扫描稍好一些，但这两种途径都会导致隐私恶化。政府需要抓捕罪犯，但他们不应该以牺牲其他人的利益为代价。1。

blocks.html_en: I simply don't trust services such as iCloud. The legal landscape is too volatile, and Apple's own "terms and conditions" are also subject to constant change. As far as I can tell, most people don't need cloud backups, and iCloud mostly shows up as an annoyance designed to extract more money from customers. In fact, most people probably don't know that Apple and Google vacuum up their files the moment they are created, for their own good, of course.

sentences.en: I simply don't trust services such as iCloud.
sentences.zh: 我根本不信任 iCloud 等服务。

sentences.en: The legal landscape is too volatile, and Apple's own "terms and conditions" are also subject to constant change.
sentences.zh: 法律环境太不稳定，苹果自己的“条款和条件”也不断变化。

sentences.en: As far as I can tell, most people don't need cloud backups, and iCloud mostly shows up as an annoyance designed to extract more money from customers.
sentences.zh: 据我所知，大多数人不需要云备份，而 iCloud 主要表现为一种烦恼，旨在从客户那里榨取更多钱。

sentences.en: In fact, most people probably don't know that Apple and Google vacuum up their files the moment they are created, for their own good, of course.
sentences.zh: 事实上，大多数人可能不知道苹果和谷歌在文件创建后立即对其进行清理，当然这是为了他们自己的利益。

blocks.html_en: Is your phone also made of indestructible materials so it can't break, and magnetically linked to your person so it can't be stolen? Backups exist so you don't lose precious photos/data if you lose the device. Most People™ do not have the technical knowledge to set up a self-owned backup system, and/or will not realize how badly their future selves will wish they had backups if the setup friction for a self-owned solution is too high to conveniently do it right this second.

blocks.html_en:
I think we are closing that gap with AI coding tools.

A growing number of people people today can figure out how to run Fable in a co work session, or codex, and that can indeed set up the self-owned backup system for you along with an alternative trustable cloud backup thats not Apple or Google.

sentences.en: I think we are closing that gap with AI coding tools.A growing number of people people today can figure out how to run Fable in a co work session, or codex, and that can indeed set up the self-owned backup system for you along with an alternative trustable cloud backup thats not Apple or Google.
sentences.zh: 我认为我们正在通过人工智能编码工具缩小这一差距。如今，越来越多的人可以弄清楚如何在协同工作会议或 Codex 中运行《神鬼寓言》，这确实可以为您建立自有的备份系统以及非苹果或谷歌的替代可信云备份。

blocks.html_en:
Technical people, maybe. Even if we hypothetically grant that vibe-coded backup systems can be trusted, deploying, using, and maintaining are still hurdles. And there’s absolutely no way that’s all tractable for the vast, vast majority of the population.

Mind you, an average person probably doesn’t even really know what a server is. And these average people need backups just as much as (probably more than) people technical enough to vibe-and-deploy.

sentences.zh: 这些普通人需要的备份与（可能超过）有足够技术能力进行振动和部署的人员一样多。

blocks.html_en:
But you still need backups right?

For most people "the cloud" is where you backup stuff. If you have a personal backup strategy that doesn't involve the cloud, you are not "most people".

Doing backups the right way take some skill and investment if you want to do it by yourself. It may involve setting up a NAS, and some discipline with physical media. You have to do your own security too. Most people don't want to do that, so, cloud backup it is.

It is not a perfect solution, even beyond the privacy considerations, like you can still lose your data by losing your account, but from my personal experience, people lose their data less often now that they have cloud backups.

sentences.en: But you still need backups right?For most people "the cloud" is where you backup stuff.
sentences.zh: 但您仍然需要备份，对吗？对于大多数人来说，“云”是您备份内容的地方。

sentences.en: It may involve setting up a NAS, and some discipline with physical media.
sentences.zh: 它可能涉及设置 NAS 以及一些有关物理介质的规则。

sentences.en: You have to do your own security too.
sentences.zh: 你也必须做好自己的安全工作。

sentences.en: Most people don't want to do that, so, cloud backup it is.It is not a perfect solution, even beyond the privacy considerations, like you can still lose your data by losing your account, but from my personal experience, people lose their data less often now that they have cloud backups.
sentences.zh: 大多数人都不想这样做，所以，云备份就是这样。它不是一个完美的解决方案，甚至超出了隐私考虑，就像您仍然可能因丢失帐户而丢失数据一样，但从我个人的经验来看，现在人们有了云备份，丢失数据的频率就会降低。

blocks.html_en:
But you still need backups right?

For most people "the cloud" is where you backup stuff. If you have a personal backup strategy that doesn't involve the cloud, you are not "most people".

Doing backups the right way take some skill and investment if you want to do it by yourself. It may involve setting up a NAS, and some discipline with physical media. You have to do your own security too. Most people don't want to do that, so, cloud backup it is.

It is not a perfect solution, even beyond the privacy considerations, like you can still lose your data by losing your account, but from my personal experience, people lose their data less often now that they have cloud backups.

blocks.html_en:
> But you still need backups right?

I back up files to my own cloud with a Nextcloud integration for Android. That being said, a monthly or so backup of devices via USB/Ethernet, like we used to in the pre-cloud area, would be enough for all intents and purposes. It's not like what people have on their phones is generally very valuable.

sentences.en: That being said, a monthly or so backup of devices via USB/Ethernet, like we used to in the pre-cloud area, would be enough for all intents and purposes.

sentences.zh: 人们手机上的东西通常并不非常有价值。

blocks.html_en:
> I back up files to my own cloud with a Nextcloud integration for Android.

Ouch man. Don't do that. Nextcloud is fairly unreliable.

> That being said, a monthly or so backup of devices via USB/Ethernet, like we used to in the pre-cloud area, would be enough for all intents and purposes.

And/or use FolderSync to sync to your PC, and then use a proper E2E system to back up to the cloud.

blocks.html_en:
- People don't need cloud backups

- People's files aren't valuable

You're making a lot of claims that are so blatantly false from what I see on my side that I don't know what to make of it. Are you projecting your own needs onto "people"? Or am I the one who's disconnected, and people truly don't care about losing all their photos?

blocks.html_en:
Everybody needs cloud backups for their photos at least.

Most people don’t have computers, those who do, do not regularly backup their photos on them.

In both family and extended family many a cry would be avoided if people paid the 5 bucks it costs to backup their photos before your phone gets stolen or lost.

sentences.en: Everybody needs cloud backups for their photos at least.Most people don’t have computers, those who do, do not regularly backup their photos on them.In both family and extended family many a cry would be avoided if people paid the 5 bucks it costs to backup their photos before your phone gets stolen or lost.
sentences.zh: 每个人至少都需要对照片进行云备份。大多数人没有电脑，即使有电脑，也不会定期在电脑上备份照片。无论是家庭还是大家庭，如果人们在手机被盗或丢失之前支付 5 美元备份照片的费用，那么许多哭泣就可以避免。

blocks.html_en:
A perfectly safe panopticon and freedom & privacy will always be at odds.

Don't let a classic "think of the children" appeal to emotion short circuit your reasoning.

blocks.html_en: The law has exceptions for electronic service providers who report what they find on their storage. If you are operating a service for friends at no charge with no business plan you probably will have to work a bit harder to avail yourself of that exception. Might be best to charge a nominal fee and at least pretend to be doing it as a service provider.

sentences.zh: 该法律对报告其存储内容的电子服务提供商有例外情况。

sentences.en: Might be best to charge a nominal fee and at least pretend to be doing it as a service provider.
sentences.zh: 最好收取象征性的费用，并且至少假装是作为服务提供商提供服务。

blocks.html_en:
I know creating a throwaway to hide your name for an opinion is a bad manner, but this one is one I really don’t want linked back to me

The VAST majority of “CSAM” is consensually created and exchanged by teens. Their future selves and their parents form this pressure group attacking everyone’s liberty and privacy to try to undo the downsides of choices they made themselves with full knowledge of what could happen.

The criminal and disgusting tail end of this type of material deserves the worst of consequences for the perpetrators and all the support in the world for the victims, but these are mostly - you guessed it - poor and unprivileged children from far away places and they certainly can’t put this much pressure on apple

sentences.en: I know creating a throwaway to hide your name for an opinion is a bad manner, but this one is one I really don’t want linked back to meThe VAST majority of “CSAM” is consensually created and exchanged by teens.
sentences.zh: 我知道创建一个一次性的东西来隐藏你的名字以获取意见是一种不好的方式，但这是我真的不想链接回我的“CSAM”的绝大多数是由青少年自愿创建和交换的。

sentences.en: Their future selves and their parents form this pressure group attacking everyone’s liberty and privacy to try to undo the downsides of choices they made themselves with full knowledge of what could happen.The criminal and disgusting tail end of this type of material deserves the worst of consequences for the perpetrators and all the support in the world for the victims, but these are mostly - you guessed it - poor and unprivileged children from far away places and they certainly can’t put this much pressure on apple
sentences.zh: 他们未来的自己和他们的父母形成了这个压力团体，攻击每个人的自由和隐私，试图消除他们在充分了解可能发生的情况下做出的选择的弊端。这种材料的犯罪和令人厌恶的结局应该让肇事者遭受最坏的后果，并为受害者提供全世界的支持，但这些大多是——你猜对了——来自遥远地方的贫穷和无特权的孩子，他们当然不能给苹果施加这么大的压力

blocks.html_en:
I think you’re right, but from another angle. In the state where I lived way back when, a state representative put forth a bill to explicitly make e-CSAM illegal. I guess it was already illegal for print media and this covered a gap in the law about cell phone pics, etc. Thing is, it had no allowance for the age of the picture taker, or even whether the picture taker was the photo subject. If a 16 year old girl took a nude selfie and sent it to her boyfriend, she was a felon.

I wrote to the rep and explained my concerns. I wholeheartedly agreed with the intent of the law, but the code was buggy. To my surprise, he wrote back in horror to say he hadn’t considered that and pulled the bill immediately. I’m proud of having done that.

I’m 100% pro yeeting child pornographers into the sun. I still don’t want to throw kids in prison or remove all traces of a right to privacy in our haste to sun-yeet them.

sentences.en: I think you’re right, but from another angle.
sentences.zh: 我认为你是对的，但从另一个角度来看。

sentences.en: In the state where I lived way back when, a state representative put forth a bill to explicitly make e-CSAM illegal.
sentences.zh: 在我很久以前居住的州，一位州代表提出了一项法案，明确规定 e-CSAM 非法。

sentences.en: I guess it was already illegal for print media and this covered a gap in the law about cell phone pics, etc. Thing is, it had no allowance for the age of the picture taker, or even whether the picture taker was the photo subject.
sentences.zh: 我想这对于印刷媒体来说已经是非法的，这弥补了有关手机照片等法律的空白。事实是，它没有考虑拍照者的年龄，甚至没有考虑拍照者是否是照片主题。

sentences.en: If a 16 year old girl took a nude selfie and sent it to her boyfriend, she was a felon.I wrote to the rep and explained my concerns.
sentences.zh: 如果一个 16 岁的女孩拍了一张裸体自拍照并发送给她的男朋友，她就是重罪犯。我写信给该代表并解释了我的担忧。

sentences.en: I wholeheartedly agreed with the intent of the law, but the code was buggy.
sentences.zh: 我全心全意地同意法律的意图，但代码有错误。

sentences.en: To my surprise, he wrote back in horror to say he hadn’t considered that and pulled the bill immediately.
sentences.zh: 令我惊讶的是，他惊恐地回信说他没有考虑到这一点，并立即撤回了账单。

sentences.en: I’m proud of having done that.I’m 100% pro yeeting child pornographers into the sun.
sentences.zh: 我为自己做到了这一点感到自豪。我百分百赞成将儿童色情制品暴露在阳光下。

sentences.en: I still don’t want to throw kids in prison or remove all traces of a right to privacy in our haste to sun-yeet them.
sentences.zh: 我仍然不想把孩子们扔进监狱，或者在我们匆忙晒太阳的过程中消除所有隐私权的痕迹。

blocks.html_en:
> pulled the bill immediately

This is weird indeed. These bills are mostly put forward in order to appear tough on crime and secure votes of the naive population ("think of the children"). Pulling the bill is detrimental as it may lead to "pedo supporter" conspiracy spreading among the district.

sentences.zh: > 立即取消账单 这确实很奇怪。

sentences.zh: 撤销该法案是有害的，因为它可能导致“恋童支持者”阴谋在该地区蔓延。

blocks.html_en:
> I still don’t want to throw kids in prison or remove all traces of a right to privacy in our haste to sun-yeet them.

The one messy corner of this is the "strict liability" for this type of material. An underage kid can take a nude photo, send it to an adult, and then the adult can criminally liable for just having it, even if he deleted it as soon as he saw it. Either both parties involved in handing something for which there is "strict liability' need to be held accountable, or "strict liability" has to be changed so a person isn't liable if he deletes or reports the material as soon as he first becomes aware of it. And this isn't likely to happen because it would provide a plausible defense for every one criminally charged.

sentences.en: > I still don’t want to throw kids in prison or remove all traces of a right to privacy in our haste to sun-yeet them.The one messy corner of this is the "strict liability" for this type of material.
sentences.zh: > 我仍然不想把孩子们扔进监狱，或者在我们匆忙晒太阳的过程中消除隐私权的所有痕迹。其中一个混乱的角落是此类材料的“严格责任”。

sentences.en: An underage kid can take a nude photo, send it to an adult, and then the adult can criminally liable for just having it, even if he deleted it as soon as he saw it.
sentences.zh: 未成年人可以拍摄裸照，并将其发送给成年人，然后该成年人可能会因拥有该照片而承担刑事责任，即使他一看到它就将其删除。

sentences.en: Either both parties involved in handing something for which there is "strict liability' need to be held accountable, or "strict liability" has to be changed so a person isn't liable if he deletes or reports the material as soon as he first becomes aware of it.
sentences.zh: 要么涉及处理“严格责任”的双方都需要承担责任，要么必须改变“严格责任”，这样一个人在发现该材料后立即删除或举报该材料就可以不承担责任。

sentences.en: And this isn't likely to happen because it would provide a plausible defense for every one criminally charged.
sentences.zh: 而这种情况不太可能发生，因为这将为每一个被刑事指控的人提供合理的辩护。

blocks.html_en:
> has to be changed so a person isn't liable if he deletes or reports the material as soon as he first becomes aware of it

AFAIK that's more or less how it works today as a practical matter. The law recognizes this situation as an affirmative defense, but does not make it impossible to be charged.

To be an affirmative defense it has to be reported immediately or destroyed, constitute three images or less, and not be sent to anyone [other than law enforcement]. Interestingly, NCMEC doesn't necessarily count for that -- if you find yourself in this situation and you want to report something, call your local police.

sentences.en: > has to be changed so a person isn't liable if he deletes or reports the material as soon as he first becomes aware of itAFAIK that's more or less how it works today as a practical matter.
sentences.zh: > 必须进行更改，这样，如果一个人在第一次意识到该材料后立即删除或报告该材料，则无需承担责任。据我所知，这或多或少是当今实际问题的运作方式。

sentences.en: The law recognizes this situation as an affirmative defense, but does not make it impossible to be charged.To be an affirmative defense it has to be reported immediately or destroyed, constitute three images or less, and not be sent to anyone [other than law enforcement].
sentences.zh: 法律承认这种情况为积极抗辩，但并不意味着不可能被指控。要成为积极抗辩，必须立即报告或销毁，构成三张或更少的图像，并且不得发送给任何人[执法部门除外]。

sentences.en: Interestingly, NCMEC doesn't necessarily count for that -- if you find yourself in this situation and you want to report something, call your local police.
sentences.zh: 有趣的是，NCMEC 并不一定重要——如果您发现自己处于这种情况并且想要举报某些事情，请致电当地警察。

blocks.html_en:
IANAL, but the notion of "strict liability" horrified me when I first head of it, and I thought it had to be some kind of a misunderstanding. So we're tossing that "innocent until proven guilty" idea out the window, huh?

I'm sure smarter people than me have sussed this out and can explain why it's a good thing, but it sits wrong with me. We can put the subject matter aside for a second: I don't think I could convict someone for having something happen to them, regardless of what the law says. Let's say drug possession was a strict liability law (and maybe it is for all I know). Finding a baggy of meth on the corner of a farmer's lot would mean that, technically, he was guilty of possession and had to prove that it wasn't really is. That's nuts. And looping back to the subject at hand, if the only evidence that someone possessed CSAM was their email inbox, without proof that it was solicited? They want me on their jury.

There should never be a circumstance where someone can't report something that happened to them to the police without a legitimate fear of being arrested. That's bad for the person, and it's bad for society.

But if their hard drive has folders grouped by age or something, prepare the solar catapult.

sentences.zh: IANAL，但是当我第一次想到“严格责任”的概念时，我感到震惊，我认为这一定是某种误解。

sentences.en: So we're tossing that "innocent until proven guilty" idea out the window, huh?I'm sure smarter people than me have sussed this out and can explain why it's a good thing, but it sits wrong with me.
sentences.zh: 所以我们要把“在被证明有罪之前无罪”的想法扔到窗外，是吗？我相信比我更聪明的人已经弄清楚了这一点，并且可以解释为什么这是一件好事，但它对我来说是错误的。

sentences.en: We can put the subject matter aside for a second: I don't think I could convict someone for having something happen to them, regardless of what the law says.
sentences.zh: 我们可以暂时把这个主题放在一边：我认为我不能因为某人发生了什么事而定罪，无论法律如何规定。

sentences.en: Let's say drug possession was a strict liability law (and maybe it is for all I know).
sentences.zh: 假设持有毒品是一项严格的责任法（也许据我所知）。

sentences.en: Finding a baggy of meth on the corner of a farmer's lot would mean that, technically, he was guilty of possession and had to prove that it wasn't really is.
sentences.zh: 从技术上讲，在农民的地角发现一袋冰毒就意味着他犯有持有毒品罪，并且必须证明这不是真的。

sentences.en: That's nuts.
sentences.zh: 那太疯狂了。

sentences.en: And looping back to the subject at hand, if the only evidence that someone possessed CSAM was their email inbox, without proof that it was solicited?
sentences.zh: 回到当前的主题，如果某人拥有 CSAM 的唯一证据是他们的电子邮件收件箱，而没有证据表明它是被索取的呢？

sentences.en: They want me on their jury.There should never be a circumstance where someone can't report something that happened to them to the police without a legitimate fear of being arrested.
sentences.zh: 他们希望我加入他们的陪审团。永远不应该出现这样的情况：有人无法向警方报告发生在他们身上的事情，而不必担心被捕。

sentences.en: That's bad for the person, and it's bad for society.But if their hard drive has folders grouped by age or something, prepare the solar catapult.
sentences.zh: 这对个人不利，对社会不利。但是，如果他们的硬盘驱动器上有按年龄或其他方式分组的文件夹，请准备太阳能弹射器。

blocks.html_en:
> So we're tossing that "innocent until proven guilty" idea out the window, huh?

We sure do. Legislators who pushed this "strict liability" thing forward were playing "tough on crime" for the audience of naive voters. Now anyone who tries to change this will commit political suicide and achieve the "pedo supporter" title.

sentences.en: Legislators who pushed this "strict liability" thing forward were playing "tough on crime" for the audience of naive voters.
sentences.zh: 推动这种“严格责任”的立法者正在为天真的选民们扮演“严厉打击犯罪”的角色。

sentences.en: Now anyone who tries to change this will commit political suicide and achieve the "pedo supporter" title.
sentences.zh: 现在，任何试图改变这一现状的人都会政治自杀，并获得“恋童癖支持者”的称号。
